name: National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The National Society of Leadership and Success (NSLS) at University of Nevada Las Vegas


The NSLS is the largest and only accredited leadership honor society in the United States. Our local chapter is
part of this national organization with more than 700 chapters and over 1.5 million existing members.
Students are selected based on exemplary academic achievement and leadership potential. Our chapter
members will have access to:

• Over $400,000 in scholarships (visit nsls.org/scholarships-and-awards to learn more)
• An exclusive NSLS job board
• A personalized letter of recommendation

Our leadership program teaches interpersonal skills that give you an advantage in a competitive job market.
If you become a member, you can also:
• Learn from top global leaders like former US Presidents, CEOs, celebrities, and more with exclusive Speaker
Broadcasts.
• Meet new friends and make lifelong connections (on-campus and virtually).
• Overcome challenges with success coaches, shortcutting your way to your goals.
• Gain access to exclusive private networking events.
Visit nsls.org to learn more and accept your nomination.


Frequently Asked Questions:Some common questions we receive are listed below. If you have any other questions, visit nsls.org to chat with a team member or email them at help@nsls.org.

 

How Do I Join?
Membership is given by invitation based on academic merit however, you can self-nominate here:NSLS Self-Nomination

What do I have to do to become inducted?
The Steps to Induction take approximately 9-10 hours over the course of the semester and you can complete it at
your own pace. To learn more, visit nsls.org/the-membership-experience.


Is this recognized on campus?
The NSLS is a Registered Student Organization (RSO). Student members in our chapter will join more than 1.5
million members nationwide.
summary: The National Society of Leadership and Success achieves profound results in helping students discover and attain their goals, offering life changing speeches from the nation's leading presenters and a community where students help one another succeed.

name: Alpha Tau Omega
description: To bind men together in a brotherhood based upon eternal and immutable principles, with a bond as strong as right itself and as lasting as humanity; to know no North, no South, no East, no West, but to know man as man, to teach that true men the world over should stand together and contend for supremacy of good over evil; to teach, not politics, but morals; to foster, not partisanship, but the recognition of true merit wherever found; to have no narrower limits within which to work together for the elevation of man than the outlines of the world: these were the thoughts and hopes uppermost in the minds of the founders of the Alpha Tau Omega Fraternity.
summary: Since 1865, ATO has promoted the social, educational, cultural, and spiritual development of all members. As America’s Leadership Development Fraternity, ATO provides a great opportunity for young men who are looking for a truly unique experience.

name: Circle K International
description:
Circle K International is the largest collegiate community service organization in the world, with over 13,000 members. Circle K clubs can be found on over 500 different college campuses in 17 different countries. Our mission is to serve the children of the world while developing our members into responsible citizens and leaders with a lifelong commitment to community service worldwide, representing our motto "Live to Serve Love to Serve."

We provide constructive opportunities for students to be involved on their campuses and communities through service work for others in need. We base our projects and work on our three tenets of service, leadership, and fellowship!

Join us for our general meetings during the 2025-2026 year online through Discord or in-person at 6 pm every Wednesday. All links and meeting details can be found on our social media!

UNLV CKI Discord

UNLV CKI Facebook Group

UNLV CKI Instagram
summary: Circle K International is the oldest student-led collegiate service organization. We operate on our tenants Service, Leadership & Fellowship to develop ourselves into young professionals and value being a servant leader within our community.

name: Psychology Club
description:
The Psychology Club (in tandem with Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology) hosts an array of interactive workshops, civic engagement initiatives, and guest speaker events.

If you are interested in psychology and psychology-related fields, Psychology Club is the organization for you! If you do not meet the requirements for Psi Chi, then Psychology Club is an ideal alternative for those interested and eager to acquire knowledge in the field of psychology, and for those highly engaged in their academic growth - therefore, our only requirement for joining is expressed interest in the aforementioned.

Involved students also have the opportunity to become more familiar with the UNLV Psychology Department. Joining Psychology Club is a great way to become involved with a cohort of individuals that possess similar ambitions and goals, as well as to become informed about pursuing a post-baccalaureate education in, and related to, the field of psychology.
summary: UNLV's Psychology Club is an organization for students who are interested in psychology and who may want to become active in the field. This is great opportunity to network with faculty and students with common interests and goals!

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc.
description:
The Theta Sigma Chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. was chartered on the campus of the University of Nevada, Las Vegas on June 5th, 1976 by 7 estute men. These men were Ray Carillo, Curtis Cook, Al Greer, Ray Guiterrez, Edward May, Freewin Austin, and Andre Thorton. The motto of Kappa Alpha Psi is "Achievement in Every Field of Human Endeavor." 

Our Objectives are:

1. To unite college men of culture, patriotism, and honor in a bond of fraternity.

2. To encourage honorable achievement in every field of human endeavor

3. To promote the spiritual, social, intellectual and moral welfare of it's members.

4. To assist the aims and purposes of colleges and universities.

5. To inspire service in the public's interest.
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi was founded Jan, 5 1911 by 10 African American men on the campus of Indiana University in Bloomington, Indiana. This makes it the first predominately black fraternity founded at a predominately white university.

name: Rebel Dancesport
description: We are the premier Ballroom and Latin dance team on campus that teaches both social and competitive Ballroom dance. We represent UNLV and Las Vegas in DanceSport competitions across the country! Our goal is to build strong social and competitive dancers who will desire to continue dancing long after their time at UNLV.
summary: Rebel DanceSport is the premier Ballroom and Latin dance team on campus! We compete at colleges all over the country, and we would love to share our passion with you!

name: Financial Management Association, UNLV
description: The Financial Management Association of UNLV is a non-profit student organization whose goal is to offer support and development to students interested in pursuing careers in the field of Finance. Our core focus is aligning student members with finance professionals throughout the country in order to help create and maintain productive long-term business relationships. FMA strives for excellence both in and out of the classroom while providing students with opportunities to build their professional expertise and business relationships.
summary: The UNLV Financial Management Association is a non-profit student organization offering support and development to students interested in the field of Finance.

name: American Marketing Association, UNLV
description:
The UNLV chapter of the American Marketing Association (AMA) provides students of all majors the opportunity to network and interact with professionals, as well as incorporate practical application of business and marketing principles. We also provide assistance in professional development. We are affiliated with the national AMA and work closely with the local Las Vegas AMA professional chapter. Join via our website linked below!

By having high standards of quality for all of our activities, the organization hopes to provide the best possible opportunities for its members to learn marketing by experiencing it. We are dedicated to the growth, development and success of our members and our community. 

Our Principles roles include:

Informing: Providing resources, education, career and professional development opportunities
Connecting: Serving as a conduit to foster knowledge sharing and relationship building 
Advancing: Promoting/supporting marketing practice and thought leadership

 

Email us at: unlvama@gmail.com

OR

DM us on Instagram: @unlvama
summary: The UNLV Student Chapter of the American Marketing Association (AMA) provides students of all majors the opportunity to network and interact with professionals, as well as incorporate practical application of business and marketing principles.

name: `Ewalu Club
description: The 'Ewalu Club at the University of Nevada Las Vegas is a student organization created to spread the Aloha spirit and Polynesian culture with the Las Vegas community. With our motto, "Eight Perspectives, One Destination" we seek students from our pacific islands but extend a warm welcome to students from all walks of the Earth. Through our community service activities, fundraisers, socials, and other events, our objective is to share our traditions and way of life in order to build strong relationships between the Polynesian student population and the local community.
summary: The 'Ewalu Club at the University of Nevada Las Vegas is a student organization created to spread the Aloha spirit and Polynesian culture with the Las Vegas community.

name: UNLV Boxing
description: The Boxing Club at UNLV is dedicated to training at competing at the highest level of collegiate competition. We compete in the USIBA United States Intercollegiate Boxing Association. We do not require that you compete, however competing members do get priority in the gym, in regards to coaching. We are located in MPE 317, the gym is normally open Monday-Friday. Please contact us instagram @unlv.boxing regarding training hours as they tend to change per semester and upon availabilty
summary: boxing, athletics, national sport, club sport

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description: The Multicultural Greek Council at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas hereby strives to be a proactive entity that will seek the development and continuing growth of its participating organizations. It is of the utmost importance to promote unity, as a means of increasing resources; promote respect and equality; providing a network for involved students to grow and improving retention of minorities in higher education.
summary: Greek, Fraternity, Sorority, Multicultural, diversity, women, men, sisterhood, brotherhood, cultural,

name: Association Representing Koreans
description: Association Representing Koreans (A.R.K.) is for anyone who is interested in Korean culture or Hospitality Industry. The objective of A.R.K. is to provide a network and share information on work opportunities and promote Korean culture through various events!
summary: UNLV, Hospitality, Korean, ARK, Dr. Yen-Soon Kim, multicultural, international, club

name: Muslim Students Association
description: The Muslim Students' Association works for the betterment of the Muslim and Non-Muslim community on campus through community service, education and social events. Our activities include social gatherings, educating all individuals about Islam, connecting with other multicultural organizations, campus guest lecturers, and other outreach programs.
summary: Uniting the UNLV community through knowledge, social gatherings, uniting with other multicultural organizations, and spreading awareness of Islam on campus.

name: Lambda Kappa Delta
description:
Lambda Kappa Delta is an honorary society for undergraduate students pursuing the health care discipline of Physical Therapy. Encouraging and recognizing scholastic excellence, Lambda Kappa Delta also prepares students for admission to Physical Therapy school by providing professional development opportunities, informational sessions regarding Physical Therapy school requirements, networking and service forums, as well as lectures by professionals in the field.  These offerings are designed to assist students in their pursuit of the physical therapy profession.

 

Connect With Us:

Join our Discord Group Chat
Follow us on Instagram
Check out our Website
summary: Pre-Physical Therapy Honor Society

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi
description: Alpha Epsilon Pi, the Global Jewish Fraternity, was founded to provide opportunities for a Jewish man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience. We have maintained the integrity of our purpose by strengthening our ties to the Jewish community and serving as a link between a man's development in college and their career. Alpha Epsilon Pi develops leadership for the Jewish community at a critical time in a young man's life.
summary: Fraternity, Jewish, Social, Philanthropy, Leadership, AEPi, Development, Academics, Community Service

name: Italian Club
description:
The club dedicates itself to the study of the Italian language and culture. Its activities celebrate Italian holidays, music, life, regions, and cuisine.

Our purpose is to raise awareness of the culture, dialects, and slang of Italy through many engaging events.
summary: The club will dedicate itself to the study of the Italian language and culture. It will have activities celebrating Italian holidays, music, life, regions, and the various foods. The club's goal is to bring awareness and organize engaging events

name: Black Graduate Student Association
description:
The University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) Black Graduate Student Association (BGSA) is dedicated to enhancing the graduate experience at UNLV. BGSA is a recognized student organization whose mission is to support graduate and professional students of African descent at UNLV by offering a safe space for dialogue, networking, and provide opportunities to develop both professionally and academically.

 

BGSA was established to serve as a foundation for building unity, support, and academic excellence among graduate students of African descent at UNLV and the Las Vegas community. These goals are accomplished by providing our members with academic and professional resources, and hosting events that not only cultivate our social experiences, but ideally enhance our overall well-being. We also design experiences that focuses on:

  • Recruitment for membership open to graduate and professional students who are interested in supporting students of color in the pursuit of graduate and professional educational excellence
  • Building relationships and working with undergraduate students at UNLV to increase the pipeline to graduate school
  • Academic and research support towards graduate and professional goals
  • An active space for fellowship and to foster community among graduate students of African descent both inside and outside the University
  • Community service and activism with the aim of giving back to Black communities throughout the Las Vegas

Membership Benefits:

  • Exposure and access to other minority scholars, administrators, and professionals at UNLV and Las Vegas
  • Tools that can aid graduates and professionals in their studies and degree completion
  • Joining a network of scholars of African descent who are dedicated to serving the larger Black community
summary: The BGSA is an organization that promotes the empowerment, professional development, and scholarship of students of color navigating through higher learning. It has an expectation of excellence in students within-and outside-the university culture.

name: International Business Association
description:
The International Business Association (IBA) was founded in 2002. As a student-led organization, our mission is to support the professional, educational, and social growth of students interested in global business. While our focus is on international business, we welcome students from all majors and all class standings. IBA provides valuable skills and experiences for navigating today’s interconnected world. We aim to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world applications by connecting students with business executives and industry leaders who possess international experience.

 

Our four core values:

  1. Be Cultured: Embrace diverse perspectives and global experiences.
  2. Make Connections: Build strong networks with professionals and peers.
  3. Grow Your Career: Develop essential skills for business success.
  4. Invest In The Community: Contribute positively, both locally and globally.

 

Follow us on Instagram: @unlviba

Join our Flare group: https://flare-event.app.link/vfZf4uBDgTb
summary: Our mission is to support students in their professional, educational, and social development within the field of international business.

name: Delta Kappa International Marriage and Family Therapy Honor Society
description: Delta Kappa, the International Marriage and Family Therapy Honor Society, is an academic and professional institution to provide marriage and family therapy professionals a face-to-face, ongoing scholarly forum for the exchange of ideas related to the development of research, theory, and clinical practice.
summary: honor society, delta, kappa, zeta, delta kappa, marriage and family therapy, MFT, couples and family therapy, CFT

name: University Association of Social Work Students
description: The University Association of Social Work Students is an organization that focuses on cultivating professional connections between those in the social work field, both at UNLV and in the community. UASWS strives to be a forum through which social work students and professionals can meet to share ideas, experiences, and to learn from each other. The club also acts as a liaison between the UNLV School of Social Work and its BSW/MSW students to ensure that everyone's voice is heard. The organization unites students with social work professionals, agencies, and resources in the community through social and educational events, fundraisers, and community service projects.
summary: UASWS strives to cultivate connections between students and professionals within the social work field, both at UNLV and within the community through special events, volunteering and empowerment, as well as social activities.

name: Latino Youth Leadership Alumni
description: The Latino Youth Leadership Alumni is an organization for those that have participated in the Latino Youth Leadership Conference. LYLA strives to increase graduation rates; maintain strong family, cultural, and community values; keep the familia aspect of the conference alive and build a better and stronger community for all alumni here at UNLV. This will be achieved by providing community service, fundraising, social events, and workshops to connect members to their roots.
summary: The Latino Youth Leadership Alumni is an organization for those that have participated in the Latino Youth Leadership Conference.

name: Alpha Xi Delta
description: Alpha Xi Delta, one of the nation's oldest women's fraternities, is an organization dedicated to inspiring its members to realize their fullest potential since 1893. Our philanthropy is The Kindly Hearts Initiative, which we are all very passionate about our cause and will continue doing our part for the community. Currently, we are locally partnered with Shannon West Homeless Youth to provide services for children and teens experiencing homelessness in the Las Vegas Valley. Our chapter is a unique group made up of many wonderful women. Our members represent many different academic majors, religious faiths, ethnic heritages, and life backgrounds, but we all are united in the common bonds of sisterhood and friendship. The Iota Epsilon chapter is located at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas and was chartered in 1999.
summary: Alpha Xi Delta is a national women's fraternity that was founded in 1893. Alpha Xi Delta is dedicated to enriching the lives of all our members and inspiring our sisters to realize their potential.

name: UNLV Debate Team
description:
The UNLV Debate Team represents UNLV at intercollegiate debate tournaments at universities across America. The team is dedicated to competitive excellence and campus and community outreach to promote debate and informed civic discourse.

People wanting to join should go to https://www.unlv.edu/debate/join to fill out our application! (Don't just hit join on the RSO page, we want the application on our website!)
summary: The UNLV Debate Team represents UNLV at intercollegiate policy debate tournaments at universities across America.  The team is dedicated to competitive excellence and campus and community outreach to promote debate and informed civic discourse.

name: UNLV Collegiate DECA
description: DECA is an association of students interested in marketing, business, hotel management, restaurant management, finance, and professional development. We compete at an international conference with universities from the 50 states, Canada, and other countries. Join Collegiate DECA and experience unique opportunities Collegiate DECA can provide for you.
summary: DECA prepares emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality and management in colleges and universities around the globe.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
Join SHPE to be part of our "familia"!

SHPE (Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers) is an organization dedicated to empowering the Hispanic community in STEM fields, while welcoming all students. It is a UNLV branch of the national organization.

SHPE offers a supportive community that focuses on:

  • Professional Development and Networking: Connect with peers and professionals, and build skills for the workforce.
  • Community and Empowerment: Foster a sense of belonging and support among students.

We have a packed schedule of future events, including:

  • Fun activities and guest speakers.
  • Preparation for the national conference, a great opportunity to meet employers.

How to Join:

Follow us on Instagram at @shpedeunlv and find us on the involvement fair under Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers to stay updated.

 

Hiring for Leadership:

For those interested in taking on a leadership role, we are currently looking to fill positions for the upcoming semester, including:

  • Event Coordinator

Fill out this interest form if you would like to be considered.
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support and development.

name: IPSM Student Leaders
description: IPSM Student Leaders aims to bring together students who are interested in pursuing a career in the sports industry. To complement our master's degree program in Intercollegiate and Professional Sports Management, and our undergraduate certificate program in Sports Leadership and Management, our mission is to help students gain sports industry experience and connections.
summary: IPSM Student Leaders aims to bring together students who are interested in pursuing a career in the sports industry.

name: UNLV Food Pantry
description:
The goal of the UNLV Food Pantry is to provide support for members of the UNLV community struggling to afford food through a discreet system, which allows Rebels to help one another. Through tremendous organizations amongst the UNLV community, the pantry has partnered with to receive any donations from the collections bins and/or ongoing campus-wide food-drives. You can find our collection bins located in: Lied Library, Student Union, and the Student Wellness Recreation Center (AKA the gym).

 

If you wish to be a beneficiary of this program or know of UNLV community members in need, food items will be available on varying dates and times to accommodate customer’s schedules. These hours are posted monthly and available on our website: https://www.unlv.edu/integratedhealth/food-pantry Location:

 

The UNLV Food Pantry is located at: 4646 University Center Driver Las Vegas, NV 89119

A UNLV issued ID is required for those who utilize the Food Pantry.
summary: The UNLV Food Pantry collects donations from the UNLV community to assist students and staff during these tough economic times.

name: Las Vegas Fraternity Association
description:
The Las Vegas Fraternity Association has been on UNLV’s campus for more than 50 years. During that time, LVFA has seen tens of thousands of men through their collegiate experiences and transformed those men into empowered leaders and established professionals.

 

LVFA is also responsible for coordinating council-wide activities and educational programming and encouraging harmonious inter-fraternity relations between the chapters.

 

The mission of the Las Vegas Fraternity Association is to promote opportunities for further development, expansion, and improvement of fraternity life and to support the efforts of its chapters in the pursuit of these improvements.
summary: The Las Vegas Fraternity Association (LVFA) is the governing council of 6 fraternities that encourages leadership, social growth, and community service.

name: Rebel Events Board (REB)
description:
Rebel Events Board (REB) is the event planning board on UNLV's campus!

Mission Statement: The Rebel Events Board is a student-driven organization dedicated to creating, planning, and executing campus-wide events in order to encourage a vibrant campus culture at UNLV. REB provides opportunities for students to develop leadership and event-planning skills as well as a way for students to become more connected to the campus community.


To find out more information about our events or how to join REB, follow us on Instagram at @rebunlv!
summary: We are the Campus Programming Board, an organization that plans major campus-wide events, such as Premier UNLV, Homecoming, Rebels After Dark, UNLV's Got Talent, and Open Mic Night!

name: Kappa Delta Chi
description:
Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Incorporated (also known as KDChi) is a Latina founded and multiculturally expanding sorority. Our sisterhood focuses itself on professional development, academic excellence, leadership, graduating all of its members, and service with an emphasis on the Hispanic/Latino population, all while creating a lifetime of priceless friendships, bonds, and unforgettable memories. Founded in 1987, it has become one of the strongest networks of diverse leaders in the country. Having more than 60 chapters from coast to coast, KDChi provides more than 30,000 hours of community service annually to our communities! 

The Alpha Zeta Chapter of Kappa Delta Chi is the first Latina-based Sorority in the State of Nevada, and the first community service and academics-based sorority at UNLV! The purpose of Kappa Delta Chi is to promote the values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity, and Leadership among women. This sisterhood aims to hold itself to a high standard and to set a positive example to the Las Vegas community. 

 

To learn more about this wonderful organization, follow us on our social media! (Links down below!) 

@unlvkdchi

Feel free to email us at with any questions regarding recruitment!

alphazeta.president@kappadeltachi.org

alphazeta.nme@kappadeltachi.org
summary: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority Inc. is a Latina-oriented academic and community service based organization established in Texas Tech University in 1987. Chartered at UNLV in 2004 as Alpha Zeta Chapter. Our values are: Unity, Honesty, Integrity and Leadership.

name: Sigma Omega Nu
description: Sigma Omega Nu was founded on November 6, 1996. It was the first Latina interest sorority founded and established at California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo. At the time, no group existed specifically for the academic, cultural, or family support of Latina women pursuing higher education. The eight Latina women who founded the sorority felt that there was a need for a strong Latina voice on their campus and so they created an organization that would fulfill that unmet need. Since then, Sigma Omega Nu's commitment to academic and family support has spread throughout California, Colorado, and Nevada. Today, Sigma Omega Nu's membership is made up of diverse women from all ethnic, cultural, religious, political, and lifestyle backgrounds.
summary: Academics, Cultura, Sisterhood, Community Service, Sorority, Leadership, Empowerment, Roots, History, Sunnies, Latina, Multicultural, SON, Sigma Omega Nu, Friendship, Bonds, Sun, Black Burgundy and Gold

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
description:
Zeta Tau Alpha Fraternity, is an international women's fraternity founded October 15th, 1898 at the State Female Normal School in Farmville, Virginia.

The mission statement of Zeta Tau Alpha is to make a difference in the lives of our members by developing the potential of each individual through innovative programming, which emphasizes leadership development, service to others, academic achievement and continued personal growth for women, with a commitment to friendship and the future based on the sisterhood, values and traditions of the past. 

The purpose of Zeta Tau Alpha is the intensifying of friendship, the fostering of a spirit of love, the creating of such sentiments, the performing of such deeds, and the moulding of such opinions as will be conducive to the building up of a purer and nobler womanhood in the world.
summary: Zeta, Tau, Alpha, ZTA, Think Pink, Breast Cancer Awareness and Education, female fraternity, leadership, sisterhood, social, scholarship

name: Chinese Martial Arts Association
description: We are a registered UNLV student organization dedicated to the practice of traditional Chinese martial arts. We encourage anyone who has ever had an interest in learning martial arts--beginners and experts alike--to join us at one of our weekly meetings and see what you've been missing!
summary: Kung Fu, Martial Arts, Chinese, International, Diversity, Association, Northern Shaolin Kung-fu, International, Student organization

name: Hillel at UNLV
description:
Hillel is the UNLV Jewish Student Center. We provide college students the opportunity to explore their Jewish identity through their faith, culture, community, history, and connection to the land of Israel. Whether you're interested in FREE TRIPS TO ISRAEL (for Jews and non-Jews alike!), social activities, Tikkun Olam (community service projects), or Israeli educational programs, Hillel has something to offer you.

Like and follow our instagram page Hillel Las Vegas at https://www.instagram.com/hillellasvegas/ to stay up to date on upcoming events! Message us on Instagram to be added to our WhatsApp Chats as well!

For more information please contact us at kat@jewishnevada.org
summary:
Hillel Las Vegas is the Center of Jewish and pro-Israel Student Life for the Vegas Valley.
FREE TRIPS TO ISRAEL, social events, community service, educational events, etc...
See our Facebook page, Hillel Las Vegas, for info on upcoming events!

name: Alpha Psi Rho
description: Alpha Psi Rho, or APsiRho, is an Asian/Pacific Islander-Based Fraterntiy that is built upon the pillars of Brotherhood, Academics, Prosperity, and Strength while promoting Asian/Pacific Islander awareness. We also demonstrate these values by constantly giving back to the UNLV and overall Las Vegas community. Established at San Diego State University in 2000, eleven years later, the UNLV Chapter of Alpha Psi Rho has recently been established and earns the title as the first and only Asian/Pacific Islander-Based Fraternity at UNLV. We pride ourselves in being high-caliber, quality gentlemen.
summary: Asian, Pacific Islander, Asian-Interest, Asian awareness, Fraternity, multicultural, brotherhood, community service, gentlemen

name: UNLV Pre-Dental Society
description: UNLV Pre-Dental Society is a student organization offered to any student interested in dentistry. The purpose of this organization is to increase the exposure of oral health awareness and help educate students in their application process. The Pre-Dental Society is directly affiliated with the UNLV School of Dental Medicine where most events are held at. We offer opportunities such as assisting in the children's and veteran's health clinics and radiology courses. The Pre-Dental Society is a club striving for excellence in future dental students. For more information please visit linkfly.to/unlvpredentalsociety.
summary: Dental, Pre-Dental, Dentist, Dentistry, Club, UNLV, Pre-Professional, Science, Academic, Honorary, Volunteer, Healthcare, Student Organization

name: Lee Business School Ambassadors
description: The Lee Ambassadors are a group of students who represent the college in many of the functions and events that take place at the Lee Business School. This is an elite group of ten members that are the face of the 3,600+ students that are a part of the Lee Business School. Whether it is small coffee breaks or large-scale events such as the Nevada Business Hall of Fame, the Lee Ambassadors are there ensuring the event is flowing smoothly, while positively representing the business college.
summary: Lee Business School Ambassadors, Ambassadors, Lee Business School, LBS, UNLV, Academics, Business

name: American Society of Landscape Architects, UNLV Student Chapter
description: The UNLV Student Chapter of ASLA represents the student landscape architecture community in Southern Nevada, more specifically Clark County. ASLA-UNLV is a small chapter, but very active due to the dedication of our members and sponsors. We are highly integrated with the NVASLA which is the Nevada Professional Chapter of ASLA. As an organization, we are encouraged to travel to national conferences, to conduct community outreach, and to network throughout the country.
summary: Landscape architecture embodies the art and science of design, planning, and management of the land. Through our education and advocacy, we aim to create a sustainable living environment and make a positive impact on our surroundings and society.

name: 91.5 HD2 - The Beat
description:
KUNV 91.5 HD2 - The Beat is a NSHE student-run non-commercial, Over the air radio station in Nevada, playing Top 40, Hip-Hop, R&B, and EDM music to talk shows and sports broadcasts. Our mission is to provide NSHE students with thing the knowledge needed for a career in the broadcast industry.

*Open to any NSHE student, regardless of major and institution.
summary: We are KUNV, 91.5 The HD2 - The Beat, Power by NSHE Students across Nevada! The silver State's only college over the air station.

name: UNLV German Club
description:
At UNLV, the German Club brings together a diverse group of students with a shared interest in the language and culture of German-speaking countries. It hosts a weekly conversation table, welcoming participants of all proficiency levels to hone their conversational abilities. We also organize events throughout each semester to celebrate their traditions and festivities.

Follow our social media pages below!
summary: The UNLV German Club brings together a diverse group of students with a shared interest in the language and culture of German-speaking countries, hosting a weekly conversation table and organizing culturally themed events throughout each semester.

name: Psychology Clinical Student Committee
description: Student committee for the Clinical Psychology PhD program.
summary: psychology, graduate student, clinical

name: Latter-Day Saint Student Association
description: LDSSA welcomes anyone that wants to come to the Institute on the south side of campus to relax, play ping pong, study, or boost your spiritual side with weekly religious classes. Every semester the Institute is full of activity with LDSSA members and others that enjoy the atmosphere. Come on over and see what's really going on for yourself!
summary: LDSSA, church, Jesus Christ, saints, religious, Christian, Mormon, Institute

name: Residence Hall Association, UNLV
description:
The UNLV Residence Hall Association (RHA) plans fun activities for multiple identities of students living on campus, advocates on behalf of residents to administration and creates stronger community ties through leadership conferences.

RHA is chartered with the Pacific Affiliate of College and University Residence Halls (PACURH) under the National Association of College and University Residence Halls (NACURH). 

Awards

WINNER: 2020 "Program of the Year" award from the Intermountain Affiliate of College and University Residence Halls.

WINNER: 2018 "Outstanding Advocacy Initiative" award from the the National Association of College University Residence Halls. 

WINNER: 2017 "School of the Year" award from the National Association of College University Residence Halls. 

WINNER: 2016 Outstanding Student Organization from the UNLV Rebel Awards
summary: The UNLV Residence Hall Association (RHA) plans fun activities for multiple identities of students living on campus, advocates on behalf of residents to administration and creates stronger community ties through leadership conferences.

name: Omega Delta Phi Fraternity Inc.
description: Since its beginning in 1987, Omega Delta Phi has been an organization perpetually dedicated to the idea of constant growth and improvement. As such, we face the new challenges of tomorrow with a growing sense of optimism and newfound awareness that “good” will not be good enough. We must work to form an organization which not only challenges the traditional perception of fraternities, but also one with deals with the issues of culture and race for a completely new perspective. Thinking outside the box is no longer an acceptable plateau; the box must now be removed completely.
summary: Omega Delta Phi is a service/social fraternity that aims at graduating its members while giving back to the community

name: Student Technologist Association in Radiological Sciences
description: S.T.A.R.S. (Student Technologist Association in Radiological Sciences) is an organization that was formed in 1998 whose purpose is to unite all students in the Medical Imaging Programs at UNLV. Our goal is to give our students academic support, pride in the profession, and an avenue to network. Essentially, it was, and always will be a group that helps, assists, and enhances students to be better professionals. Our students are a diverse group of individuals which vary both in age and backgrounds. S.T.A.R.S. helps blend the diversity of our group into a cohesive family type group which fosters teamwork, respect, and a mutual goal of being the best we can be.
summary: X-ray, Radiography, Radiology, Ultrasound, Medical Imaging, Nuclear Medicine, CMI

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated
description: The purpose of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority Inc. is to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improve their social stature, to maintain a progressive interest in college life, and to be of service to all mankind.
summary: The first historically African American Sorority founded on January 15, 1908. The Kappa Xi chapter at UNLV maintains important ties with the community and is apart of the National Pan-Hellenic Council.

name: Indian Students Association Maitri
description: The Indian Student Association (ISA) is a vibrant student organization dedicated to fostering connections among Indians and those interested in Indian culture. Committed to providing a welcoming and safe environment, ISA serves as a platform for individuals to explore, celebrate, and engage with the rich tapestry of Indian traditions, values, and practices. Through a diverse array of events and meetings, ISA aims to showcase the beauty and diversity of Indian culture, offering opportunities for students from all backgrounds to partake in cultural experiences, festivities, and educational activities. Whether through festive celebrations, cultural showcases, or informative discussions, ISA endeavors to create an inclusive community where members can forge meaningful connections and deepen their appreciation for Indian heritage.
summary: ISA is a vibrant community celebrating the diverse cultures of India. We offer a welcoming space for students to connect, learn, and grow together through cultural events, discussions, and service initiatives.

name: Best Buddies
description:
Best Buddies is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment and leadership development for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD).  

 

UNLV Best Buddies is a newer organization at the University of Nevada-Las Vegas. Our mission is to build one-to-one friendships between students with and without IDD. Our goal is to make UNLV an inclusive campus. Join Best Buddies and make unforgettable memories and life long friendships! If you have any questions or concerns please don't hesitate to email nunezdel@unlv.nevada.edu
summary: Friendship, Inclusion, Diversity

name: MIS Society
description:
Welcome to the Management Information Systems Society – your gateway to an empowered academic journey at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. Our mission is to create an all-inclusive platform where classroom theories come to life through practical applications, while fostering invaluable connections within both our student community and the world beyond.

Discover MIS Society Activities:

  • Guest Speakers: Hear from IT managers, data scientists, and more, as they share career insights.
  • Hands-on Workshops: Learn data analysis, coding, and cybersecurity tools used in MIS.
  • Network Opportunities: Connect with alumni, faculty, and peers who love MIS.
  • Cutting-edge Talks: Explore AI, blockchain, and MIS trends in dynamic seminars.
  • Pro Development: Master interviews, resumes, and communication for internships and jobs.
  • Social Mixers: Unwind, connect, and forge lasting friendships with fellow members!

Join us at linktr.ee/unlvmissociety or contact missociety@unlv.edu
summary: MIS Society is a student organization that offers professional development opportunities to graduate and undergraduate students interested in Management Information Systems and related technologies.

name: Rebel Vets Organization
description:
Rebel Veterans Organization is to support veterans and military-connected family members in their higher education pursuits & goals beyond college. We advocate for policies that help our members successfully navigate their academic careers.

 

The Rebel Veterans Organization strives to provide responsive academic, social, professional, and administrative support to student-veterans, reservists, guardsmen, and active-duty members & their spouses.

 

Our goal is to build camaraderie between veterans and civilians within the UNLV community, pursue opportunities that strengthen our existing ties within the Las Vegas community, and aid veterans as they transition into successful new careers.

 

We work with UNLV alumni to help our members network for those future professional opportunities and the organization continues to serve the public by helping those in need through various community service projects.

 

We are proud to be one of 1,500 chapters of Student Veterans of America.
summary: We are an organization that welcomes all members who are veterans, active duty, spouses, guardsmen, dependents, and civilians. We are a community that supports each other, strengthens community ties, and assists with future endeavors.

name: UNLV Spectrum
description:
UNLV Spectrum is an undergraduate student organization dedicated to the queer/LGBTQ community and its allies. We focus on activism, education, outreach, and creating a safe place for LGBTQ+ students on campus where they can be who they are and find likeminded people. Meeting times and location will be posted on all of our socials as they are planned out.

Keep in touch with us:

(Mostly Discord)

Join our discord server here!

Instagram

Facebook

Twitter

Tags: gay, queer, lgbt, lgbtq, lesbian, trans, transgender, nonbinary, bisexual, activism, social justice
summary: UNLV Spectrum is an undergraduate student organization dedicated to the queer/LGBTQ+ community and its allies. We focus on activism, education, outreach and safe spaces.

name: Phi Alpha Delta (Social Work)
description: Phi Alpha was originally organized in 1962 to provide a closer bond among students of social work and to promote humanitarian goals and ideals nationally. Our local chapter, the Delta Chapter, was founded at UNLV in May 1982 and welcomes students who have attained excellence in scholarship and achievement in social work. Lifetime dues are $60 by check or money order.
summary: honor society, social work, justice, services, education,

name: UNLV Student Nurses Association
description:
The Student Nurses Association (SNA) is a student organization dedicated to providing leadership opportunities, broadening knowledge, exercising community involvement, and advocating for and contributing to advances in nursing education.

All nursing students are eligible for membership within SNA and are urged to join!

Students in the School of Nursing founded the Student Nurses Association in 1972. This association is now a local chapter of the National Student Nurses Association (NSNA).

The Goals of SNA are to:

  • Ensure quality nursing education
  • Provide service to the Las Vegas area
  • Support fellow students through the mental rigors of nursing school
  • Advocate a strong sense of community through all levels of nursing
  • Encourage development of leadership qualities and confidence of each member

Advantages of SNA Membership Include:

  • You chose how involved you want to be in SNA
  • SNA is a resume booster
  • Elaborate networking opportunities with other students and facilities
  • Special graduation cords for members who reach 16 SNA hours and graduation medals for members who reach 32 SNA hours
  • Officers receive graduation ceremony recognition and regalia
  • Community service opportunities
summary: Association open to all UNLV nursing students! Dedicated to providing leadership and professional networking opportunities, exercising community involvement, and supporting students throughout their nursing education.

name: Tau Beta Pi
description:
The Tau Beta Pi Association was founded at Lehigh University in 1885 by Edward Higginson Williams, Jr., to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering, and to foster a spirit of liberal culture in engineering colleges.

Contact: tbp@unlv.edu

Tau Beta Pi Website
summary: Tau Beta Pi, TBP, The Engineering Honor Society - Nevada Beta

name: Society of Automotive Engineers
description:
UNLV's Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) design, build, test, and compete in three unique international collegiate vehicle design racing series.

UNLV SAE Rebel Racing was established in 2008. The students in UNLV SAE are tasked with running every aspect of our student organization which includes; organizing interactions with other UNLV organizations, yearly budgeting, acquiring funding, vehicle design and testing, traveling to competition venues and various race day procedures.

Our student organization is not just limited to engineering majors. Any and all majors are welcome to join! Active membership is the best way to supplement your classroom education, and it opens doors for careers after college.


If you have any questions please swing by our office in TBE-B room 175.
summary: We are a team of students who design, build, test, and compete a baja off-roading car in the international Baja SAE collegiate series. All while learning the hands-on application of engineering and how to work in a team atmosphere.

name: Association of Pre-Health Professionals
description:
Association of Pre-Health Professionals is an organization which houses all students that are interested in pursuing a career in all health care fields, including but not limited to: medicine, dentistry, optometry, veterinary, pharmacy, podiatry, and physician's assistance (PA) programs. The organization provides members with opportunities to develop into a competitive applicant via clinical experience, fundraising and humanitarian activities. Such requirements will allow the member to develop in-depth, genuine experiences which in turn cultivate beneficial skills that will help place the member gain an edge when applying to their school of choice. APHP members are held to the highest leadership and professional standards in line with what would be expected of them in their future field. 

Please visit our website here and fill out the New Membership Application to become a member! Please feel free to email aphpunlv@gmail.com with any questions, comments, or concerns!
summary: Pre-med, pre-professional, pre-dental, pre-pharmacy, sciences, pre-health, biology, pre-optometry, medicine, physician assistant, PA, pre-veterinary, podiatry, veterinary volunteer, pre-nursing, humanitarian, wellness, professional development, shadowing

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
"To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."

The National Society of Black Engineers strives to accomplish the following objectives for our organization:

  • Stimulate and develop student interest in the various engineering disciplines
  • Strive to increase the number of minority students studying engineering at both the undergraduate and graduate levels 
  • Encourage members to seek advanced degrees in engineering or related fields and to obtain professional engineering registrations
  • Promote public awareness of engineering and the opportunities for Blacks and other minorities in that profession
  • Function as a representative body on issues and developments that affect the careers of Black Engineers  

Innovative project ideas are generated and implemented throughout the year on the chapter, regional and national levels. Some of NSBE's present activities include tutorial programs, group study sessions, high school/junior high outreach programs, technical seminars and workshops, a national communications network (NSBENET), two national magazines (NSBE Magazine and NSBE Bridge), an internal newsletter, a professional newsletter (Career Engineer, a supplement in NSBE Magazine), resume books, career fairs, awards, banquets and an annual national convention.

We welcome everyone that shares the vision to excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community.

 

Watch our "About UNLV NSBE | 2021-2022" video: https://youtu.be/2JeIWMZo4hk.

 

Member Benefits

    • Access to 20+ companies at regional conferences and over 300 at the National Convention career fair. 
    • Academic support is provided via tutoring and academic achievement programs. 
    • KAPLAN test preparation workshops available at conferences. Discounted Kaplan Test Prep services and materials. You can also receive a chance to win a free test prep class offered by Kaplan.
    • Leadership Development – Receive exceptional training at regional conferences, the National Convention, and through your local chapter. You also have the opportunity to serve on a Regional and/or National Executive Board. 
    • Post your resume free of charge to top engineering companies in NSBE’s Job Placement Center (JPC) (need link). Search for internships, co-ops, and full-time job opportunities. 
    • Apply for various scholarships and grants. Scholarships are awarded every year to deserving undergraduate students. Many of our scholarships do not require a GPA. 
    • Meet thousands of other students and technical professionals in your field of study. 
    • Compete in a vast number of programs that help prepare you for graduate school and your future career. 
    • Give back to the community through NSBE’s T.O.R.C.H. and Pre-College Initiative programs. 
    • Receive a subscription to NSBE Magazine. Each issue offers the latest industry news and articles tailored to individual areas of engineering. (a $20 value) 
    • Discounts Registrations to conferences and events including the Annual Convention, the largest student planned conferences in the world. Technical training and unparalleled networking opportunities are available at these conferences as you meet people from all over the world.
summary: The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), is a  nonprofit organization that is owned and managed by its members. With more than 24,000 members around the world, NSBE is one of the largest student-governed organizations.

name: National Society of Minorities in Hospitality
description:
2023-2024 National Society of Minorities in Hospitality (NSMH)

The National Society of Minorities in Hospitality is the premier professional organization for hospitality students. We recognize diversity and multiculturalism as anyone can provide value and has the potential to be a leader of today's world. We host industry professionals as speakers, exclusive NSMH property tours, and social events with other hospitality-oriented chapters throughout the year. We are also involved in hosting Hospitality Week and plenty of community service events to build your resume. Join us as we transform today's students into tomorrow's leaders!

The highlight of this organization is taking part in the annual conferences- western regionals and the national conference- where students have the opportunity to meet an array of recruiters and land that dream job in the industry! 

General meetings are held on Wednesdays at 5:30 pm. 

*Dates & Location varies. Contact us at nsmh@unlv.nevada.edu for more info!
summary:
NSMH is the premiere professional organization for hospitality students.

We are an organization of students and professionals representing an array of social and cultural backgrounds and a wealth of unique perspectives and ideas.

name: CSUN Student Government
description:
CSUN Student Government is the autonomous representative governing body of the undergraduate student population of the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. This well-sustained organization is maintained by three branches: Executive, Legislative, and Judicial.
 
The Executive Board oversees all operations. Together, it consists of the Student Body President, Vice President, Senate President, and appoints a Senate President Pro Tempore from the Senate. The President's Cabinet consists of the Executive Director, Chief of Staff, Secretary, and the three directors overseeing department programs. The cabinet advises and supports the branch with any internal and external matters. The Executive Branch also supervises all directors, associate directors, and assistant directors, appointed to various departments within CSUN.

The Legislative Branch of student government is the Senate. The senate votes on various bills and funding proposals throughout the year. Twenty-five senators from all nine colleges make up the senate, with each college receiving proportional representation based on enrollment. The Senate President oversees senators and chairs the senate meetings which take place every Monday at 6:00 PM in Student Union room 208 B/C.

The Judicial Council consists of nine justices, elected to 18-month terms, which include the Chief Justice and Associate Chief Justice. The Judicial Council hears cases relating to CSUN activity, interprets the constitution and bylaws, and swears in CSUN officials. All Judicial Council officials are nominated by the Executive Board and approved by the Senate.
 
https://www.unlv.edu/csun/
summary: CSUN Student Government represents the voice of the students, builds a campus community, provides the entire undergraduate student body with services, scholarships, campus life activities, and funding for registered student organizations.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
description: Since 1956, the American Institute of Architecture Students has been the voice of students to the educational system and the profession of architecture and design in North America and beyond. The association helps to build interest and enrich the educational experience of students (of all ages) and others in architecture and design. The UNLV Chapter of the American Institute of Architecture Students has been active since 1988 and has managed to become stronger, more exciting and more organized with each year. We are the ultimate source for architecture students (or those just interested in the field) to connect, network and grow with other peers pursuing the same goal.
summary: The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is an independent, nonprofit, international student-run organization dedicated to providing unmatched programs, information, and resources on issues critical to architectural education.

name: Phi Kappa Phi
description: Welcome to the Honor Society of Phi Kappa Phi, Chapter 100 at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. Chapter 100, recognized by national headquarters as a 2012 Chapter of Excellence, has been chartered at UNLV since 1967. What started out a group of 20 faculty members and five students has grown into a chapter that initiates approximately 200 new members each year. Phi Kappa Phi is the only national honor society that recognizes superior scholarship in all fields of study and invites into membership the highest ranking students from every branch of learning at more than 300 colleges in the United States, Puerto Rico and the Philippines. Because Phi Kappa Phi is so highly selective, membership is recognized by graduate and professional schools as well as employers. At UNLV, Phi Kappa Phi is the only honor society noted on the university's official transcripts. Members are eligible for local and national awards and fellowships, find mentors and networking opportunities within the society, and receive valuable services and discounts from corporate partners.
summary: Phi Kappa Phi is the only national honor society that recognizes superior scholarship in all fields of study and invites into membership the highest ranking students from every branch of learning at more than 300 colleges.

name: Alpha Phi Gamma
description:
In Spring 2010, Alpha Phi Gamma was established at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. Each sister works towards a common goal: to bring together all women of different nationalities through the bonds of friendship and sisterhood. Alpha Phi Gamma strives to promote Asian Awareness to the University and surrounding communities. For those inspired to follow the footsteps of the founders, this organization promotes scholarship, academic excellence, personal development and leadership.

 

Interested in joining or have questions? Here's how to contact us!

 

Website: https://aphigunlv.wixsite.com/home

 

Instagram: @aphig_unlv

 

Twitter: @aphig_unlv

 

TikTok: @aphigunlv

 

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/APhiG.UNLV1/

 

Email: aphigunlv.recruitment@gmail.com

 

YouTube: Alpha Phi Gamma UNLV
summary: Alpha Phi Gamma - Iota Chapter is the first Asian-American Interest Sorority at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas as well as in the State of Nevada.

name: Alpha Gamma Delta
description: Alpha Gamma Delta is an international womens fraternity that promotes academic excellence, philanthropic giving, ongoing leadership and personal development and above all, a spirit of loving sisterhood. Guided by our Purpose, Alpha Gamma Deltas strive to attain a higher standard, thereby improving their lives, the lives of those around them and the communities in which they live. Together, we continually work to inspire the woman and impact the world.
summary: Alpha Gamma Delta is an international fraternity for women that exists to provide opportunities for personal development through the spirit of sisterhood.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description:
EWB-USA Vision: Our vision is a world in which the communities we serve have the capacity to sustainably meet their basic human needs, and that our members have enriched global perspectives through the innovative professional educational opportunities that the EWB-USA program provides.

EWB-USA Mission: Support community-driven development programs worldwide by collaborating with local partners to design and implement sustainable engineering projects, while creating transformative experiences and responsible leaders.
summary: Engineers Without Borders USA (EWB-USA) builds a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs and equip leaders to solve the world’s most pressing challenges.

name: Intervarsity Christian Fellowship
description:
The PURPOSE of InterVarsity Christian Fellowship is to establish and advance at UNLV witnessing communities of students and faculty who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord: growing in love for God, God's Word, God's people of every ethnicity and culture, and God's purposes in the world.

We accomplished our purpose by creating venues for individuals to hear, act, and respond to the gospel though small group Bible studies, large group meetings, and evangelistic outreaches.

Our VISION is to see the life-transforming love of Jesus reach every student in every corner of UNLV.

Our MISSION is to love God, reach the university, and develop world changers.

One Student's Experience:

“I just have 10 more days in the US until I go back to Brazil. I want to let all of you know that the year I spent here was such a blessing. I'm so thankful God led me to that InterVarsity booth on my first week of Fall semester. At that moment, I did not imagine the amount of GOOD friends I was about to make. I learned and grew a lot being with each one of you. My wish is that God continues to bless this fellowship. My desire is that your passion for missions continues to grow in each heart that belongs to InterVarsity. I hope I will not be forgotten because I won't forget any of you. You guys are very special to me and we will see each other again soon, God willing! Regards, hugs and kisses from your sister in Christ."

"Should I not be concerned about that great campus UNLV in which there are 30,000 students who I love." (Jonah 4:11, paraphrased)

What we believe:

The only true God, the almighty Creator of all things, existing eternally in three persons Father, Son, and Holy Spirit full of love and glory.

The unique divine inspiration, entire trustworthiness, and authority of the Bible.

The value and dignity of all people: created in God's image to live in love and holiness but alienated from God and each other because of our sin and guilt, and justly subject to God's wrath.

Jesus Christ, fully human and fully divine who lived as perfect example, who assumed the judgement due sinners by dying in our place, and ascended as Savior and Lord.

Justification by God's grace to all who repent and put their faith in Jesus Christ alone for salvation The indwelling presence and transforming power of the Holy Spirit, who gives to all believers a new life and a new calling to obedient service.

The unity of all believers in Jesus Christ, manifest in worshiping and witnessing churches making disciples throughout the world. 

The victorious reign and future personal return of Jesus Christ, who will judge all people with justice and mercy, giving over the unrepentant to eternal condemnation but receiving the redeemed into eternal life.
summary: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship's purpose is to establish and advance at UNLV witnessing communities of students and faculty who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord.  We gather to grow in our faith and share the love of Jesus with our campus.

name: Catholic Rebels
description:
We are the St. Thomas Aquinas Newman Center, serving the University of Nevada- Las Vegas (UNLV) campus!

Our mission is to proclaim the Gospel of Jesus Christ on campus supported by the Dominican traditions of Prayer, Study, Community and Preaching.

With so many student organizations at UNLV, our parish serves as a sanctuary for students, staff, and anyone who would like to grow in their faith and be a part of a community. We hope to give back to our communities by helping those in need, using our gifts to help others, offering faith formation, and hosting various social activities.

Students at the Newman Center are always welcome to study, nap, cook, pray, or relax!

Our peer ministers are Alison Begin, Andrew Ferguson, and Gabriella Nault

For more information, follow our social media or our website listed below.
summary: The St. Thomas Aquinas Catholic Newman Center at UNLV provides a faith-based spiritual community for Catholic students on campus!

name: Rebel Ice Hockey Club
description:
Official Site: http://www.rebelhockey.com

Rebel Ice Hockey is an intercollegiate ice hockey team at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. Formed in 2005, the team is governed by the American Collegiate Hockey Association (ACHA). Last season, the Rebels had a best-ever 26-4-1 record and berth in the national tournament semifinals. 

The mission of the University of Nevada, Las Vegas Ice Hockey Club is to provide a competitive ice hockey team at UNLV to compete against other college and university club teams at the local, regional and national level and to promote school pride and spirit. The program also strives to provide student-athletes with leadership opportunities in self-governance, marketing, fundraising and other aspects of managing a sports program.
summary: Rebel Ice Hockey is an intercollegiate ice hockey team at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. Formed in 2005, the team is governed by the American Collegiate Hockey Association. The Rebels look to make a run at nationals during the 2023-2024 ACHA season

name: UNLV Taekwondo Club
description:
What We Practice:

Korean martial art and sport of Taekwondo. Focuses on cultivating the mind, body, and spirit through rigorous training and self-defense techniques using the hands and feet.

 

Our Mission: 

Our mission is to provide a safe and inclusive environment for students to learn and practice the art of taekwondo. We strive to foster a sense of community and respect among our members, while promoting physical fitness, mental discipline, and personal growth. Through regular training and participation in competitions, we aim to develop our skills and represent our club with pride and sportsmanship. Our goal is to inspire a lifelong love of taekwondo and to share its values with our campus and beyond.

 

 

Keep in contact and stay updated with our Social Medias:

https://linktr.ee/unlvtkd
summary: Taekwondo, martial art, Korean, Club Sports, fitness, exercise, Asian

name: UNLV Psi Chi Chapter
description:
UNLV Psi Chi is a student organization designed for ambitious, hard-working students interested in pursuing a future in psychology and other psychology-related fields. Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology, aims to recognize and promote excellence in the science and application of psychology. As one of many chapters for a worldwide recognized organization, UNLV Psi Chi offers guidance for those seeking a post-baccalaureate education through interactive workshops, civic engagement initiatives, and intellectually stimulating reunions. If you are interested in joining the UNLV chapter, please first complete the Psi Chi International application, in addition to our chapter application.

We are accepting new applications at all times throughout the year, however, due to being a primarily student-led entity, please expect delays in the completion of the application process.
summary: Academics | Civic Engagement | Psi Chi International Honor Society and Psychology Club | Psychology

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description:
Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. was established on November 12, 1922 at Butler University.

Our slogan is "Greater Service, Greater Progress"

It is the mission of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. to enhance the quality of life for women and their families in the U.S. and globally through community service, civil, and social action. Our goal is to achieve greater progress in the areas of education, health awareness, and leadership development.
summary: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Incorporated is a historically black organization dedicated to serving the community on the basis of sisterhood, scholarship, and service. The Nu Eta undergraduate chapter was chartered at UNLV on February 20, 1993.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers, UNLV Student Chapter
description: Our mission is to continually strive to encourage the development of a professional consciousness, present the opportunity for civil engineering students to become acquainted to working together effectively, promote a spirit of congeniality, and provide friendly contact with the engineering profession.
summary: The American Society of Civil Engineer UNLV Student Chapter is one of the largest student organizations in the College of Engineering. We are known around campus as the ones who build the legendary Concrete Canoes!

name: Student Nutrition and Dietetic Association
description:
Welcome to the UNLV Student Nutrition and Dietetic Association!

The Student Nutrition and Dietetic Association (SNDA), sponsored by the Nutrition Sciences program at UNLV, provides its members with leadership, networking, and volunteer opportunities while raising awareness about nutrition and physical activity. The association’s creed “By the Students, For the Students” represents the positive impact that its members make in educating the public and helping each other.

We consistently strive to serve our community, and we share a number of volunteer opportunities with our members throughout the year; providing further opportunity to grow and gain nutritional and health related knowledge.

UNLV SNDA regularly volunteers with multiple community organizations, including: Create A Change Now, Food Day, Get Healthy Clark County, JDRF, Safe Nest, The Shade Tree, Three Square & FaVE!, UNLV Food Pantry, and More!

 

We welcome new members and all contributions towards raising awareness about nutrition, health, and well-being.  Anyone interested can get connected: 

  • Website – unlvsnda.com

  • Facebook – www.facebook.com/UNLVSDA

  • Twitter – twitter.com/UNLVSNDA

  • Instagram – instagram.com/unlvsnda 

  • Youtube - SNDA VP Communications (https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCNHj9vdPOgXc2oQtaIald9A)
summary: The UNLV Student Nutrition and Dietetic Association is a student organization dedicated to promoting healthy eating and activities on-campus and in our local communities.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. Alpha Epsilon Omicron Chapter
description: Omega Psi Phi was founded inside the Science Building (later renamed Thirkield Hall) at Howard University located in Washington, D.C., November 17, 1911. The founders were three undergraduates -- Edgar Amos Love, Oscar James Cooper and Frank Coleman. Joining them was their faculty adviser, Ernest Everett Just. Omega Psi Phi is a unique group of men that encourage high standards; community service and assist in the building of character and leadership development among men. Members of Omega share a common foundation comradeship that last a lifetime.
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college consisting of over 750 chapters nationwide.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity Incorporated
description: Culture for Service, Service For Humanity
summary: Founded on Brotherhood, Scholarship and Service

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
description:
Tau Kappa Epsilon (TKE) is an international fraternity that was founded at the Illinois Wesleyan University in 1899, with their core principles of love, charity, and esteem.

The Pi-Lambda Chapter of Tau Kappa Epsilon at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, was installed as a chapter on February 13, 1982.

Our mission is to "to aid men in their mental, moral, and social development for life." In essence, we build Better Men for a Better World.
summary: Making better men for a better world.

name: Chess Club UNLV
description:
OUR COMMUNITY DISCORD: https://discord.gg/jJWRpNaCXE

Chess Club hosts meetups and tournaments that provide a welcoming and accessible environment for all students attending UNLV.

Weekly Meetups will always be free!

UNLV Tournaments entry fees to be determined.

Travel Tournaments require travel and entry fees.

OUR INSTAGRAM: https://www.instagram.com/unlvchessclub
summary: The Chess Club UNLV intends to provide a community and environment for all students to play, learn and watch chess. Tournaments and learning sessions that feature absolute minimal cost in order to spark interest in this ancient game here at UNLV.

name: UNLV BlackJack Battalion
description:
In its 60-year history, the University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) has transformed from a dusty outpost on the south edge of town to a thriving urban research institution. Along the way, thanks to its innovative frontier spirit, UNLV has kept pace with one of the country’s fastest-growing and most enterprising cities.

The Army ROTC program at UNLV is proud of its history; and is one of the best ROTC programs in the western United States. The program trains leadership and other essential skills for students to learn how to lead today's soldiers.

The program is run by Army Officers and Non-commissioned Officers with a wealth of experience.
summary: Army ROTC provides Cadets with the character-building aspects of a diverse, self-disciplined civilian education with tough, centralized leadership development training. Through Army ROTC Cadets will be able to earn a commission in the United States Army.

name: UNLV Bowling Club
description: We are the UNLV Bowling Team. We compete regionally and nationally in intercollegiate tournaments under the United States Bowling Congress collegiate division. We have qualified for postseason national tournaments every year of our existence, and for several years have been ranked in the top 20 among all collegiate teams in the country.
summary: bowling, athletics, sports

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description:
Alpha Delta Pi International Sorority holds the distinction of being the first secret society in the world established for collegiate women. Founded in 1851 at Wesleyan Female College in Georgia, Alpha Delta Pi set the standard for sorority women. The original name was the Adelphean Society, "Adelphean" being derived from the Greek word meaning sister. Our members hold themselves to the highest principles of scholarship, sisterhood, leadership and philanthropy, as our organization continues to expand both nationally and internationally.

 

The Epsilon Rho chapter of Alpha Delta Pi at UNLV will be recruiting again in the Fall of 2025.

Please contact us if you have any questions.

Founded: May 15, 1851

Motto: "We Live for Each Other"

Colors: Azure Blue and White

Symbols: The Lion/Diamond

Flower: Woodland Violet

Jewel: Diamond

Publication: The Adelphean of Alpha Delta Pi

Philanthropy: The Ronald McDonald House Charities

Mascot: "Alphie" the Lion

Chapters: 157+

Members: 250,000+

 

Interested in joining? Please take a moment to visit our website, https://epsilonrho.alphadeltapi.org/ for more information on Fall Recruitment 2025. Hope to see you there!
summary:
Alpha Delta Pi - Epsilon Rho
Be the First. 
Nickname: ADPi
Motto: "We Live For Each Other"
Colors: Azure Blue and White
Symbol: Diamond
Mascot: Lion

name: American Student Dental Association
description: The American Student Dental Association is a national student-run organization that protects and advances the rights, interests and welfare of dental students. It introduces students to lifelong involvement in organized dentistry and provides services, information, education, representation and advocacy.
summary: Dental, ASDA, Dentistry

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description: The National Pan-Hellenic Council, Incorporated (NPHC) is currently composed of nine (9) International Greek letter Sororities and Fraternities: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc., Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc., Iota Phi Theta Fraternity, Inc., Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc., Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. and Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. NPHC promotes interaction through forums, meetings and other mediums for the exchange of information and engages in cooperative programming and initiatives through various activities and functions.
summary: Divine 9, NPHC, BGLO

name: UNLV Women's Lacrosse
description:
The UNLV Women's Lacrosse club is designed to educate interested people about the sport of women's lacrosse. Through practice, every participant will acquire the necessary skills to be successful in the women's game. Through scrimmages and a competitive schedule with teams at and above our level, every player will be challenged and have a chance to function as a member of a team.

If you are brand new to the sport, that is perfect! We love teaching the skills of the game and we have a lot of talented players and role models on the team that will help you pick up the game. Lacrosse is a spring sport but we play a less intense fall season to prepare for the spring. What we have found helpful when we first started playing was Googling or YouTube-ing videos of women’s lacrosse games to see a little of how the game was played and then went onto www.uslacrosse.org to look at the rule book.

There are no tryouts for our team and anyone who wants to play is welcome to come out! We are always looking for new players to grow our roster and to grow the sport of women’s lacrosse in Las Vegas.
summary: Athletic? Love sports? Looking to join a club team? The UNLV Women’s Lacrosse team wants you! There are no tryouts for our team and anyone who wants to play is welcome to come out!

name: UNLV Anime Club
description:
UNLV Anime club is a safe place for UNLV students interested in Japanese animation, manga, and video games. It's an oppurtunity to watch anime, meet new friends, play games, win prizes, and have fun.

 

Meetings are held in the Student Union every Tuesday and Wednesday from 4pm - 8pm during the Fall and Spring semesters. (First half we watch our chosen series and second half we play the game for the week)

 

Our schedule is posted on our Facebook page and Discord Server, along with any updates and announcements during the semester.

 

Events

During the semester, members will participate in events that will earn them points towards a final prize celebration at the end of the semester. Placement in the prize pool will be determined by the amount of points earned throughout the semester.
