Fall 2025 events:

  • Anime Bingo: Anime Club’s go to ice breaker and warmup game for the beginning of the semester. Mark off your waifus and husbandos in popular bingo variants such as standard, double bingo, kite, floating six pack, and blackout. Though this game does not reward points, it does send every winner away with a mini prize.
  • Anime Taboo: Can you describe a word on this card without using certain words? Can you describe this character without naming other characters or the anime it is from? Well this is the game for you! Come describe these anime and pop culture terms to your team without us buzzing you on the listed words below!
  • Murder Club: Something deathly is going down in the club room. A secret organization with a sinister intent is out to destroy the UNLV Anime Club! It is up to the lone wolf investigation squad to uncover these evildoers and lead the rest of the club in making sure that they are brought to justice! Logic and cooperative play are key to your success in this anime club variant of classic party game, Mafia/Slasher/Werewolf.
  • Anime Charades/Pictionary (Parades): Have you ever been playing taboo and thought to yourself, “This would be great with less words and more gesturing?” Well then this is the game for you! Draw a slip of paper and act/draw out the anime creature, character, trope, or object written on it.
  • Anime Jeopardy: Another fun game played in our meeting that is anime based and you have to answer in the form of a question. What is Anime Jeopardy? Correct! Another anime themed version of a classic game show!
  • Game Cafe: A hodge podge of tabletop games to choose from (may or may not be anime related)
  • Arcade Day: Jackbox and other video games to choose from.
  • Minigame Day: A mix of shortened versions of old club games and test rounds for possibly new future featured games.
  • Taskmaster: Inspired by the hit televsion series (all episodes available on Youtube), solve various tasks created by the officers, creativity is the most valuable tool in this game.
  • Cosplay Casino Night: Come in cosplay and play our anime themed casino games for points! No real money is involved. All chips go towards points in club.
  • Anime Ultimate Trivia: Do you know your anime music? And you recognize this scrambled image? Can you pick the odd man out? Can you get this question right about a wide array of anime? Here you go! After many semesters of testing different forms of trivia, we have combined music, picture and text-based trivia into one fun-filled trivia game! 
  • Officer Day: Always wanted to give one of the longer officer challenges a try but never had the time? Take this time to finish collecting officer medals before lucky board.
  • Movie Night: A theme to dress up for, snacks & drinks, and 1-2 movies to get comfy with.
  • Super Mega Ultra Lucky Board: The final event of the semester, this game is based solely on your luck of the draw! Pick an item from the lot and see how that effects you! Points are earned individually and this can make or break your semester! 

 

Not Featured in this Semester’s Lineup

  • Iron Cosplay: Based on the Food Network Show "Iron Chef America," teams have one hour to create a cosplay from fabric, duct tape, staples, hot glue and cardboard. References will be provided for the characters. At the end of the hour, cosplays will be judged on accuracy, creativity, and functionality.
  • Campus RPG: Run around campus. Complete these quests. Earn your rewards. Defeat the boss! Based on a classing role playing game, you will pick a class and venture across campus completing quests and defeating monsters! Can you be the first to defeat the boss?
  • Anime Feud: Students across the campus have answered these 10 question! Can you get the top answers or will your opponent steal it from you?
  • Anime Scattergories: This is a game...of Concentration. No repeats...or hesitations. Category is......whatever we have come up with for that day!
  • Animerica Says: Guess the most popular opinions of the internet on random anime topics like "characters who can beat goku" or "the new Big 3"
  • Anime Wheel of Fortune: Spin the wheel to earn points for your team in an anime themed version of this classic game show!

 

Officer Challenges

Each of the officers creates a challenge for the members to do during the semester. These challenges give an addition 500 points EACH upon completion as well as a specialized medal for your membership badge. After receiving all the medals, you will receive bonus points! 

 

Prizes

Prizes include figures, plushies, DVD sets, manga sets, bags, etc. Anything you can think of, we will try to get. Prizes are first come, first serve only, so be prepared in case the prize you want gets taken. We try to make sure there is something for everyone.

 

Feedback

Feedback is always appreciated! We want to make Anime Club the best environment and experience we can so feel free to let us know what you think! Feel free to email us or visit our Facebook page.

https://www.facebook.com/UNLVAnimeClub

We Also have a Discord!

https://discord.gg/c7TN5zY
summary: UNLV Anime club is a safe place for UNLV students interested in Japanese animation, manga, and video games to watch anime, meet new friends, play games, win prizes, and have fun!

name: UNLV Anthropology Society
description:
STATEMENT OF PURPOSE

The purpose of the Anthropology Society is to provide academic exchange of knowledge related to students within the Anthropology Department and to students who have a general interest in the field. Events provide unique opportunities to experience anthropological field work and promote communication between students and faculty to broaden professional networks.

 

As a member, will also be eligible to apply for our scholarship as well as apply for leadership opportunities within our the organization.

 

Organizational Goals

Encourage and support student research

Provide leadership opportunities

Enhance cultural awareness

Foster academic success

 

DUES

$10 Fall/Spring or $5 for one semester

Dues are paid by cash or check in the Anthropology Department main office: WRI-B first floor.
summary: A student organization aimed at enhancing communication and interaction among students interested in anthropology.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
description:
Hello,

If you are interested in joining AED (the only pre-health honor society at UNLV), please contact our advisor, Dr. Bubb at Dan.Bubb@unlv.edu. After he verifies your GPA (3.3 minimum requirement) we will schedule your new member orientation. We are very excited to welcome new members to the AED NV Beta chapter! For specific questions, please email Francesca Lungu (lunguf1@unlv.nevada.edu) directly with "AED INQUIRY" in the subject line!

The Health Pre-Professional Honor Society, Alpha Epsilon Delta, encourages like-minded individuals to come together, promoting their positive, productive interaction, facilitating communication and empowering students to make better-informed decisions in pursuit of their respective professions in healthcare. AED encourages and recognizes excellence, stimulates an appreciation of the importance of pre-health education, provides a forum for students with common interests, and uses its resources to benefit health organizations, charities and the community.

As the Nevada Beta chapter of this nationally recognized honor society, our members exemplify selfless service and professionalism-- all while having fun! We volunteer both on and off campus with organizations across the valley, benefit from professional development and shadowing opportunities, attend meetings and speaker presentations from healthcare professionals and prospective schools, and build a network of highly motivated individuals that often become lifelong friends.

Please visit our AED Nevada Beta Chapter's website using this link.

For more information about our national office, please visit this link. We hope to see you join!

 

Sincerely,

The Executive Board

Alpha Epsilon Delta, NV Beta
summary: Hello! If you are interested in joining AED (the only pre-health honor society at UNLV), please contact our advisor, Dr. Bubb at Dan.Bubb@unlv.edu. After he verifies your GPA (3.3 minimum requirement) we will schedule your new member orientation!

name: Cinefemmes
description:
CINEFEMMES is a UNLV student organization founded in 2011 by femme filmmakers with the goal of creating and developing a space of support for all filmmakers, both working and aspiring. 
 
The mission of CINEFEMMES at its core is to provide guidance, knowledge, opportunity, support, and inspiration to the UNLV Film community, and to serve filmmakers as a bridge to opportunities. CINEFEMMES was created with the express purpose of assisting filmmakers in forging lasting relationships that can lead to future collaborations and participation in local and out-of-state filmmaking communities. 
 
CINEFEMMES hosts a variety of events, including trivia nights, film screenings, community mixers, and presentations from guest speakers who have found success within the film industry and want to inspire and educate filmmakers with their stories. Furthermore, in 2012, CINEFEMMES created the Femme Award for Best Female Filmmaker, which is presented at UNLV’s student annual film festival, Spring Flicks. 
 
Members are not expected to be a film major/minor and/or part of underrepresented groups in the film industry. 
 
If you or a friend are interested in film, check us out!
summary: CINEFEMMES is an all-inclusive organization that promotes and pursues equality, diversity, representation in the film industry, both on and off the screen.

name: UNLV Toastmasters 976032
description:
Toastmasters, an international non-profit organization created in 1924 in California, helps people from all walks of life improve their communication and leadership skills through public speaking. Joining UNLV Toastmasters will help you become the leader and speaker you want to be.

If you are looking for our location on the day of the meeting, please visit our website at unlvtm.toastmastersclubs.org/directions.html or call Curtis Helt at (702) 501-8043.

public speaking, leadership, developing communication skills, personal growth, professional development, listening skills, decision-making, giving feedback, mentoring
summary: Need to practice for a job interview? Need to give a speech for a communications class? Or just need to get over your fear of public speaking? Then Toastmasters is for you! We help people from all different backgrounds improve their communication skills!

name: Native American Student Association
description: NASA will serve as a social organization dedicated to the preservation of Native culture and the educational attainment at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. NASA will provide members with a social network of fellow Native students, involvement opportunities, resources, and experience necessary for success in collegiate life and beyond.
summary: "Preserving Language, Culture, and Promoting Higher Education"

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description:
The American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) at UNLV are committed to providing a wide variety of experiences and networks to students to aid their growth.  This is done through ASME’s focus on professional development, hard skill workshops, community service activities, social events, and career experiences.  ASME’s unique network spans wide, not only directly connecting students to professionals within engineering and career services, but also to other students both within UNLV and in ASME sections across the country.  From workshops teaching basic engineering skills to facilities tours and socials to simply destress, ASME works to help students develop, connect with one another, and explore a plethora of career options, all while having a great time!  For more information about ASME, join our discord, check out our Instagram, fill out our Google Form, or do all of the above!  Thank you, hope to see you in the very near future!

 

Linktree: https://linktr.ee/asmeunlv

Discord: https://discord.gg/HrsCNtRYsF

Instagram: @asmeunlv

Google Form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScl1CLN_sraVMXGBQo9Iv3BtB8MQVUgfM11XcNnQ-lWL2s1FQ/viewform?usp=sharing
summary: ASME aims to aid student development as both engineers and professionals through workshops, facility tours, and many other activities!  ASME also works on networking, both by hosting club socials and connecting with other schools or professionals!

name: Choral Ensembles, UNLV
description:
Do you want to sing in a choir at UNLV but don't have the money to pay tuition to enroll for credit? Join us as a student club activity!
 
The University Chorale rehearses two days a week, Tuesday and Thursday, from 11:30 AM to 12:50 PM in the Beam Music Center on the northeast corner of UNLV's campus. Perfect for students with busy schedules, the choir performs repertoire from around the world and much of history: from popular works to classical, folk songs to large choral-orchestral masterworks.
 
Members of the University Chorale can join the UNLV Choral Studies Area's performance tours and travels in the United States and around the world. Members toured Portugal and Spain in 2019 and the Netherlands, Belgium, Luxembourg, and Germany in Summer 2023 and in June 2024 members traveled to New York City to perform at Carnegie Hall.
 
University Chorale is open to all undergraduate and graduate students and UNLV staff. There is no audition required for the ensemble.
 
Students of all experience and ability levels are encouraged to join. We want YOU to keep singing!
summary: UNLV Choral Ensembles membership is open to undergraduate and graduate students, as well as music majors and non-majors. Certain choirs are also open to high school students, community members, and alumni.

name: Panhellenic Council
description:
The purpose of the Panhellenic Council at UNLV is to develop and maintain sorority life and inter-sorority relations at a high level of accomplishment. PHC also promotes superior scholarship, educational programming, civic engagement, and leadership development.  We are currently the largest female-only student organization at the University of Nevada Las Vegas and represent a community of 500+ undergraduate women!

There are 6 National Panhellenic Conference (NPC) sororities that are a part of the Panhellenic at UNLV:

  • Alpha Delta Pi
  • Alpha Gamma Delta
  • Alpha Xi Delta
  • Gamma Phi Beta
  • Sigma Kappa
  • Zeta Tau Alpha

Formal recruitment activities are held at the beginning of each fall semester. Make your college experience one of the most valuable and meaningful times of your life. Learn about joining a sorority and make the choice to be a part of Greek Life today!
summary: The mission of the Panhellenic Council is to develop and maintain sorority life and inter-sorority relations at a high level of achievement while promoting superior scholarship, educational programming, civic engagement and leadership development.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description: Pi Kappa Alpha is dedicated to developing men of integrity, intellect, and high moral character and to fostering a truly lifelong fraternal experience.
summary: Scholars, Leaders, Athletes and Gentlemen

name: Black Student Organization
description:
The Black Student Organization (BSO) at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas is a student-led community dedicated to uplifting, supporting, and empowering Black students on campus. Our mission is to create a welcoming space where students can connect, celebrate culture, and engage in meaningful dialogue around issues that impact the Black community.

Through events, workshops, and collaborations, BSO strives to promote academic excellence, professional development, cultural awareness, and social connection. We serve as both a resource and a family, working to ensure that Black students feel seen, heard, and valued at UNLV.

Whether through mentorship, advocacy, or celebration, BSO is committed to fostering unity and building a legacy of leadership, resilience, and pride.

Ways to Get Involved:

  1. Join on Involvement Center – Click Join to stay in the loop of up.

  2. Come to Meetings & Events – Join us at general body meetings and cultural events to connect with the community.

  3. Stay Connected – Follow us on Instagram @bso_unlv and join our GroupMe.

  4. Take Part – Volunteer at events, join a committee, or even apply for leadership opportunities within BSO.
summary: Our Black Student Organization is a social political organization that aims to support the black diaspora through cultural enrichment, volunteerism and advocacy for students.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law
description:
Phi Alpha Delta International Pre-Law Fraternity (PAD) is the only Pre-Law, co-ed Fraternity on campus. Our mission is to bond prospective law students with the principles of: integrity, compassion, courage, professionalism, and service to pursue a successful law career. We offer a diverse array of events throughout the semester that foster leadership, camaraderie, and academic success to all our members.  Founded in 1902, Phi Alpha Delta now has over 300,000 initiated members throughout the United States. Approximately one out of six attorneys in the United States is a member of PAD, and four of the sitting Justices of the United States Supreme Court are members of P.A.D. 

PAD is open to students of all majors at any point in their undergraduate degree, whether you're committed to attending law school or not. Meetings are open to any student interested!

 

You can stay most up to date with all of our events by following us on Instagram: @unlvphialphadelta. You can also click the linktree link in our Instagram biography which will take you to our groupme and other various social media and information outlets! Finally, as PAD is a pre-professional organization, you are able to join our organization along with other greek life sororities and fraternities!
summary: Phi Alpha Delta (PAD) is the only Pre-Law, Pre-Professional, co-ed Fraternity on campus. Our mission is to bond prospective law students with the principles of integrity, compassion, courage, professionalism, & service to pursue a successful law career.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description: Alpha Phi Omega (APO) is the largest, co-ed fraternity in the nation, and the largest in service. Anyone can join, even those in traditional Greek organizations. Cardinal principles of APO are: Leadership, Friendship, and Service. Alpha Phi Omega UNLV officially chartered on May 1st, 2010. Look out for updates on how to rush this upcoming semester or check out our IG @unlvapo! We're always accepting all UNLV students with a passion for Leadership, Friendship, and Service (male, female, part-time and full-time, all majors and class levels).
summary: Interested in doing community service? Join Alpha Phi Omega! We are the largest national co-ed service fraternity that focuses on promoting leadership, friendship, and service.

name: The Epicurean Society
description:
Mission Statement:

An organization for UNLV students, whose purpose is to use the cuisines of the world brought to Las Vegas as an educational tool and a shared experience that transcends language, history, and culture while networking with industry professionals.

Values:

- Educate the student body about differing culture cuisines and the methods by which they are prepared

- Tour locations across the city of Las Vegas that hold a focus on food and restaurants

- Establish volunteer and job opportunities through organization contacts and resources

- Create professional relationships with fellow students and industry professionals

- Organize and execute fundraisers, sales, and events
summary: An organization focused on food and restaurant management that connects students and faculty in the education of new dishes and recipes, outings to restaurants, suppliers to major Las Vegas properties, and the organization and execution of catered events

name: Graduate & Professional Student Association
description:
The Graduate & Professional Student Association serves all currently enrolled University of Nevada, Las Vegas graduate and professional students. As membership in the GPSA is automatic, all graduate and professional students are encouraged to utilize the GPSA as a resource. 

The GPSA holds monthly council meetings and sponsors an annual Research Forum held in the spring, in an effort to enhance the quality of the educational and social experience for all UNLV graduate and professional students. One of the most important services which the GPSA offers UNLV graduate professional students is the allocation of funds for research and conference travel. These funds support projects which directly affect a student's degree program as well as makes a contribution to their field.
summary: The Graduate Professional Student Association is the recognized governing body serving the currently enrolled and admitted UNLV graduate and professional student population.

name: Phi Delta Theta
description:
Phi Delta Theta was built on three pillars that haven’t budged an inch since the Fraternity was founded by “The Immortal Six” way back in 1848 at Miami University. The pillars are: The cultivation of friendship among its members, the acquirement individually of a high degree of mental culture, and the attainment personally of a high standard of morality. Shortened, we refer to the principles as friendship, sound learning and rectitude.

Helping every individual to meet his true potential is the bedrock of the Phi Delta Theta Fraternity. By celebrating each person’s true self, and by learning from each other’s strengths while helping to improve each other’s weaknesses, every member of Phi Delta Theta develops into a greater version of himself than he could ever on his own. Rather than try to find young men to “mold” into some ideal, we celebrate the uniqueness of each individual and, through encouragement, values, example and brotherhood, empower every brother to exceed his personal expectations.
summary: Phi Delta Theta was built on three pillars that haven’t budged an inch since the Fraternity was founded by “The Immortal Six” way back in 1848 at Miami University. The pillars are: friendship, sound learning and moral rectitude. NV Beta Chapter

name: American Medical Student Association, Pre-medical Chapter
description: Welcome to the AMSA UNLV Involvement Center homepage! We are a national pre-medical/medical organization representing the concerns of physicians-in-training. We represent 67,000 medical students, pre-medical students, interns, residents and practicing physicians from across the country. To get to know us better, please visit our website at amsaunlv.com, where you can find information, applications, a calendar of events, and more. If you want to get in touch, send us an e-mail to amsa.unlv@gmail.com. We look forward to meeting you!
summary: Specialized in helping pre-health students achieve aspirations of attending professional school.

name: Association of Environmental and Engineering Geologists
description: The Association of Environmental and Engineering Geologists (AEG) contributes to its members professional success, and public interest by providing leadership, advocacy, and applied research in environmental and engineering geology. Environmental and engineering geology includes geotechnical consulting for construction sites, hydrogeology projects and more.
summary: Geology, Engineering Geology, Engineering, Environmental Geology

name: UNLV Young Democrats
description: The UNLV Young Democrats represents the voices of any young Democrat in the governmental and political process. We help to exchange all knowledge and information with other young Democrats alike as well as cooperate in working with any other political affiliations, and to do so we shall organize events and activities to promote the interest and well-being of our democratic views in the state of Nevada.
summary: The official Young Democrats/College Democrats organization at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description:
Beta Alpha Psi is an honorary organization for Financial Information students and professionals. The primary objective of Beta Alpha Psi is to encourage and give recognition to scholastic and professional excellence in the business information field. This includes promoting the study and practice of accounting, finance, and information systems; providing opportunities for self-development, service and association among members and practicing professionals, and encouraging a sense of ethical, social, and public responsibility.

Applications for Fall 2025 will open on the first day of the semester. New applicants must have a minimum of 30 completed credit hours, a cumulative GPA of 3.3+, a UDGPA of 3.0+, and a declared major in Accounting, Finance, or Information Systems.
summary: We are an Accounting, Finance, and Information Systems honor society aimed for preparing future professionals and leaders in the world through professional development, service, and leadership.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers at UNLV
description:
Join the Discord server: https://discord.gg/DPADVKV

 

IEEE UNLV is dedicated to the education of members towards the advancement of the theory and practice of electrical, electronics, communications and computer engineering, as well as computer science, the allied branches of engineering and the related arts and sciences; means to these ends include, but are not limited to, the holding of meetings for the purpose of collecting and distributing information to students and faculty about local, regional, and national issues. Also, discussions relating to publications and circulation of works of literature, science and art pertaining to and any other activities necessary, suitable and proper for the fulfillment of these objectives.

IEEE-UNLV is a professional organization, directed toward the advancement of the standing of the members of the professions it serves; means to this end include, but are not limited to, the conduct and publication of surveys and reports on matters of professional concern to the members of such professions, collaboration with public bodies and with other societies for the benefit of the engineering professions as a whole and the general public, and the promotion of ethical conduct.

The IEEE-UNLV shall strive to enhance the quality of the learning experience through and viable means. It shall endeavor to promote understanding of the influence of such technology on the public welfare.
summary: IEEE, electrical, engineers, engineering, electronics, digital, computer, computer science, workshops

name: Honors Student Council
description:
As the largest student organization within the Honors College, the Honors Student Council aims to enrich the collegiate experience of our students by fostering a vibrant and supportive community. 

 

Through hosting a variety of social events and engagement opportunities, HSC helps students build connections, achieve personal growth, and make a meaningful impact on their peers and the wider UNLV community.
summary: To promote camaraderie and cohesiveness among Honors College students. This organization seeks to promote and plan activities within the Honors College and the overall UNLV community.

name: UNLV Rebel Girls & Company
description: The UNLV Rebel Girls & Company is the Dance Team at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. We support the universities primary mission of education, research and public service. UNLV's department of Intercollegiate Athletics measures success by how the program contributes to the overall development of the athlete as a student and as a good citizen.
summary: The UNLV Rebel Girls & Company is the official Dance Team at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description:
Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. is a private, non-profit organization whose purpose is to provide assistance and support through established programs in local communities throughout the world. A sisterhood of more than 200,000 predominantly Black college-educated women.

The Sorority currently has over 900 chapters located in the United States, England, Japan (Tokyo and Okinawa), Germany, the Virgin Islands, Bermuda, the Bahamas, and the Republic of Korea. The major programs of the Sorority are based upon the organization's Five-Point Programmatic Thrust.

  • Economic Development
  • Educational Development
  • International Awareness and Involvement
  • Physical and Mental Health
  • Political Awareness and Involvement
summary: HX, DST, Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.,

name: Phi Alpha Theta, Psi Sigma Chapter
description: We are a professional society whose mission is to promote the study of history through the encouragement of research, good teaching, publication and the exchange of learning and ideas among historians. We seek to bring students, teachers and writers of history together for intellectual and social exchanges, which promote and assist historical research and publication by our members in a variety of ways.http://patpsisigma.wordpress.com/
summary: academic, history, liberal arts

name: Nepalese Students' Association - UNLV
description: It is a non-profit social organization recognizing the Nepalese students currently studying in the University of Nevada Las Vegas (UNLV). This organization symbolizes the culture of Nepal and the group of students from Nepal. The primary objective of this organization is to represent the international community of Nepal at UNLV. It will represent Nepal in any international programs and festivals organized by the UNLV international community. Another objective of this organization is to provide guidance to the new Nepalese students in UNLV. It will help them know the university better and assist them to set up at the initial stage in the international academic environment. This organization will also celebrate the festivals of Nepal and tend to share the culture and festivals with the international community here at UNLV.
summary: Nepalese Students' Association is a non-profit social organization recognizing the Nepalese students currently studying in University of Nevada Las Vegas (UNLV).

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. was founded on December 4, 1906 on the campus of Cornell University in Ithaca, New York. The seven men we refer to as “Jewels”, where the men responsible for the founding of the fraternity. They saw the need for a strong bond of Brotherhood among African descendants in this country. The organization served as a study and support group for minority students who faced racial prejudice, both educationally and socially, at Cornell. The Jewel founders and early leaders of the Fraternity succeeded in laying a firm foundation for Alpha Phi Alpha’s principles of scholarship, fellowship, good character, and the uplifting of humanity. Today Alphas still strive to continue the principles of excellence while transforming into a more inclusive organization open to all races.

Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for our communities.

 

The Sigma Psi chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. here at the University of Nevada Las Vegas was founded in 1993. As our country’s society became more racially-integrated and changed, so has the Fraternity. While originally founded for Black male college students, the fraternity does not discriminate by race, religion or sexual orientation. Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. is an experience incomparable to any other Greek Organization. The legacy, the history, the brotherhood, the leadership, the growth, and everything else this organization offers are unrivaled. Members are proud to have made this Fraternity a life-long commitment. 

"Not all great men are member's of my dear Fraternity, but all Alpha men aspire for greatness; to further excel, to further strive, and to further grow. They remain true to the scope of Alpha, Limitless!"

 

Any male student may be presented as a candidate for membership in a college chapter provided that he:

- Attends an institution where an active chapter is located;

- Has successfully completed one semester or two quarters of a regular year's

  program;

- Is a full-time student taking courses leading to his first academic degree;

- Is in good academic standing with a cumulative grade point average of not 

  less than 2.5 on a 4.0 grade point scale; or equivalent except where the minimum grade required by the

  college for graduation is greater, then the minimum grade for graduation must be used for eligibility;

- Is of good character;

- Is registered to vote, if eligible and qualified; and

- Is sponsored by one (1) active (financial) member.

"I want this Fraternity to standout in the affairs of the Nation."

Jewel Vertner Woodson Tandy
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. is a Black Greek Lettered Organization founded in 190, created to help and support minority college students. Members aspire to excel academically while aiding their communities. Truly a brotherhood unalike any other.

name: Alliance of Non-Traditional Students
description:
The Alliance of Non-Traditional Students aka ANTS, was formed to provide support and fellowship for the NTS from admissions through graduation. Over 30% of undergraduate students at UNLV are categorized as non-traditional (NTS). 

Our goal is a collaborative effort to strengthen campus relationships and bring awareness to the needs of this large, diverse campus demographic.

Enrollment is open to students ONLY.

Are you a Non-Traditional Student?

Do you identify with ANY ONE OR MORE of the following:

  • Over age 24, though age is not a requirement
  • A parent
  • A part-time student
  • Several years between high school and/or college
  • A senior citizen
  • Formerly Incarcerated 
  • Returning to finish your education 
  • Neurodiverse 
  • Evening Student
  • Justice Impacted
  • Married/Domestic partnership
  • Socially impacted 
  • Disabled
  • Veteran
  • Working Full or Part-time 
  • Adult Caregiver
  • Please note that this is not an exhaustive list
summary:
ANTS provides support and fellowship for the NTS (non-traditional student) from admissions through graduation.

Having a sense of belonging is essential for a successful college experience!
 
All are welcomed!

name: UNLV Wrestling Club
description: UNLV Wrestling is a collegiate wrestling team that competes in the National Collegiate Wrestling Association (NCWA) with a full season (duals/tournaments) and post-season. The team is building off the tradition set by the UNLV Wrestling NCAA D-1 teams of the '70s and '80s. UNLV Wrestling is the reigning NCWA West Coast Conference Champions (2019-Present) and placed top-10 in the 2020-Present NCWA National Wrestling Championships (Allen, TX). Be sure to follow us on Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram: @UNLVWrestling. If you're interested in joining the team, please complete our Athlete Interest Form: https://goo.gl/forms/VwpN1sYJBQPtGu0K3
summary: UNLV Wrestling is a collegiate wrestling team that competes in the National Collegiate Wrestling Association (NCWA) with a full season (duals/tournaments) and post-season.

name: American Choral Director's Association UNLV Student Chapter
description:
ACDA (American Choral Directors Association) is a professional organization for music educators with an emphasis on singing, choral conducting, vocal music education, and advocacy.  Our student chapter brings this professional organization to our UNLV campus!

Find out more about the organization at acda.org
summary: Choral conducting, singing, music education.

name: Lambda Alpha: National Collegiate Honor Society for Anthropology
description:
What is Lambda Alpha? 

Lambda Alpha is the National Anthropology Honor Society. The purpose of the society is to encourage and stimulate scholarship and research in Anthropology by recognizing and honoring superior achievement in the discipline among students, faculty and other persons engaged in the study of Anthropology.

Why Should I Join?

Lambda Alpha sponsors many fun and academically uplifting activities.

We host academic ‘Brown Bag’ lectures from UNLV professors, and graduate students which include topics such as applying to graduate schools, finding employment after graduation, CV workshops, scholarship and grant workshops, and much more. 

Lambda Alpha offers an annually awarded local chapter scholarship, funded by membership dues and fundraising. Lambda Alpha also offers a national scholarship providing $5000 to a deserving graduating senior majoring in anthropology and a $1000 annual Dean’s List award to a deserving junior. A Graduate Research Grant is designed to encourage overseas thesis research. The award is variable ranging from $2000 to $6000.

Finally, being a member of Lambda Alpha shows you are interested in academics, and is looked upon favorably when applying to graduate schools.

How Do I Join?

To be considered for Lambda Alpha membership, you must turn in the application form and the attached card, a one-time $25 national membership fee, and a $20 annual local chapter fee. Membership applications are in the anthropology office WRI-B112. Applications and fees can be turned in to the anthropology office. You will be contacted by email when your application has been approved.

Membership applications can be found in the "documents" section of this page. Hard copies can be found in WRI-B112 (the anthropology main office).

The requirements for membership in Lambda Alpha are as an undergraduate student you must have a cumulative GPA of at least 2.5, an Anthropology GPA of at least 3.0, and you must have previously completed or are currently enrolled in 12 hours of anthropology coursework. Graduate students must have a cumulative GPA of at least 3.25. 

For questions contact: lambda.alpha@unlv.edu

Requirements for membership in Lambda Alpha:

Undergraduate Students

1)   You must have a cumulative GPA of at least 2.5

2)   You must have an Anthropology GPA of at least 3.0

3)   You must have previously completed or currently be enrolled in 12 hours of Anthropology coursework

4)   You must be a declared Anthropology major or minor

Graduate Students

1)   You must have a cumulative GPA of at least 3.25. First semester graduate students may use their undergraduate GPA in lieu of this requirement
summary: National Anthropology Honors Society. Undergraduate and graduate students gain opportunities to network within the discipline (including professors!) and hone skills important to academic and professional development.

name: French Club
description:
The UNLV French Club (Le Cercle Français) is dedicated to learning about the French culture and the Francophone world. We also serve as an outlet for the campus community to speak and practice French, regardless of their level of French fluency.

To find out about upcoming events:

Newsletter: frenchclub@unlv.edu

Instagram: unlvfrenchclub

Facebook: UNLV French Club
summary: The UNLV French Club (Le Cercle Français) is dedicated to learning about Francophone culture around the world. We also serve as an outlet for the campus community to speak and practice French, regardless of their level of French fluency.

name: Society of Light-saber Duelists
description: We are the Society of Light-saber Duelists UNLV. We choreograph and perform quality, custom light-saber duels for stage and screen performances.
summary: We are the Society of Light-saber Duelists. We choreograph and perform live lightsaber duels for stage and screen performances.

name: Student Organization Resource Center
description: The SORCE is a room with resources for student organizations, including marketing, printing, and crafting supplies.  The SORCE staff can help you find involvement opportunities that best fit your interests.  The SORCE room is located in SU 305 and operates from Monday - Thursday from 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. during the academic year.
summary: The Student Organization and Resource Center is located in room 305 of the Student Union. We support student organizations in their programs as well as assist students who want to get involved find organizations they may be interested in.

name: UNLV PGA Golf Management Student Association
description: The Purpose of the PGA Golf Management Student Association is to provide a forum for all PGA Golf Management students to openly discuss relevant school, golf and community related issues: to organize and achieve the objectives that the Association feel are important for the PGA/PGM™ concentration so that future internships, golf-related employment, and professional development will be executed in accordance with the established guidelines of the Professional Golfers’ Association of America.
summary: The UNLV PGA Golf Management Student Association consists of all students enrolled in the UNLV PGA Golf Management program as a concentration in the William F. Harrah College of Hotel Administration.

name: Black Law Students Association
description: Greetings from the Black Law Students Association (BLSA) of the William S. Boyd School of Law. Our mission is to provide support, guidance, and a sense of community to our members. Our main function is to support our members in the development of their legal careers and to provide opportunity in all areas. The National Black Law Students Association (NBLSA) is a national organization designed to promote the professional needs and goals of our members. As the largest student run organization in the country, NBLSA is comprised of six regions, representing over 6,000 members across the nation. Together, BLSA and NBLSA seek to cultivate talented, ambitious, and socially conscious lawyers of tomorrow.
summary: BLSA’s mission is to provide support, guidance, and a sense of community to our members. Our main function is to support our members in the development of their legal careers and to provide opportunity in all areas.

name: UNLV Team Handball Club
description: The UNLV Team Handball Club welcomes players of all skill levels to join in the fast-paced, exciting world of team handball. Whether you're a seasoned athlete or new to the sport, our club offers a supportive and inclusive environment where you can learn, compete, and grow. We focus on developing teamwork, strategy, and sportsmanship while building lasting friendships and representing UNLV with pride. Join us to experience the thrill of handball and be part of a dynamic and passionate community!
summary: The UNLV Team Handball Club invites players of all levels to experience the excitement of team handball. We focus on teamwork, skill development, and sportsmanship in a welcoming environment. Join us to compete, learn, and be part of a great community!

name: CARE Advocates
description:
CARE Advocates are student social justice leaders who provide interpersonal violence advocacy, educate the UNLV community through presentations, and create events to spread awareness for the purpose of reducing violence, empowering individuals, and fostering a victim/survivor centered environment through an intersectional feminist lens. 

CARE Advocates are a part of the larger collaboration to end rape culture, victim-blaming and the stigmas around interpersonal violence while providing a safe space for all identities. CARE Advocates understand how all forms of social justice intersect and realize that interpersonal violence is another form of oppression. We stand in solidarity and support all movements that empower marginalized groups knowing this includes our clients as well.

Each Advocate undergoes a comprehensive 45-hour training that prepares them for advocacy, education presentations and activism. Training covers the different barriers clients of all identities may face, on and off campus resources, consent, safety planning, protection orders and how to promote a community free of interpersonal violence. We encourage you to actively participate in CARE events and incorporate training into your everyday life. Being a CARE Advocate is not just a volunteer position but a lifestyle of activism, education and support.

If you'd like to become a certified victim advocate, email us at carecenter@unlv.edu or call us at 702-895-4475 for more information and the application.
summary: CARE Advocates are trained and certified victim advocates dedicated to furthering the UNLV CARE Line, a 24-hour, free, & confidential hotline for victims, survivors, & secondary victims of sexual assault, domestic/dating violence, & stalking.

name: Healthcare Administration Student Association
description:
The purpose of the Healthcare Administration Student Association (HCASA) is to provide students with opportunities to network with industry professionals, engage with faculty and staff, and build meaningful connections within the healthcare community. We support student development by facilitating access to professional conferences, funding certifications for career advancement, and encouraging participation in case competitions that strengthen critical thinking and leadership skills. HCASA fosters collaboration through social networking platforms and empowers future healthcare leaders by bridging the gap between education and professional growth.


Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/UVW4kgzTKK
summary: The purpose of the HCASA is to foster collaboration and understanding between undergraduate and graduate students in the Healthcare Administration degree program and other related programs with professional groups and healthcare agencies in Las Vegas.

name: UNLV Rugby Football Club
description: This is the storied men's rugby club for UNLV. We practice and compete in the DIII Gold Coast Conference. Everyone is welcome. No experience necessary!
summary: We practice and play rugby against local and national men's clubs. Everyone is welcome. We practice on Tuesday/Thursday at 6-8 P.M. at the UNLV Intramural Field.

name: UNLV Student Affiliates in School Psychology
description:
Our philosophy is based upon the idea that the student who is informed of and involved in professional issues today forms the nexus to a strong discipline in the future. The UNLV Student Affiliates in School Psychology (UNLV SASP) is an organization formed under the auspices of the Division 16 Executive Committee of the American Psychological Association (APA). Currently, it is the only student affiliation of its kind within this discipline. 

UNLV SASP aims to provide an integrated communication network for graduate students concerning legislative issues impacting the future of our profession, professional/ethical issues, internship/employment opportunities, shared concerns across a variety of topics, and information across other disciplines of psychology. UNLV SASP's vision is to advance effective practice to improve students' learning, behavior, and mental health outcomes in home, school, and life.

Our purpose is to empower future school psychologists, counselors, and educators by creating an inclusive and unified community to advance the opportunities of pre-service professionals

The University of Nevada, Las Vegas chapter of SASP focuses on providing opportunities for further development for all students admitted into the University of Nevada, Las Vegas School Psychology Program and positively impacting the campus and local Las Vegas community.
summary: UNLV SASP is a community for graduate students affiliated with the discipline of and related to school psychology. We strive to build networks, educate and foster understanding of the field, and develop leaders to strengthen the discipline in the future.

name: Sigma Kappa Theta Eta
description: Sigma Kappa Sorority is a social organization of collegiate and alumnae women committed to promoting the ideals of life-long friendship, intellectual and spiritual fulfillment and service for the greater good.
summary: Sigma Kappa Sorority is a social organization of collegiate and alumnae women committed to promoting the ideals of life-long friendship, intellectual and spiritual fulfillment and service for the greater good.

name: Eta Sigma Delta UNLV Chapter
description:
• Our signature events: Breaking Barriers, ESD Talks, Dine with Alumni, Induction Ceremony, Volunteering. 

  • Founded in 1978 by a group of students at the University of New Hampshire Whittemore School of Business and Economics.
     
  • There are over 90 active Eta Sigma Delta Chapters

  • Eta Sigma Delta includes opportunity for student to distinguish themselves in industry executives eyes.

    An undergraduate student must

      • Have officially declared as a hospitality management major (or related field)
      • Have completed at least 60 credit hours
      • Have a cumulative GPA of at least 3.5

    A Graduate student must

      • Have completed 50% of their credit hours required for their graduate program
      • Have a cumulative GPA of at least 3.5
summary: ESD is an international hospitality honor society that recognizes hospitality students for outstanding academic achievement. ESD partners with industry executives, and helps students build unique connections.

name: Hospitality Financial & Technology Professionals UNLV Chapter
description:
HFTP is a global nonprofit hospitality association that uniquely understands the industry’s problems. We assist our members in finding solutions to industry problems more efficiently than any organization via our expert networks, research, conferences such as HITEC, and certification programs. HFTP is recognized as the spokes group for the finance and technology segment of the hospitality industry.

 

Please be a HFTP member not only with UNLV Chapter, but also as a whole!

HFTP Student Membership Application: https://hftp.wufoo.com/forms/q103zr2x056ro4s/
summary: Students joining HFTP UNLV can benefit from the extraordinary networking opportunities, industry-leading certification programs and events, and essential resources for professional growth offered to all HFTP members.

name: Sri Lankan Students Association
description: SLSA, Sri Lankan Students Association, is a student-based organization dedicated to maintaining our identity, share our culture, and help one another in our community. We pride ourselves in sharing and promoting the understanding of  Sri Lankan culture in the UNLV community as well as banding these individuals together to create a stronger community base. This group is open to all students who share a familiar background, want to further their knowledge on our culture, and would enjoy the opportunity to immerse themselves in a cross-cultural experience.
summary: SLSA, Sri Lankan Students Association, is a student-based organization dedicated to maintaining our identity, share our culture, and help one another in our community

name: UNLV Competitive Mock Trial Team
description: The UNLV Mock Trial Team competes at the undergraduate collegiate level as a part of The American Mock Trial Association (AMTA). Members play attorney and witness roles while arguing criminal and civil cases. We conduct a fabricated trial in which we perform opening statements, direct and cross examinations, and closing arguments. Members develop a foundational understanding of the Federal Rules of Evidence through their use in objections.
summary: The UNLV Criminal Justice Mock Trial Team prepares the next generation of advocates. We argue fabricated criminal and civil AMTA cases on the Prosecution/Plaintiff and Defense sides to compete against other universities from across the nation.

name: UNLVolunteers
description:
UNLVolunteers is a student organization planning service programs to encourage civic engagement and positive social change by providing meaningful service experiences for the UNLV community with partners across the Las Vegas valley.

UNLVolunteers features service project opportunities such as DASH, Service Day, and service projects planned by our UNLVolunteers program assistants in the critical areas environment, health & wellness, food & housing insecurity, youth & education and all other areas not captured by these previously aforementioned 4 impact areas.

  • We are mindful that service work will not change the circumstances of issues our communities experience, but this work is necessary to meet people’s immediate needs, and it opens doors to opportunities for social change work to be done.
summary: UNLVolunteers is a student organization planning service programs to encourage civic engagement and positive social change by providing meaningful service experiences for the UNLV community with partners across the Las Vegas valley.

name: First-Year Experience
description:
Becoming a UNLV student is a huge deal. You’ve already accomplished so much to get here; now, we’re committed to helping you create a college experience that lives up to those accomplishments. Here’s the good news: you get to customize and create an FYE that works for you. Our events and programs are designed to help you connect, explore, grow, thrive, and believe in an FYE that you personalize to meet your goals.

 

Mission: The First-Year Experience (FYE) provides seamless, integrated, and comprehensive support to empower new students’ personal and academic growth. FYE directly impacts student achievement by creating academic and co-curricular pathways for students to establish key relationships, connect with campus resources, and reach their educational goals through graduation and beyond.

 

Vision: All new UNLV students will engage with and personalize a meaningful, high-impact, research-driven First-Year Experience that positively impacts their holistic success.
summary: First Year Experience (FYE) is a collection of people, resources, and programs designed to help you navigate your first year successfully, connect to the people and happenings at UNLV, and set yourself up for a successful transition to UNLV.

name: Fraternity & Sorority Life
description:
Fraternity & Sorority Life at UNLV has 4 governing councils: Las Vegas Fraternity Association (for men), Multicultural Greek Council (both men's and women's organizations), National Panhellenic Council (both men's and women's organizations), and Panhellenic Council (for women). In total, there are 31 social fraternities and sororities at UNLV.

 

Descriptions and information about all chapters can be found on our website.

 

Follow us on Instagram to stay up to date about FSL: Instagram.com/unlvfsl

 

Please see our Documents section for Council Governing Documents and the Fraternity & Sorority Life Handbook.
summary:
A group page for all 37 social fraternities and sororities at UNLV. Follow us on social media: @unlvFSL 
Interfraternity Council, Multicultural Greek Council, National Panhellenic Council, and Panhellenic Council

name: Society of Women Engineers, UNLV Student Chapter
description:
The Society of Women Engineers (SWE) is a not-for-profit educational and service organization that empowers women to succeed and advance in the field of engineering, and to be recognized for their life-changing contributions as engineers and leaders. Founded in 1950, SWE is the driving force that establishes engineering as a highly desirable career for women through an exciting array of training and development programs, networking opportunities, scholarships, outreach and advocacy activities, and much more. 

We hope you'll consider joining SWE for the 2025-2025 academic year. To learn more about our current events, we will be actively updating our Involvement Center Events and News pages with upcoming events, as well as updating our discord. Join the SHPE de UNLV discord here: SWE DISCORD
summary: To empower women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expand the image of the engineering and technology professions as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity and inclusion.

name: Neon Lit Reading Series
description: The NeonLit Reading Series is a monthly event featuring writers from UNLV's MFA and PhD programs. Kathryn Kruse, a short fiction writer and community organizer began the monthly program in 2009. Since the beginning it has drawn standing room only crowds to downtown Las Vegas art galleries, rooftops, and restaurants. The readings reflect the diverse student body and offer students and the general public an opportunity to hear a wide variety of nonfiction, fiction, and poetry.
summary: Neon Lit Reading Series is a monthly event featuring writers from UNLV's graduate Creative Writing programs (MFA and PhD), and invited guests.

name: The National Society of Collegiate Scholars
description: The National Society of Collegiate Scholars (NSCS) is an honors organization that recognizes and elevates high-achieving students. NSCS provides career and graduate school connections, leadership and service opportunities, and gives out more than $1 million annually in scholarships, awards, and chapter funds. If you’d like to join NSCS, you need to have a GPA of 3.0 or above in your first or second year here at UNLV.
summary: The National Society of Collegiate Scholars is an honors organization that recognizes and elevates high-achieving students. Our mission of academic excellence is guided by the values of scholarship, leadership, and service.

name: Chabad at UNLV
description:
Chabad UNLV is the warmest place to be away from home.

It is a home where all are welcome regardless of background or affiliation. A place where every Jew is family.
A Jewish student Group at UNLV that provides educational, social, and recreational programming for students and faculty.

A space where students socialize in a comfortable home-like setting with great friends, superb food and stimulating discussion.
It is inspired by the vision of the Lubavitcher Rebbe of righteous memory.

It is directed by Rabbi Tzvi and Rivky Bronchtain
summary: A home away from home where Jewish students at UNLV can explore their faith in meaningful and relevant ways.

name: Revolution Campus
description:
An international group committed to unity, purity, and putting God first. This group is dedicated to pursuing the dream of spreading God's word and love all over the world.

We are a group of disciples whose sole purpose is to fulfill God's Great Commission (Matthew 28:18-20). We are non-denominational Christians dedicated to making disciples, baptizing disciples, teaching them to obey and preaching the Good News to all nations.
summary: An international group committed to unity, purity, and putting God first. We are committed to winning the spiritual battle against the flesh and living according to the scriptures.

name: Every Nation Campus at UNLV
description:
Every Nation Campus at UNLV is a Christ-centered, socially responsible, church-based college and young adult ministry that exists to empower students to build relationships, grow in faith, and make a difference.

In our weekly meet-up groups, we discuss various issues that impact our lives--such as relationships, racism, injustice, mental health, family, purpose, faith, and many more--and talk about ways in which we can create our own impact on society.

Being a church-based campus ministry, we are a part of the Every Nation global family of churches. As an international movement, we value church planting, campus ministry, and global missions. To this date, we have over 100 student organizations in different colleges and universities across North America and over 700 campus ministries around the world.
summary: In our weekly meet-up groups, we discuss various issues that impact our lives and look at practical ways that we can make a difference.

name: Bhakti-Yoga Club
description:
Mission: Our organization seeks to serve the campus community by inspiring students to learn about and embark on the journey of self-discovery, which is described in the ancient Sanskrit texts such as Bhagavad-gita as Bhakti-Yoga. We are motivated to serve all members of UNLV by providing alternative means to coping with the anxieties and concerns of everyday college life. Our prime motivation is to help individual students and the community at large reach their fullest potential together through education on integrative spiritual practices and a holistic lifestyle. By our service we seek to nourish the hearts of our colleagues, our club members, and the environment we share.

Bhakti-Yoga Club is open to all students from any background. There are no fees or dues for joining our organization. Our activities are meant to introduce students to subjects of consciousness, yoga, meditation, environmental sustainability, self-improvement, natural healing, vegetarianism and spiritual practice. In addition to our weekly yoga/meditation/vegetarian dinner program, every semester our organization hosts guest lecture series, seminars, cooking classes, cultural festivals, as well as dance and music performances - all with the goal of helping students to participate and really take something home with them.

Through our efforts, we hope to enhance the campus diversity and sense of community by creating an open space for all members of UNLV to explore non-traditional philosophies and their practices, together.
summary: The Bhakti-Yoga Club is a diverse conscious collective for anyone interested in the rich spiritual philosophy and lifestyle of Yoga as described in the classic texts of ancient India, such as Bhagavad-gita.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated was founded January 16, 1920 on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. by our five pearls: Arizona Cleaver Stemmons, Pearl Anna Neal, Myrtle Tyler Faithful, Viola Tyler Goings, Fannie Pettie Watts.

The Radiant Rho Pi Chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was chartered in Las Vegas, Nevada on May 28, 1998. After years not on UNLV’s campus, the chapter was reactivated on March 12, 2013. Like other Zeta Phi Beta chapters, Rho Pi strives to model after the principles of scholarship, service, sisterhood, and finer womanhood.
summary: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated is a community-conscious, action-oriented organization. Its focus is on scholarship, service, sisterhood, and finer womanhood. The sorority implements its mission through the following key organizational initiatives.

name: UNLV Cheer
description: The UNLV Cheer team represents the UNLV Rebels both on and off the field. We serve as public relation ambassadors of intercollegiate athletics and support the University. We create an atmosphere that enhances education through: study hall, advising priority registration, and grade reports. Our tryouts are held in May of every school year, members of the team must be a full time student at UNLV with a minumum of a 2.0 GPA.
summary: The official cheer team of UNLV that provides exciting and challenging entertainment and support at all UNLV Athletic events.

name: UNLV Men's Volleyball
description:
This program will provide male volleyball players the opportunity to practice and compete with other institutions in a competitive setting.  UNLV Men's volleyball club will register with the National Collegiate Volleyball Federation and compete in the Mountain West Conference along with other prestigious divisions.

 

SUPPORT US BY FOLLOWING THE UNLV MENS VOLLEYBALL INSTAGRAM @UNLVMVB. Feel free to DM with any questions!
summary: A competitive all male travel club volleyball team that competes both in the Mountain West division and at the National level.

name: Public Health Student Association
description: The UNLV Public Health Student Association (PHSA) offers a way for public health students to engage on campus and in the surrounding community. The PHSA connects undergraduate and graduate public health students with the common goal of promoting scholarship, leadership, and service. Each semester, PHSA activities include group meetings, volunteer projects, and other events on campus with the UNLV School of Public Health (SPH). Through these activities, PHSA members make important connections on campus and in the community while also engaging with professionals in the public and private sectors to learn more about the field of public health. Members of the PHSA also work together to evaluate their respective degree programs and provide feedback to the SPH when necessary.
summary: The UNLV Public Health Student Association seeks to promote scholarship, leadership and service among undergraduate and graduate students studying public health in the UNLV School of Public Health.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America, University of Nevada Las Vegas Chapter
description: The Public Relations Student Society of America is the premier organization for students interested in public relations and communications. We seek to advance the public relations profession by strengthening generations of future professionals. We advocate rigorous academic standards for public relations education, the highest ethical principles and diversity in the profession.
summary: The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is the foremost organization for students interested in public relations and communications.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The Society of Physics Students (SPS) at UNLV is dedicated to uniting people who share a passion for physics and its research. Our mission is to cultivate a community of like-minded individuals to discuss exciting topics and developments in the field while also offering valuable resources and mentorship to those pursuing careers or are simply interested in physics.
summary: The UNLV chapter of SPS is part of the national organization dedicated to supporting any college student with a love of physics.  Membership gives access to scholarships, professional networking, and a community of peers excited about physics!

name: MBA Student Association
description: Today’s leaders must be effective decision makers with top-notch business skills and acumen. The UNLV MBA Student Association is committed to unite MBA students for the purposes of networking, philanthropic outreach, and academic competition. The mission of the UNLV MBA Student Association is to promote the UNLV MBA Program and to assist in its development.
summary: The goal of the MBA Student Association is to augment the MBA curriculum by providing MBA students with a focused graduate experience in business that enhances their leadership and professional capabilities.

name: Global Entrepreneurship Experience
description: The Global Entrepreneurship Experience is a program in the UNLV Lee Business School created to help incoming freshman study entrepreneurship on a global scale. Through an immersive learning experience, GEE students gain experience in crafting unique business models to bring new products and services to world markets. The GEE program stimulates creativity and innovation, emphasizes strategic thinking, and promotes leadership development and team-building. The program gives students from any major the tools to become entrepreneurial in their chosen profession. You must be accepted into the program and be taking GEE classes in order to be in the minor.
summary: The Global Entrepreneurship Experience minor features a hands-on entrepreneurship curriculum and is open to students pursuing any major.

name: Kinesiology Club of UNLV
description:
The Kinesiology club of UNLV is for undergrad students that wish to get involved within their Kinesiology major or have an interest in athletics & health. We provide a forum of extracurricular activities such as networking seminars, leadership opportunities, physical activity events, continuing education and social events. We promote a healthy lifestyle and academic values to carry into future careers. 

______________________

Click here to view all our links!

Follow us on social media!

Twitter: @KappaIotaNu

Instagram: @unlv_kin

Facebook: facebook.com/UNLVKappaIotaNU
summary: We focus on leadership opportunities, networking, and community involvement within our field of kinesiology. Our goal is to bring together students and form a fun environment, a welcoming atmosphere, and a productive, professional network for the future.

name: Flying Rebels
description:
The Flying Rebels Booster Club is a student organization run by Air Force ROTC cadets and UNLV students.

AFROTC focuses on teaching you the necessary leadership skills to become an Air Force officer through a vigorous and multifaceted training program.

As a cadet, you will be required to attend academic and leadership courses geared towards AFROTC cadets. At the end of this four year program, cadets will receive a minor in Aerospace Studies. These courses include:

  • A weekly academic aerospace studies (AES) class where you will learn more about the Air Force and how to become a more effective leader.
  • A weekly leadership laboratory (LLAB) where cadets will learn about Air Force leadership while being given the chance to improve upon various military-specific skills.

On-top of academic requirements, cadets will also be required to attend a minimum of two physical training sessions each week.

Air Force ROTC also provides cadets with other opportunities such as:

  • Scholarships
  • Summer programs
  • Base visits
  • Field training
  • Sports competitions
  • Career days
summary: Mission with the purpose of bolstering morale, increasing quality of life, and strengthening community partnerships.

name: 1st Generation Club
description:
The 1st Generation Club (1GC) aims to cultivate a space where first-gen students can connect, share experiences, and gain resources to achieve their academic, professional, and personal goals. 

 

A first-generation college student is someone whose parent(s) did not complete a 4-year college or university degree, regardless of other family member's level of education, and who is entering college with limited knowledge of the traditions and patterns of college life & academia. You are still considered a first-gen student if your sibling went to college before you, your parents went to college in another country, your parents started college but didn't finish, and more! Regardless, we welcome everyone to our meetings.

 

This organization is led by fellow first-gen peers, and we are open to all UNLV undergraduate and graduate students.

 

Our upcoming 1GC meetings and events for Fall 2025 will be updated through our Involvement Center Calendar, our Instagram, and our website. 

 

HOW TO JOIN 1GC

 

- Click "Join" at the top of our Involvement Center page (this page)

 

Stay connected with us

 

For the latest updates, follow our Instagram HERE.

Join our Discord HERE to connect with the members of our community.
summary: An alliance of students navigating the university system for the first time, dedicated to serving UNLV's population of first-generation, transfer, non-traditional, and students with children by offering academic, professional, and social support.

name: Collegiate Association for the Research of Principles
description:
Our Vision

An inspired youth movement progressing society to become a global family united upon Universal Principles.

 

Our Mission

To empower young adults to become global leaders who will contribute towards building a unified world centered upon God’s love, by providing the education and training based upon our core values.

 

Core Principles

  1. God - Renew our spirituality through seeking truth and relating with God as our Parent

  2. Family - Realize one family under God through practicing integrity and true love

  3. Peace - Raise true leaders who create a peaceful environment and united world
summary: CARP Las Vegas is a youth movement that empowers young adults to become global leaders who will contribute towards building a unified world centered upon God's love, by providing education and training based upon our core values.

name: National Communication Association Student Club
description:
The NCASC helps students enhance their leadership skills, seek career opportunities, as well as network and build interpersonal relationships.

We encourage participation from all group members, so that all voices are heard. Through each meeting, we brainstorm about upcoming projects together.

Whether you're an arts major, a math major, a history major, or perhaps undecided, the NCASC is open to all UNLV students. We encourage diversity! This way, members can learn from one another, through similarities and through differences.

Our projects are both educational and exciting. We hold social functions as well as volunteering with other organizations, clubs, etc, or anything campus or non-campus related. Some of our recent projects held were teaming up with Make-A-Wish Foundation and  Opportunity Village.

Whatever events you want to plan or goals you want to accomplish, this club gives you the perfect opportunity for self growth and leadership development. The NCASC would love to have you on our team!

Remember, the NCASC is what you make it!
summary: The National Communication Association Student Club promotes the study, teaching, and application of the principles of communication through educational and social functions that are relevant to the membership of the club.

name: UNLV Economics Club
description:
Mission Statement:

  • The purpose of UNLV Economics Club is to further educate its members and form relationships with its current members, alumni, and local businesses. We are open to all majors regardless of their level of interest in economics.

Why should you join?

  • Interact with students who have similar interests
  • Networking opportunities with alumni and local businesses
  • Great preparation for students for life after college
  • Leadership opportunities
  • Career building workshops
  • Refreshments…

Club Format

The Club will meet two times per month (given availability) and will consist of either of the formats:

  • Mixer event where members can network and gain connections that may help them in their university career and even external careers.
  • Guest speaker where members will participate in a lecture structured meeting with a guest speaker to explore insights that cannot always be found in a traditional classroom setting. The aim of the guest speaker is also to provide members with potential career opportunities and general development.

 

If you have any questions or would like more information please contact:

 

econclub@unlv.edu

 

Lauren Tomlinson - Club President

tomlil2@unlv.nevada.edu

 

Eric Chiang - Advisor

eric.chiang@unlv.edu

 

You can also chat with us directly through our Discord Server!
summary: A club for people interested in learning more about economics from professionals, experts, and each other. While exploring topics in economics, and economics' intersection with other subjects like politics, public policy, law, and alike.

name: First Love @ UNLV
description:
You Belong in First Love!

First Love UNLV is the BEST place to be!

We're a campus ministry that strives to help young people to know God and work for Him. As part of First Love Church USA and First Love Church Las Vegas, we encourage everyone to use their talents to glorify God!

What are you waiting for? Join First Love today!

Text Mark (805) 832-1285, Camille at (702)-742-0967, Chinonye at (781) 558-6648, or message us directly through the Involvement Center if you want to join us or have any questions.
summary:
A home for young people, by young people, and FOR young people!
Full of FIRST LOVE for the Lord.

name: Future Latinos in Medicine
description:
Future Latinos in Medicine is a pre-health organization committed to providing community outreach to serve the Latinx community of Las Vegas. We provide support for underrepresented Latinx undergraduates who are interested in pursuing careers in the medical field.

We have spoken to 300+ middle/high school students about the importance of higher education, and science-related careers, and shared our experiences as first-generation college students.

At the core of our mission is the importance of compassion, teamwork, responsibility, and a sense of community. Our objectives are the following:

 

  1. To raise awareness amongst first-generation students about various careers in medicine.
  2. To serve our underrepresented community in the medical field through direct involvement and outreach services. 
  3. To increase the number of underrepresented healthcare professionals in Southern Nevada. 

Visit our website here! https://futurelatinosinmed.wixsite.com/flmunlv

There is an interest member form on our website if you'd like to be contacted by our members or sign up for our organization. We encourage all students to come and meet us to find out more about our organization. Thank you!
summary: Future Latinos in Medicine is a pre-health organization committed to providing community outreach to serve the Latinx community of Southern Nevada. FLM provides support to multicultural and underrepresented undergraduates who aspire to be in medicine.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description:
This organization shall further the missions established by the American Institute of Aeronautics and
Astronautics through the following:

1) Raising awareness of and fostering interest in the aerospace industry among the student body
2) Promote local, regional, national, and international AIAA programs and competitions
3) Facilitate the implementation of aerospace-related projects and research at UNLV
summary: AIAA is the best place on campus to learn about aerospace! Whether you are mildly interested in aerospace or know the altitude a U-2 spy plane can reach, AIAA is the place for you! We host a variety of projects focused on applying engineering principles.

name: UNLV Model United Nations
description: Model United Nations (MUN) at UNLV offers a platform for civic and social engagement in the Las Vegas area and facilitates productive debate of international affairs and politics. Our members discuss matters of international diplomacy and development, building vital leadership and advocacy skills that aid in their personal and professional development. We simulate meetings of the United Nations at our meetings and participate in debate-style conferences around the country, competing with other university teams. We also hold social events and participate in community service around Southern Nevada. By providing a space for members to practice writing, public speaking, and team working in the face of real-world situations and relevant challenges, we hope to cultivate the leaders, advocates, and diplomats of tomorrow!
summary: Model United Nations (MUN) is a club dedicated to public speaking, discussion, and debate about international affairs and geopolitics.

name: UNLV Ethics Bowl Club
description:
Mission: The UNLV Ethics Bowl team aims to advocate the advancement of philosophy through academic discourse. Specifically, by participating in the Intercollegiate Ethics Bowl Competition, teams form reasoned answers to ethical cases. Upon joining, students have the chance to perfect their critical thinking, collaboration, intellectual improvisation, and understanding key philosophical questions.
