What is Ethics Bowl? Ethics Bowl is a collaborative yet competitive event in which student teams examine, analyze, and discuss a series of wide-ranging timely, and timeless ethical dilemmas such as personal ethics, business ethics, medical ethics, and much more.
summary: UNLV Ethics Bowl aims to advance scholarship by providing students the opportunity to participate in exciting and thought-provoking Ethics Bowl competitions.

name: American Dental Education Association
description: The American Dental Education Association (ADEA) is The Voice of Dental Education. Its members include all 76 U.S. and Canadian dental schools, over 800 allied and advanced dental education programs, 66 corporations and more than 20,000 individuals.
summary: The American Dental Education Association (ADEA) is The Voice of Dental Education. Its members include all 76 U.S. and Canadian dental schools, over 800 allied and advanced dental education programs, 66 corporations and more than 20,000 individuals.

name: Academy of LDS Dentists
description: Professional Development and Continuing Education Service Fellowship Among Professionals
summary: Professional Development and Continuing Education Service Fellowship Among Professionals

name: UNLV Women's Rugby Football Club
description:
The UNLV Women's Rugby Club is here! In the 2025-2026 school year, we are growing our team and working towards getting a dedicated group of women together to play sports and be part of a great team environment. If you want to join our sisterhood, contact us at the emails below or on Instagram!

No experience necessary! We cannot wait to see you out on the field!
summary: We are a competitive women's rugby team dedicated to spreading the joy of rugby, as well as women empowerment. We are aiming to create a life long sisterhood through the love of the game and the bonding of our teams.

name: SGT Clint Ferrin Memorial Clinic
description: Organization created to raise awareness and provide dental treatment for U.S. military veterans.
summary: Organization created to raise awareness and provide dental treatment for U.S. military veterans.

name: American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry
description: The American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry (AAPD) is the membership organization representing the specialty of pediatric dentistry. Our 9,500 members work in private offices, clinics and hospital settings and serve as primary care providers for millions
summary: The American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry (AAPD) is the membership organization representing the specialty of pediatric dentistry. Our 9,500 members work in private offices, clinics and hospital settings and serve as primary care providers for millions

name: Film@UNLV
description: Film@UNLV is a co-ed group designed to complement undergraduate student's school curriculum with film festival travel, hands-on experience, direct access to industry professionals and networking opportunities with other film-focused students from leading universities all over the world. Students participate with industry thought leaders in roundtable and panel discussions on all aspects of the film business.
summary: Film@UNLV is a co-ed group designed to complement undergraduate student's school curriculum with film festival travel, hands-on experience, direct access to the industry and networking opportunities with other film-focused students from around the world.

name: Gamma Phi Beta
description:
Gamma Phi Beta Sorority, one of the 10 oldest women's organizations in North America and the first to be called a "sorority," was founded November 11, 1874, at Syracuse University in New York. 

The Sorority has chartered more than 180 collegiate chapters across the United States and Canada, and has more than 200,000 collegiate and alumnae members worldwide. The Sorority includes 138 active collegiate chapters and 160 active alumnae chapters.

The Eta Rho chapter of Gamma Phi Beta was established here at UNLV in the spring of 2016. We are the 185th established chapter of Gamma Phi Beta.
summary: Gamma Phi Beta is an international sorority dedicated to building confident women of character who celebrate sisterhood and make a difference in the world around them.

name: UNLV UndocuNetwork
description:
The UNLV UndocuNetwork is a network of allies, undocumented students, staff, and peers whose mission is to empower, inspire, advocate and create thriving spaces for undocumented students, TPS recipients, refugee students, UndocuQueer students, Afroimmigrants, AIP immigrants and any other immigrant students at UNLV! We aim to become as inclusive and intersectional so our undocumented community can thrive in Higher Education.

To stay updated with upcoming events and meetings, please visit instagram.com/undocunetwork/ or email UndocuNetwork@gmail.com.

#HeretoStay #UndocuJoy #DreamActNow! #ProtectTPS #UndocuScholar
summary: The UNLV UndocuNetwork is a network of allies whose mission is to support and empower undocumented students at UNLV!

name: Latino Pre-Medical Student Association
description:
LPMSA is the undergraduate counterpart to the Latino Medical Student Association (LMSA) in UNLVSOM and UNRSOM, a national organization designed for minority medical students. We are dedicated to serving the Latino community of Las Vegas with the aim of increasing the representation of Latinos in healthcare, as there is currently a severe shortage of Latinos in medicine.

Through educational outreach, LPMSA strives to encourage minorities in high school to go to college and pursue a career in healthcare, while providing a support network for pre-medical undergraduate students. We work to support undergraduates by establishing professional relationships and networking opportunities with medical students in LMSA, as well as local physicians.
summary: LPMSA is a pre-professional organization structured to provide support and opportunities for Latino students. The organization also strives to improve healthcare for underserved, minority populations.

name: Turning Point USA
description: Turning Point USA is a student movement advocating the importance of free markets and small government. Since its inception we are now at over 1,000 college campuses nation wide, and will continue to move forward with our movement. Find more information on TurningPointUSA.Net
summary: Turning Point USA (TPUSA) is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization founded on June 5, 2012 by Charlie Kirk. The organization’s mission is to identify, educate, train, and organize students to promote the principles of free markets and small government.

name: Las Vegas Jewish Experience
description: The Las Vegas Jewish Experience (LVJE) is passionate about providing Las Vegas’s college students with opportunities to experience Judaism and connect with their Jewish roots.
summary: The Las Vegas Jewish Experience (LVJE) is passionate about providing Las Vegas’s college students with opportunities to experience Judaism and connect with their Jewish roots.

name: American Society of Interior Designers UNLV Student Chapter
description:
American Society of Interior Designers is a student-run organization dedicated to connecting, mentoring and preparing students to enter the professional world.

 

Providing access to lecture series, events, and competitions and helping students network for opportunities.
summary: American Society of Interior Designers is a student-run organization dedicated to connecting, mentoring and preparing students to enter the professional world.

name: Epsilon Alpha Sigma
description: With its strict philanthropic focus, EAΣ has made an impact both on a local and global scale. As a cultural organization, EAΣ expresses no religious or political affiliation but rather focuses on the humanitarian aspect of the conflicts plaguing our Arab countries today. Through this focus, we aim to expose our peers to the diversity that exists within the Arab world and the impact Empowered Arab Women make both within our immediate communities and all around the world. With chapters blossoming all over the nation, we look forward to spreading our sisterhood and thus increasing our philanthropic impact. The purpose of this sorority shall be to unite it's members in a sisterhood based upon the bonds of sincere and lasting friendship, to stimulate one another in the pursuit of knowledge and success, to promote the moral and social culture of it's members, and to develop plans for guidance and unity in action. Most of all, this sorority seeks to promote respect and loyalty to ones heritage and to remind its members to never forget the culture of our ancestors.
summary: Striving to bring an Arab presence to Greek life, this organization is the first Arab sorority in the nation.

name: Rebel Roots
description:
Love To Garden Or Want To Learn How? Join gardening club!

  • Help care for garden beds, grow your own victory gardens
  • Learn tips and tricks from master gardeners of Nevada
  • Volunteer at garden parties, cookouts, and movie screenings under the stars!
  • Get event emails with membership request!

Rebel Roots Garden Club has 5 plots at the UNLV Community Garden, and is managed separately from the rest of the community garden. 

To learn more about The UNLV Campus Community Garden, click this link to be taken to their departmental page.

Join the club's Discord!
summary: Gardening helps create a sense of community, learning, and sustainability among students, faculty, and staff through the common goal of producing 100 percent organic, natural harvests.

name: The UNLV Sociology Society
description:
Sociology is the search for an understanding of all facets of the social world in which we live, including but not limited to structure and organization, behavior and action, meaning and value, and knowledge and power.

 

The purpose of this organization is to promote leadership, growth, understanding, knowledge, and strong fellowship in the study of sociology through a myriad of activities. By doing so, we can better serve the UNLV campus community, the greater Las Vegas area, and the world around us. Sociology helps us see how our own individual experiences connect to a much larger socio-historical picture, and in the process helps us build a much more nuanced perspective of the world while instilling the values of community and understanding.

 

The Sociology Society hosts activities that include but are not limited to: social events, game nights, sociology skills workshops, field trips, volunteer opportunities, guest speakers, and group discussions. We hope to cultivate an enthusiasm and energy through our society and its activities that inspires students and community members to learn, lead, and serve in a diverse and changing world.

 

If you are interested in seeing what we are all about, please send us an email and request to be a member of the Involvement Center. All upcoming events will be posted on our Instagram, our Discord, as well as the Involvement Center (RSVP if you can!)
summary: We aim to promote leadership, intellectually stimulating conversations, and strong fellowship in the study of sociology through a myriad of activities such as social events, field trips, volunteer opportunities, guest speakers, and group discussions.

name: Rebel Venture Fund
description:
The RVF is UNLV’s venture capital (VC) fund that makes equity investments in private high-growth companies throughout Las Vegas and Southern Nevada. The fund invests in startups at every stage and provides knowledge, resources, and support to the growing tech and small business community here in Nevada. Unlike other university VC funds, the RVF is entirely student lead and run. Students work closely with entrepreneurs and leaders in the business community to conduct screenings, due diligence, investments, negotiations, and close deals.

Founded in 2013, utilizing a founding gift from Jeff Moskow of $500,000, the Rebel Venture Fund (RVF) provides experiential learning opportunities for UNLV students through participation in real world venture capital funding.

RVF draws its membership from graduate and undergraduate students who work closely with the RVF management board, which consists of local venture capitalists, angel investors, attorneys, entrepreneurs and business executives, who help to oversee the investment process. In addition to having strong ties and relationships with business leaders, investors, and professionals in the community, RVF is supported by the UNLV Center for Entrepreneurship.

Las Vegas has undergone lots of change in recent years, especially in the entrepreneurship, technology and small business communities. RVF’s goal is to support this growing community, help better connect the many resources in Nevada, and help it flourish and become a tech and entrepreneurship hub.
summary:
The Rebel Venture Fund is UNLV's very own student-led venture capital investment group that
invests capital in startups and early stage high-growth companies.

name: The Scientista Foundation Chapter at UNLV
description:
UNLV Scientista is on a mission to empower women majoring in science, technology, engineering, mathematics, and medicine (STEMM) by providing a strong campus community, online resources, and visible role models.

In order to achieve these goals, UNLV Scientista will:

  • Organize socials and study groups
  • Connect people with similar goals and interests
  • Provide networking and job opportunities 
  • Recognize female achievements in STEM through Scientista Spotlights
  • Facilitate outreach to inspire and encourage young people in the community
  • Provide information on graduate and professional schools

This organization is a chapter of the National Scientista Foundation.
summary: The UNLV Scientista Foundation is a part of a national organization that empowers pre-professional women in science, technology, engineering, math and medicine (STEMM) through content, communities, and conferences.

name: OMS Pre-Doctoral Society
description: This is an official student organization located at the UNLV School of Dental Medicine. We hope to inspire, educate, and assist pre-doctoral dental students to matriculate into the specialty of Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery.
summary: UNLV School of Dental Medicine chapter of the American Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons.  Dedicated to inspiring and education the next generation of Oral & Maxillofacial Surgeons.

name: Engelstad Scholars
description:
The Engelstad Program was established by the Engelstad Family Foundation through an endowment of $12.625 million to the UNLV. The gift is the largest active scholarship in the university’s history and the largest active scholarship endowment in the history of the Nevada System of Higher Education.

Scholars admitted to the program are required to complete 100 hours of community service annually through a one of our partnering nonprofit organizations. The scholarship awards $2,500 per semester for up to eight consecutive fall and spring semesters to students who maintain their eligibility.
summary: The Engelstad Scholars Program is a service-based and leadership-focused scholarship program for high-achieving, low-income graduates of Clark County High Schools.  Membership is solely open to current scholars in the program.

name: Academy of General Dentistry, UNLV Chapter
description: The Academy of General Dentistry (AGD) is a professional association whose main purpose is to promote lifelong learning and advocacy for dental professionals. The UNLV Chapter was formed to afford dental students the opportunity to learn beyond the classroom and to become involved with the second largest dental association in the United States.
summary: UNLV AGD is an organization dedicated to promote education, advocacy, and opportunities to help dental students advance their careers beyond the classroom.

name: Habesha Student Association
description:
Habesha Student Association is a multicultural organization that aspires to learn about and appreciate the rich cultures of two East African countries, Ethiopia and Eritrea. Though many have heard of these countries, many people do not know that within Ethiopia and Eritrea, there are a number of different regions, each with their own unique languages, foods, customs, etc. By encouraging all individuals of all regions within Ethiopia and Eritrea, as well as anyone else interested, to join we are able to learn from one another about these different cultures. 

HSA’s primary goal is to place these cultures on display, thereby helping Habesha students become immersed and proud of their roots, while at the same time educating the public.

HSA wants to create an environment where students can unite through their differences. It is when we unite that we can make a difference and aid our community. By teaming up with local Ethiopian and Eritrean organizations, we want to provide meaningful and impactful service to the community.  Moreover, students can make lasting friendships with other students who share similar cultures and experiences.
summary: Habesha Student Association is an organization that aspires to learn about and appreciate the rich cultures of Ethiopia and Eritrea. By encouraging all individuals to join, we are able to learn from one another about these different cultures.

name: Themed Entertainment Association (TEA)
description:
The UNLV chapter of Themed Entertainment Association (TEA@UNLV) is a student-driven organization dedicated to fostering career exploration within the entertainment technology field. By leveraging the collective experience and knowledge of its members, TEA@UNLV equips students with the necessary skills to pursue their passions in this dynamic industry. The organization achieves this through workshops, hands-on projects, and, most notably this year, an immersive experience project that welcomes student participation and contribution. Anyone from any major with any experience is welcome! 

This semester, our team will be participating in the 2025 Ride Engineering Competition (2025 REC)! Feel free to join and pitch in any help with making a functional flat-ride model!
summary: We are a university chapter from the main platform, Themed Entertainment Association (TEA), fostering education about the themed entertainment and live entertainment industries. We offer a platform for learning more about the entertainment industry.

name: Silver Wings
description: Silver Wings is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense. It is made up largely of civilians interested in personal development, leadership, service, and support of our military personnel. Enlisting in the military is not required, nor the focus of the organization. There are immense opportunities for scholarships, networking, and professional development for all majors.
summary: National, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service. Immense opportunities for scholarships, networking, and professional development available.

name: Rebel Improv
description: Rebel Improv is an organization that is dedicated to teaching students the art of improvisation. What's improv? Improv is a form of live theatre made up on the spot, without a script. We provide a safe environment where students can create, build confidence, and gain teamwork skills. Our schedule consists of practices, team building events, and performances both on and off campus.
summary: We are a group of students who learn, perform, and build communities all through the means of both short and long form improvisational theater. Welcome to any experience, skill level, or even audience members!

name: Psychological & Brain Sciences Student Committee
description: The Psychological & Brain Sciences Student Committee (PBSSC) is led by current graduate students in experimental psychology at UNLV. The main goals of the PBSSC are to promote social cohesion and well-being, advocate for graduate students in experimental psychology, and serve as a liaison between its students, faculty, and department.
summary: The main goals of the Psychological & Brain Sciences Student Committee (PBSSC) are to promote social cohesion, advocate for graduate students in experimental psychology, and serve as a liaison between its students, faculty, and department.

name: UNLV Real Estate & Business Society
description:
The UNLV Real Estate & Business Society (REBS) provides students with networking and career opportunities through our mentorship, internship & scholarship programs. We offer various real estate-centered activities such as site visits, urban development, city planning, and more. REBS is supported by some of the most prolific industry professionals in our community and networking allows students to build sustainable partnerships that will be mutually beneficial long term.

Monthly fun & informative events are held to engage students with the real estate industry practitioners. We strongly believe in giving back to our community through fundraisers and volunteer work. 

We are proud to be sponsored by Ovation Development!
summary: A student-led organization offering personal development experiences to the next generation of the real estate community.

name: Sigma Delta Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description: The brothers of Sigma Delta Alpha (ΣΔΑ) are university men who embody the spirit of brotherhood to assist each other in achieving their academic goals. We are a proud fraternity that acknowledges a shared cultural heritage. We represent our people in the world of academia and we are determined to be positive role models to the youth in our communities.
summary: Sigma Delta Alpha is a fraternity that promotes the Latino culture, along with working with the community as we strive to be positive examples for the youth in the Las Vegas community.

name: UNLV Rebel Judo
description: Judo is an electrifying blend of combat sport and martial art, where raw power meets strategic brilliance. It’s a test of both body and mind, demanding agility, strength, and razor-sharp focus. On your feet, it’s about mastering techniques to sweep opponents off balance, lift them sky-high, and send them crashing to the mat with precision and force. On the ground, it’s a chess match of dominance, using pins, chokes, and joint locks to outmaneuver and submit your opponent. Judo isn’t just a fight—it’s a dynamic dance of skill, resilience, and unrelenting determination.
summary: The Judo Club teaches a martial art focused on powerful throws, takedowns, and ground control. Perfect for self-defense, building confidence, or pushing limits, Judo blends skill and strategy to help you grow physically and mentally. Join us today!

name: Howard R. Hughes College of Engineering Student Ambassadors
description: UNLV Howard R. Hughes College of Engineering Student Ambassadors (ESA) is an RSO managed by the Engineering Undergraduate Recruitment Coordinator (RC) or similar UNLV College of Engineering (COE) faculty representatives. The ESA program is an academic and pre-professional club focused on service work on and off campus. The program conducts presentations and tours, college fairs, and events on campus (Including, but not limited to, Rebel Preview and Rebel Ready Week) for all students. The ESA program represents the COE and works with the Admissions and Recruitment Offices to promote Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM) and its career opportunities and majors as high school students and transfers look at UNLV.
summary: We are a volunteer student organization of engineering students serving the Howard R. Hughes College of Engineering. We lead tours & presentations, assist prospective students, and promote STEM awareness through early outreach and community engagement.

name: Organization of Women Law Students
description: The Organization for Women Law Students (OWLS) is for any law student dedicated to supporting women in the legal profession. Our goals are to help our members succeed in law school and to provide opportunities to expand professional connections to help our members find a job after graduation. We seek to enhance and elevate professional networking through opportunities to meet faculty and successful women in the legal community.
summary: Dedicated to Empower Women in the Law

name: UNLV School of Medicine Latino Medical Student Association
description: LMSA mission is to recruit and support individuals in the healthcare profession who will strive to improve the access to medical care of Latinos and underserved populations.
summary: LMSA mission is to recruit and support individuals in the healthcare profession who will strive to improve the access to medical care of Latinos and underserved populations.

name: Student National Medical Association
description:
Student National Medical Association (SNMA) is committed to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students, addressing the needs of underserved communities, and increasing the number of clinically excellent, culturally competent and socially conscious physicians.

SNMA chapters based at allopathic and osteopathic medical schools throughout the nation, and some colleges, implement our programs and activities locally. SNMA programs are designed to serve the health needs of underserved communities and communities of color. In addition, SNMA is dedicated both to ensuring that medical education and services are culturally sensitive to the needs of diverse populations and to increasing the number of African-American, Latino, and other students of color entering and completing medical school.
summary: Student National Medical Association (SNMA) is committed to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students [and] addressing the needs of underserved communities.

name: Asian Pacific American Medical Students Association
description:
- To address the unique health challenges of Asian and Pacific Islander American (APIA) communities.
- Foster an inclusive environment that promotes cultural exchange and learning
- Organize social and networking events where medical students can relax and get connected with members in our local community
- Facilitate opportunities to learn how to care for API patients in a culturally sensitive manner
summary: APAMSA is a national organization of medical and pre-medical students committed to addressing the unique health challenges of Asian and Pacific Islander American communities.

name: University of Nevada, Las Vegas School of Medicine Branch of the American Medical Women’s Associations, Inc.
shortName: AMWA, UNLV School of Medicine Chapter
description: The American Medical Women’s Association is an organization which functions at the local, national, and international level to advance women in medicine and improve women’s health. We achieve this by providing and developing leadership, advocacy, education, expertise, mentoring, and strategic alliances. AMWA brings together those in medicine and health-related fields to promote women’s health and to support members’ professional and personal development.
summary: The American Medical Women’s Association empowers women to lead in improving health for all within a model that reflects the unique perspective of women.

name: Rebel D&D
description: The Dungeons and Dragons Club at UNLV is a club to meet new people and practice skills related to creative writing, statistics, and theater/drama through the world-renowned table-top role-playing game Dungeons and Dragons and other various table-top role-playing games. This organization will bring together people from many different backgrounds to play and learn Dungeons and Dragons and more. Join our Discord for more information!
summary: We promote an environment of creativity and self-discovery while playing the titan of role-playing games: Dungeons & Dragons. We have plenty of tabletop games that we run weekly! Join our Discord at https://discord.gg/azfrz5ggmp

name: Layer_Zero
description:
Welcome to Layer Zero: UNLV’s Technical Cybersecurity Organization

Layer Zero is UNLV’s hub for all things cybersecurity, welcoming students from all backgrounds and experience levels. Whether you’re an aspiring professional or just curious, you’ll find endless opportunities to learn, connect, and grow in our innovative and supportive space.

What We Offer:

  • Cybersecurity Workshops & Projects: Learn hands-on skills through workshops and collaborative projects designed to build real-world expertise.

  • CTF Competitions: Test your knowledge and skills in Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions, including high-profile events like the National Cyber League.

  • Guest Speakers & Conferences: Gain insights from industry leaders and attend conferences like DEFCON, BSides Las Vegas, and DoE CyberCon.

  • Social Events & Networking: Build connections with peers and professionals through engaging social events.

  • Professional Development: Access resources to prepare for industry-standard certifications valued by government agencies and top employers.

Compete in Capture the Flag Events

CTFs are exciting cybersecurity competitions where participants solve challenges to uncover hidden “flags” and score points. As a member of Layer Zero, you’ll have opportunities to join competitions like:

  • National Cyber League (NCL)

  • NCAE Cyber Games

These competitions help sharpen your technical skills and are a great addition to your professional portfolio.

Why Join Layer Zero?

  • No Experience Needed: Start where you are—we’ll help you grow.

  • Cutting-Edge Skills: Develop expertise that sets you apart in today’s job market.

  • Community of Learners: Be part of a team that’s as passionate about cybersecurity as you are.

Discover your potential with Layer Zero. Take your first step into the world of cybersecurity today!
summary: Layer Zero is the leading technical cybersecurity organization on campus. We host workshops, projects, social events, conferences, and guest speakers. Build skills and connect with a community that values growth and collaboration. No experience necessary

name: The Rebellion
description:
The Rebellion is the official student section of UNLV athletic events. The Rebellion is a student group who is dedicated to promoting, planning, and creating experiences for students at UNLV athletic events. The Rebellion is a way for students to get involved on campus and showcase their talents in the fields of marketing, event planning, and student engagement.  

 

Interested in tailgating in Lot X alongside us this football season? Apply here now!
summary: The Rebellion is the official student section of UNLV athletic events.

name: Bangladesh Student Association, UNLV
description:
The Bangladesh Student Association (BSA) at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) is a dynamic and inclusive organization, unwavering in its commitment to serving the interests of students of Bangladeshi descent within the UNLV community. Founded with a clear vision and purpose, BSA is dedicated to fostering a robust sense of community while actively contributing to the cultural, educational, and social fabric of UNLV.
We recognize the significance of creating an environment that encourages mutual understanding, academic growth, and international goodwill within the vibrant and diverse UNLV setting. At BSA, we go beyond this acknowledgment; we actively embody these values in our mission to add distinctive contributions to UNLV and society at large.
Our mission extends beyond cultural exchange and academic enrichment. BSA serves as a conduit for members to actively engage with the UNLV community, adding distinctive values that extend beyond our cultural origins. We believe in making contributions to UNLV and society through:
1. Cultural Enrichment:
BSA is a platform for cultural understanding, sharing the richness of Bangladeshi heritage. By celebrating diversity, we contribute to the multicultural tapestry that defines UNLV.
2. Academic Excellence:
We support the academic pursuits of our members, emphasizing collaboration, knowledge exchange,and educational initiatives to enhance the academic experience at UNLV.
3. Community Engagement:
BSA encourages members to actively participate in community service initiatives, extending our impact to the broader Las Vegas community, and embodying values of service and social responsibility.
4. Global Awareness:
Recognizing the interconnectedness of the world, we foster a global perspective among our members, preparing them to be responsible and engaged global citizens.
Join BSA at UNLV, where we aspire to create a sense of belonging and cultural pride while actively contributing to the values that define UNLV and positively impact the broader society. Welcome to a community that values unity, diversity, and meaningful societal contributions within the heart of the UNLV experience.

  • To promote awareness and appreciation of Bangladesh in the community through events and social gatherings

  • Provide a platform for the Bangladeshi students at the University to have an opportunity to share their language and culture with others of similar background

  • Offer assistance with adjusting to University life for incoming Bangladeshi students
summary: UNLV's BSA cultivates community, fostering cultural exchange and academic growth for Bangladeshi students, promoting inclusivity and international goodwill on campus.

name: UNLV HOSA
description:
HOSA–Future Health Professionals is an international organization run by and for students interested in pursuing careers in the health professions.

Founded in 1976, HOSA has over 5,000 secondary and postsecondary/collegiate chapters in 54 state associations with over 260,000 members. HOSA is officially recognized by the U.S. Department of Education along with eight other career and technical student organizations. HOSA is an exclusively health oriented organization. We can devote 100% of our resources to helping students become effective, compassionate, health professionals and leaders in their chosen field of work.

The unique benefits of HOSA include volunteer service, state and national scholarships, educational symposia, over 60 different competitive and recognition events at the state and national conferences, networking with HOSA partners, as well as leadership opportunities at the local, state, and national levels. All health science and health professions schools highly prefer applicants who demonstrate academic achievement in addition to well-rounded students with actual experience with health-related community service, leadership skills, and school involvement. HOSA membership provides you with all of these key opportunities.

Fill out the form to join as a member: Official UNLV HOSA Membership Form ('25-'26)
summary: HOSA Future Health Professionals is the premier organization for student leaders pursuing careers in the healthcare industry. HOSA's mission is to promote healthcare careers through community engagement and first-hand clinical experience.

name: UNLV Society of Economic Geologists Student Chapter
description: The Society of Economic Geologists, Inc., (SEG) is an international organization of individual members with interests in the field of economic geology. The Society's membership includes representatives from industry, academia, and government institutions. Annual conferences, field trips, short courses, and publications ensure an active communication of economic geology-related concepts within the membership and with the economic geology profession at large. The UNLV SEG Student Chapter seeks to bring together like-minded individuals interested in better understanding our resource-rich environment and the minerals it contains. We are committed to advancing the study of mineral deposits and applying geological science to exploration, evaluation, and production, and secondly by disseminating scientific information through publications, courses, meetings, and field trips. We provide a link for students to network with both students and professional geoscientists in industry, academia, and government.
summary: SEG is an organization which seeks to connect students and industry professionals working in the mining and minerals industries and to provide educational opportunities in the form of field trips and workshops.

name: Leadership Engagement and Development Team
description:
The Leadership Engagement and Development (LEAD) Team is a student programming council that plans and implements events and workshops dedicated to empowering and promoting UNLV student leadership development and civic engagement.

@sllatunlv on instagram
summary: The Leadership Engagement and Development (LEAD) Team is a student programming council that plans and implements events and workshops dedicated to empowering and promoting UNLV student leadership development and civic engagement.

name: UNLV Alternative Breaks
description:
Purpose

Our purpose is to contribute to positive social change through the empowerment of our local communities.

Due to our positionality within the university, we are focused on the growth and development of UNLV students into social change agents; we do this by planning and hosting experiential, community-based learning trips in which students can learn from communities outside of the Southern Nevada/Las Vegas region. We aim to plan trips in accordance with our five core values of simplicity, community, solidarity, justice, and continuation.

 

Vision

Our vision is to create a community of active and educated individuals dedicated to the pursuit of social justice.

 

Mission

Our mission is to promote community engagement within the UNLV student body through experiential, community-based learning trips that focus on pertinent social justice topics. 

We host one-day, weekend, and week-long trips on topics such as food and housing insecurity, immigration and refugee experiences, mass incarceration, ecology and sustainability, reproductive and sexual rights, and more. We organize engagement activities such as service projects and speaking panels with community leaders and organizations so that students are able to learn about the impacts of their trip topics, dissect the root causes of those issues, and bring back new knowledge and skills for positive social change within our communities. The overarching goal of the program is to foster the development of active community collaborators through education, community engagement, dialogue, and reflection.

 

About

Alternative break trips are a great opportunity for students to experience a different community and explore social justice topics through various activities with those communities (such as service projects, discussion panels, presentations, etc.). While students travel, they will learn, live simply, and get to know their fellow travelers and host community. 

Our main focus is on community-based learning, so students can expect a balance of 80% experiential learning (Mon-Thurs) with the remaining 20% dedicated to recreation (Fri). Learning happens in sessions with community partners and in structured group reflection time (1-1.5 hours) every evening. 

Administrative or academic faculty members attend to support learning and administrative aspects. These trips are subsidized by the University to be affordable to students, but still have some financial commitment from participants (cost dependent on the trip).
summary: Our mission is to promote community engagement within the UNLV student body through experiential, community-based learning trips that focus on pertinent social justice topics.

name: Life Science Student Association
description:
The mission of the Life Sciences Student Association is to nurture students majoring or minoring in Biological Sciences and Biochemistry with the goal of raising retention rates for students in these degree programs. In our endeavor, we will provide several services to new and existing students of Biological Sciences and Biochemistry.

Undergraduate students will be provided with the opportunity for free mentorship from upperclassmen and professionals. Mentors will guide their mentees toward effective habits and skills to better their academic achievement and help them obtain their future professional career goals. We hope to provide an environment that advocates individual intellectual growth and development.
summary: Undergraduate students, majoring or minoring in Biological Sciences and Biochemistry receive free peer mentorship, and guidance from upperclassmen and learn about career opportunities from professionals in the field of Biology or Biochemistry.

name: Student Involvement & Activities
description:
Mission Statement:

Student Involvement and Activities is committed to helping students build connections to the vibrant and diverse UNLV community through innovative programming and involvement opportunities that elevate students’ academic experience, resulting in engaged and empowered leaders in a global society.

Four Core Values

  • Community Building
  • Leadership
  • Inclusion
  • Advocacy

Functional Areas

  • Student Organizations
  • Fraternity and Sorority Life
  • Campus Activities
  • CSUN Student Government
summary: Student Involvement & Activities (SIA) is guided by our four core values of Community Building, Leadership, Inclusion, and Advocacy.  SIA's purpose is to foster a sense of belonging and affinity to UNLV through involvement opportunities.

name: Student Connection & Engagement
description: Our mission affirms that belonging, visibility, and engagement are essential to student retention, well-being, and academic success. Our programs are designed to provide students with the tools, relationships, and affirming spaces they need to thrive both personally and academically.
summary: The Department of Student Connection and Engagement seeks to create a welcoming environment for all students to flourish.

name: Service Learning & Leadership
description:
Service Learning & Leadership's mission is to create curricular and co-curricular experiences for participants to discover self, learn in community, and influence systems while pursuing positive social change.
Service Learning & Leadership oversees the following programs:
    • LEAD Team (leadership development & democratic engagement)
    • UNLVolunteers (local service/volunteering)
    • Alternative Break Trips (service and learning trips)
    • Service-Learning (service integrated into the classroom)
summary: Service Learning & Leadership is an office and an RSO. SLL's mission is to create curricular and co-curricular experiences for participants to discover self, learn in community, and influence systems while pursuing positive social change.

name: Rebel Reads
description:
Rebel Reads is a book club that wishes to focus on young adult fiction, but is entirely open to all categories and genres. We will be meeting every other week for book discussions, comments, suggestions, and activities. Our goal is to read three books every semester for a total of six books every year! 

Please email us to find out information for the next meeting at unlvrebelreads@gmail.com!
summary: Rebel Reads is a book club with a concentration on young adult fiction.

name: Shadow Lane Sustainability Committee
description:
Vision: Reduce healthcare related waste and improve campus sustainability.

Mission: Help our campus become more sustainable by educating our student body, promoting behavioral change, reducing our energy and environmental footprints, improving recycling services, performing community outreach, and conducting sustainability research projects.
summary: We strive to reduce healthcare related waste and improve campus sustainability.

name: UNLV School of Dental Medicine Business Club
description: The purpose of the UNLV SDM Business club is to help students learn about the business aspect of dentistry. Focusing on business acquisitions, start-ups, demographics, marketing, insurance negotiations and so much more.
summary: The purpose of the UNLV SDM Business club is to help students learn about the business aspect of dentistry. Focusing on business acquisitions, start-ups, demographics, marketing, insurance negotiations and so much more.

name: UNLV's Disney Club 33
description:
Mission:

We are a social organization for all who enjoy the wonders of Disney. Through our enthusiasm, we will have events and meetings that showcase the many attractions, movies, food and magic of Disney. By talking about and experiencing the park itself, UNLV's Disney Club 33 attempts to inspire a deeper understanding of the Walt Disney Corporation.

 

“You can design and create the most beautiful place in the world. But it takes people to make the dream a reality” ~Walt Disney

Events:

Our events will follow the order of meeting variations stated below with different themes and subjects that align within their category. Previous events include;

 

Craft Night: This night is dedicated to making Pinterest-worthy small Disney-themed arts and crafts. You’ll want to have on a shelf in your room, wear, or post to instagram to show off your beautiful work.

 

Themed Food n’ Talk: By making our own themed food, we will be having our own take on popular delicacies based off of those found in the Disney parks or movies. We also have in-depth discussions with a list of given topics.

 

Disney Scavenger Hunt: Embark on an adventure around UNLV and solve the mystery through finding artifacts hidden around on campus using disney-themed clues.

 

Game Night: Enjoy a little competition against your friends and fellow members.  Show off your knowledge and tact with a game of cards, your favorite board game, in a trivia showdown, or through a video game PvP.



Special Events:

Annual Disneyland Trip: To fully enjoy the parks, we will be planning a trip to the California Disney parks during Spring break. To cut down on the costs, we will be having various fundraisers throughout the year.

 

2025-2026 Board:

President: Raegan Livers

Vice President: Aniston McTyre

Treasurer: Akira Robinson

Secretary: Jessica Shellman

Social Media Manager: Kaylee Tuss

Advisor: Dr. Patty Coaley
summary: We are an organization for all who enjoy the wonders of Disney. We have social meetings including movies discussions, games, crafts, scavenger hunts and more! We have a fun and safe community where we can celebrate the magic that Disney holds.

name: UNLV Aikido Club
description:
One translation of the martial art of Aikido is the "Way of Peace". Morihei Ueshiba, the original founder of Aikido, sought balance and, inevitably, peace through his martial art and philosophy. Aikido seeks to develop strong bonds between practitioners, promote mind and body coordination through various martial techniques.

The UNLV Aikido Club provides a welcoming, non-competitive, safe and clean environment for students to grow and learn no matter what their age, gender, physical fitness or training level. Aikido is more than a kicking and punching practice; it is a social, philosophical, and physical experience that you can use every day of your life.
summary: An organization dedicated to the physical practice, philosophical study & real world applications of the art of Aikido.

name: Rebels History Club
description:
To facilitate, house, communicate and raise interest in History across the campus of UNLV! A way to connect, engage, network, and socialize with fellow history buffs, professionals, and everyone interested in History through meetings, events, and other activities.

The Rebels History Club at UNLV is open to anyone interested in History. You do not have to be a History major or meet a minimum GPA requirement to join. However, it can be a stepping stone to joining Phi Alpha Theta- National History Honor Society in the future!
summary: A way to connect, engage, network, and socialize with fellow history buffs, professionals, and everyone interested in History through meetings, events, and other activities.

name: Rebel Game Devs
description:
Game devs unite!

Rebel Game Devs is a group of students interested in fine-tuning our video game development skills. Our organization is open to anyone interested in any game development discipline: music, sound, graphics, animation, gameplay, scripting, marketing, and software development. We're open to anyone interested in entering the video game industry regardless of experience. This is a great opportunity to start a video game portfolio, gain experience working on a software development team, discuss current trends and topics with other future or current developers, and network with prospective and professional members of the industry.

Let's make a game!

Discord Link: https://discord.gg/tkHKPwNn2U
summary: Calling all UNLV game devs! Rebel Game Devs is the place for students of UNLV interested in all aspects of game development to congregate. From programming to writing to art to audio, we cover it all!

name: UNLV Tennis Club
description: The UNLV Tennis Club wants to promote tennis as a competitive and recreational sport. We are open to all UNLV students, faculty, and alumni and is a great way for Rebels to meet new people while having a workout. We will put on a number of events from small get-togethers, group classes, and mini tournaments. We will also offer volunteer opportunities for tennis events around town.
summary: The UNLV Tennis Club provides members an opportunity to learn tennis, sharpen playing ability, get a workout, and meet other players! The UNLV Tennis Club provides a community where tennis players of all ability levels can come and enjoy tennis.

name: UNLV Rowing Club
description:
Join the UNLV Rowing Club at our university and unleash the power of ultimate fitness and strength while embarking on an epic journey to learn rowing with an incredible team. No experience is needed! Whether you're a seasoned athlete or a newcomer, we welcome everyone to join us for the Fall and Spring Semesters.

Rowing provides a full-body workout that engages all major muscle groups, sculpting your body into a powerhouse of strength and stamina. You'll also have the opportunity to learn together with a crew of passionate teammates, forging unbreakable bonds and creating a supportive and friendly atmosphere.

Our skilled coaches will guide you from the first stroke to mastering expert-level skills, even if you have never set foot in a boat before. We understand your hectic life and offer various practice options to tailor your training around your busy schedule.

To join our dynamic team, please reach out to our current President, Chase Campbell, via email at campbc18@unlv.nevada.edu. Our practice schedule for the Fall and Spring Semester is as follows:

- Monday: 5:30 am - 7:30 am
- Wednesday: 5:30 am - 7:30 am
- Friday: 5:30 am - 7:30 am
- Saturday: 7:00 am - 9:00 am

We practice at Lake Las Vegas.
summary: A competitive men's and women's intercollegiate rowing team

name: Nevada Water Environment Association
description:
Nevada's Water Environment Association (NWEA) is an educational non-profit member organization of the Water Environment Federation (WEF) designed to provide water quality professionals around the State with the latest in water quality education, training, and business opportunities. NWEA’s diverse membership includes scientists, engineers, regulators, academics, utility managers, plant operators, and other professionals involved with municipal and industrial water treatment. 

 

The UNLV Student Chapter of NWEA aims to give students and young professionals programs, resources, and networking opportunities to launch a successful career in the water and wastewater field. We accomplish this by providing professional development and leadership opportunities through involvement in: (1) engineering design competition that addresses solutions to local water issues within WEF and NWEA, (2) having guest speakers, (3) sharing resources and (4) networking within the student chapter and with local professionals.
summary: NWEA provides students and young professionals the keys to launch a successful career in the water and wastewater field by promoting involvement within WEF (National Chapter), NWEA, and providing professional development and leadership opportunities.

name: UNLV Chapter of AISES
description:
Student membership is free! Join the National org then contact us to become a member of the UNLV chapter!

Mission 

AISES is a national nonprofit organization focused on substantially increasing the representation of Indigenous peoples of North America and the Pacific Islands in science, technology, engineering and math (STEM) studies and careers.

 

Vision

The vision of AISES is for the next seven generations of Indigenous people to be successful, respected, influential, and contributing members of our vast and ever-changing global community.
summary:
AISES: Advancing Indigenous people in STEM studies and careers every day.

Is your major in engineering, gaming, hospitality, technology, math-related or any of the sciences? Join us! 
We are building a UNLV chapter of AISES.

name: UNLV Association for Computing Machinery
description: The Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) is an international learned society for computing. It was founded in 1947, and is the world's largest scientific and educational computing society. UNLV ACM is the student chapter here at UNLV.
summary: The Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) is an international learned society for computing. It was founded in 1947, and is the world's largest scientific and educational computing society. UNLV ACM is the student chapter here at UNLV.

name: UNLV Formula Racing
description:
Introduction

UNLV Formula Racing (UFR) is a formula-style motorsports club at UNLV that competes in the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) Formula Student design competition. We are focused on designing, building, and racing Nevada’s first all electric race car. As a motorsports organization, we do more than just engineering; we run finance, sponsorship, graphic design, and events planning, just to name a few!

 

DESIGN                    BUILD                    RACE

 

At UFR our motto is design, build, race. Those three words encompass our ultimate goal and aspirations as a team. Design: Every part of our car is carefully thought out and designed so that we can make as many performance gains as possible, while still managing a healthy budget and timeline. Build: Students get excellent opportunities to receive hands-on experience with not only design, but the actual building of a race car. Students receive machining experience and electrical training, in addition to countless other skills. Race: When it comes to UFR, the one thing everyone is excited about is racing. Racing is a universal passion that we all share, at UFR we all work together towards achieving the goal of racing and eventually winning at competitions.

 

Quick Facts:

  • UNLV Formula Racing was originally founded as UNLV Formula Hybrid. We have since changed our goals towards full electric racing, and we are currently working on our very first electric vehicle. 
  • Cars competing in FSAE competitions are incredibly quick. Delft University of Technology set the world record for fastest 0-62mph time in a 4 wheeled vehicle at just 1.4 seconds!
  • Competitions are hosted every June in Michigan, but one off competitions are hosted all around the country.
  • Teams from around the world come to compete. Everyone from Singapore to Germany shares a common passion for FSAE Competition.
  • FSAE is a great way to get into the motorsports industry. The majority of racing engineers were previously FSAE students. Even students from UNLV go on to work for Honda and Stewart-Haas Racing. 
  • We have partnerships with a wide variety of Industry giants. From Haas Automation, K2W Precision, SolidWorks, Tesla, and many more, students get a great opportunity to work with industry professionals.

 

Want to Join? 

UFR is a club for everyone on campus. We encourage students from all disciplines and majors to join and help out if they share a passion for racing. Some of our most successful students are in Business, Graphic Design, Political Science, and Information Systems fields, to name a few. There are always tasks and things to do at UFR, so feel free to check us out and see if you can help out.

To start the process of becoming a member of UNLV Formula Racing, please reach out to one of the contacts in the contact information section. We are generally quickest to respond to our email, but Instagram is also a good way to get in touch. Just send us an email with a short introduction about yourself and what your interests or passions in racing, electric cars, or engineering are. Once you let us know that you are interested and would like to join we reach out and can help to get you started on projects.

 

Benefits to Membership

  • Joining a community of like minded racing enthusiasts.
  • The opportunity to do groundbreaking and highly technical work.
  • FSAE is always a great resume builder, and something that industrial giants in the racing industry are always looking for in applicants.
  • Working on a project that does not end. There are always testing and improvements to be made. At UFR you can participate throughout your entire degree.
  • No entry level barriers. Students of all skill levels and years are encouraged to join.
  • Free machine and electrical training.
  • Connections, scholarships, internships exclusively provided through FSAE and SEMA.
  • Annual passes to SEMA and FSAE Competitions.
  • Learning environment. UFR is a place to learn and practice the engineering design process. At UFR we encourage students to use the club as a chance to learn what engineering is like in practice.
  • Work on your own time. UFR allows you to work on designs and car building entirely on your own free-time.
  • Gain on campus connections to professors, staff, and other faculty members.

 

We invite you to join UNLV Formula Racing and be a part of the future of racing at UNLV. Whether you are a student interested in engineering, business, or design, there is a place for you in our team. By joining UNLV Formula Racing, you will gain hands-on experience in all aspects of the electrical car development process and contribute to groundbreaking engineering in Nevada. Don't miss out on this opportunity to be a part of something truly extraordinary. Email us at formulasae@unlv.edu to get involved.

 

Industry Partner, Donors, Sponsors:

UFR works with a wide variety of partners and sponsors, however we are always excited to engage in new relationships with racing entities. If you are interested in contributing to UNLV Formula Racing's efforts please considering making a gift to our Rebel Raiser page. All proceeds go directly to the team's activities. All gifts are appreciated! 

https://rebelraiser.unlv.edu/project/33487
summary: UNLV Formula Racing (UFR) is an engineering organization that competes in the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) Formula SAE Competition. UFR designs, builds, and races a car built from the ground up.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description:
Who we are

The Vietnamese Student Association at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas is a warm and close community of prospective students and current students of UNLV.  We welcome students and friends from all over the world to join us and learn more about Vietnamese culture and tradition.

Our mission

To build a family for students to support each other while also strengthening our friendship with students from various cultures.

What we do:

  • Support prospective students, and current students in daily tasks, cultural differences, and academic matters.
  • Organize fun, interactive indoor/outdoor activities to help to relieve stress from studying and explore Viet's traditions.
  • Promote Vietnamese culture with other students on campus and emphasize cultural variety in campus life
  • Share experiences and opportunities about studying, working, and living in the States/abroad. 

Check our website to find out more!

Get in touch with us at: https://linktr.ee/VSAUNLV
summary: The purpose of the VSA is to promote and celebrate Vietnamese cultures within the UNLV community, to create a welcoming and inclusive environment for all UNLV students, regardless of their backgrounds, and to foster friendships and support one another

name: Lowkey Art Therapy
description: Lowkey Art Therapy is a club designed to allow students to express themselves and de-stress in a creative and safe place. Students can be creative while also being able to share their thoughts and emotions in a confidential and judgment-free environment. We aspire to improve students' well-being through the use of visual art.
summary: Lowkey Art Therapy is a club designed to allow students to express themselves and de-stress in a creative and safe place. Students can be creative while also being able to share their thoughts and emotions in a confidential and judgment-free environment.

name: Beyond Thought Creative Arts Journal
description:
UNLV's Creative Arts Journal runs Beyond Thought Creative Arts Journal. We we publish fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry, multimedia & performing arts, and 2D/3D art. The journal's goal is to promote artistic freedom among undergraduate students from different backgrounds across the country.

 

We provide opportunities for students to gain experience in a professional publishing setting. There are 10 teams members can join: Submission Review (one for each of the 5 genres), Event, Blog, Marketing, and Web & Graphic Design. Those looking to enhance their resume or build their portfolio have that opportunity with the journal.

 

Art deserves a sanctuary and Beyond Thought cultivates an environment where artistic ambition can flourish. We create, we inspire, and we welcome the art thriving all around us.

Join our Discord server to get in on the action!
summary: UNLV's Creative Arts Journal runs Beyond Thought Creative Arts Journal. We publish fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry, multimedia, and 2D/3D art. We create, we inspire, and we welcome the art thriving all around us

name: Student Union & Event Services
description: We are committed to growth by creating exceptional guest experiences by providing safe, clean, well-maintained facilities and equipment; opportunities for student education and development; and value-added services while adhering to our core values.
summary:
We are committed to growth by creating exceptional guest
experiences by providing safe, clean, well-maintained facilities and
equipment; opportunities for student education and development; and
value-added services while adhering to our core values.

name: UNLV HOPE Scholars
description:
The UNLV HOPE Scholars Program is a partnership between the Clark County School District Title I HOPE Program, Nevada Partnership for Homeless Youth, and UNLV.  The program is specifically designed to assist UNLV students who qualify as unaccompanied homeless youth in Clark County in securing year-round housing, academic support, and financial support while attending UNLV. 

The UNLV HOPE Resources Program offers support to students experiencing food, housing, and basic needs insecurity. With our Take What You Need Program, students are able to access basic need items such as hygiene products/toiletries, clothing, nonperishable foods, school supplies, household and kitchen items, and childcare items. We aim to connect students to on and off campus resources, including on-campus housing support through the Clark County Housing Grant.
summary: The UNLV HOPE Scholars Program is a partnership between the Clark County School District Title I HOPE Program, Nevada Partnership for Homeless Youth, and UNLV.  The program is specifically designed to assist graduating seniors who qualify as unaccompanie

name: Campus Recreational Services
description:
Our Mission

To promote healthy lifestyles through diverse opportunities and experiences that stimulate personal growth and a connection to UNLV.

Our Vision

Every individual will be inspired to build meaningful connections and discover endless possibilities.

Programs and Services Offered

  • 200 meter or 1/8 mile Indoor Jogging Track
  • 6-lane, 25 yard Lap Pool
  • Spa, Leisure, and Vortex Pool
  • Multi-Purpose Activity Courts (MAC)
  • Cardio Equipment, Selectorized Strength Equipment, Functional Trainers, and Free Weights
  • Intramural Sports: Individual, Team & Special Events
  • Sport Clubs
  • Pro Shop
  • Locker Rentals
  • Open Rec Equipment Rentals
  • Outdoor Adventures and Equipment Rentals
  • Private Swim Lessons
  • Learn to Swim Group Lessons
  • Personal Training
  • Fitness Assessments including Body Composition Analysis
  • Group Fitness (Rebel X) - Classes: 65+ classes per week
  • Massage Chairs available at: Rebel Wellness Zone
  • Einstein Bros. Bagels and I.O. Fuel Smoothies
  • Towel Service
  • Fitness Trainer Certification Course
  • American Red Cross Certifications
  • PEX Activity Classes for Academic Credit
  • These services are available to SRWC members and UNLV students who are registered for at least 4 or more credit hours during the current semester.

Campus Life Commitment to Equity & Inclusion

We, members of UNLV Campus Life, courageously dedicate ourselves to inclusive and equitable practices in order to eradicate discrimination and create justice. We will:

  • actively seek our communities' voices,
  • acknowledge and address barriers,
  • challenge ourselves and others, and
  • celebrate and engage the power of our diverse talent.
summary: Our mission is to promote healthy lifestyles through diverse opportunities and experiences that stimulates personal growth and a connection to UNLV.

name: Graduate College
description:
With an institutional mission of advancing graduate education at UNLV, the Graduate College supports our more than 155 graduate certificate, master's, specialist, and doctoral programs. We work closely with departments and the graduate faculty to provide UNLV's more than 4,000 graduate students with the highest quality academic experience, not only through course work, but also through research/creative activity and professional development opportunities. Our overarching goal is to support and promote UNLV's unique blend of graduate programs while providing services that facilitate graduate study and enhance student learning.

Our Graduate College top tier mission is to provide strong, collaborative leadership promoting a lively intellectual climate where:

  1. the highest quality graduate scholarship and research can flourish;
  2. we provide advanced educational opportunities to graduate students and empower them to success;
  3. we offer exemplary student services across the graduate lifecycle;
  4. we cultivate a graduate community of faculty and student scholars who thrive in collaboration with partners throughout Nevada; and
  5. we strategically grow our professional graduate programs and our research doctoral community.

Our core values and guiding principles are: excellence, equity, diversity, opportunity, and impact.

We strive daily to cultivate a campus climate that celebrates new ideas and diversity of perspectives, to encourage and support outstanding graduate research and creative activity, and to advocate for graduate education and the numerous benefits it brings to faculty, our campus, our students, and the community. The Graduate College collaborates with academic colleges and departments to provide outstanding graduate programs and to assist graduate students from admissions through graduation.
summary: With an institutional mission of advancing graduate education at UNLV, the Graduate College supports our more than 155 graduate certificate, master's, specialist, and doctoral programs. We work closely with departments and the graduate faculty to provide

RebelCard – Your Official UNLV ID
