name: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description:
The UNLV Wellness Zone and the Student Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS) and offer a variety of services to help students address mental health concerns to make the most out of the college experience.

 

UNLV Wellness Zone

 

The UNLV Wellness Zone environment is one of inclusion, energy, and relaxation. Our programming targets the eight dimensions of the Wellness Wheel.

 

Occupational — career, certifications, work ethic, school, training, interests, abilities

Spiritual — values, intuition, character, ethics, tradition, enthusiasm, spiritual fulfillment

Intellectual — time management, study skills, goal setting, memory, test preparation, imagination

Physical — recreation, nutrition, sleep, relaxation, energy level, fitness, preventative maintenance

Emotional — personality, stress management, self-efficacy, love, joy, hardiness

Environmental — community service, world and political awareness, natural resource management, recycling, food choices

Social — relationships, culture, organizations, family, friends, clubs, parties, communication

Financial — money management, basic needs, building good credit, establishing personal budgets, learning not to let money indicate your self-worth

 

Discover UNLV. Discover Wellness. Discover Yourself. 

 

Put your wellness first with our free wellness supplies, including safer sex and menstruation products. Decompress in our relaxation room with automated massage chairs. Learn more about your body's responses and functions with our biofeedback device, which will give you a real-time body analysis to help you improve your wellness and reduce stress. Address your physical and fitness needs with the UNLV Recreation services of fitness assessments, personal training, and body composition located in our office. 

 

You can request our services that include: targeted wellness programming, Certified Peer Education (CPE) training, Mental Health First Aid (MHFA), and classroom/RSO presentations. 


Do you want to make an impact at UNLV through Wellness? Do you have a passion for holistic wellness and/or plan to have a career in this area? Reach out to take on a leadership role in restarting one of our Registered Student Organizations by emailing us so we can connect!

 

Healthy Rebels - Play a key role in promoting health and wellness amongst UNLV students and the community. You will become a Nationally Certified Peer Educator through National Association of Student Personnel Administrators (NASPA) and work alongside Wellness Educators to make a significant difference on campus. As a Healthy Rebel, you will gain skills in leadership, program planning, facilitating, and public speaking while building your resume through hands-on experience.  

 

The UNLV Wellness Zone is located on the 2nd floor of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center (SRWC) building. Just swipe your Rebel card to enter the gym and go to the second floor. Our staff includes two full-time wellness educators and hourly student workers.

 

To learn more about UNLV Wellness Zone services call 702-895-4400 or email UNLVWellnessZone@unlv.edu

 

Student Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)

Your college years are an exciting and challenging time. Academic and environmental demands can be highly stressful. You may feel a variety of emotions, such as anxious, depressed, lonely, and many others. These feelings can sometimes interfere with academic performance, social relationships, and your physical health. CAPS can help you manage your feelings and learn coping skills so that you can make the most out of your college experience. Our clinicians are trained to help you work through problems commonly experienced by a diverse college student population. 

 

CAPS is staffed by a multidisciplinary team and provides various services including consultations, care management, individual therapy, relationship  therapy, group therapy, crisis services, Eating Disorder Treatment Team, medication services, substance abuse and addiction services, workshops, Togetherall , YOU@ UNLV, and UNLV community events. 

 

CAPS has 2 locations.  One is on the 3rd floor of the Student Recreation and Wellness Center (SRWC) building. Just enter the Student Wellness Center on the first floor and speak to our front desk to go up to the third floor. The other is in SSC-A.

 

To request a CAPS appointment call: 702-895-3627
summary: The staff of UNLV Wellness Zone and Student Counseling & Psychological Services are dedicated to helping you achieve overall wellness by providing you resources to manage your mental health concerns and successfully navigate life as a college student

name: Career Services
description:
UNLV Career Services & Workforce Development empowers students and alumni to design fulfilling careers and meaningful lives through Life Design principles and personalized support. We offer:

  • 1:1 Career Coaching
  • Workshops & Presentations
  • Networking Events
  • Career & Internship Fairs
  • CareerLaunch Online Resources

Career Coaching

Our Career & Life Design Specialist helps you explore who you are, where you want to go, and how to get there. We support students and alumni with:

  • Self-Discovery & Career Exploration: Identify interests, strengths, and values; explore career paths and use assessments to guide decisions.
  • Planning & Goal Setting: Build personalized action plans, turn ideas into achievable steps, and explore pathways and timelines - including graduate and professional school options.
  • Job Search Skills: Strengthen résumés and cover letters, practice interviewing, develop your elevator pitch, and learn how to navigate job boards, follow up professionally, and negotiate offers.

Additional Resources

CareerLaunch – 24/7 Career Readiness Hub: Explore tools for résumés, interviewing, career exploration, and more.

  • Visit CareerLaunch: careerlaunch.unlv.edu 
  • Check-out the Career Resources Hub: careerlaunch.unlv.edu/channels/career-resources-hub
  • View the Career Handbook: UNLV Career Handbook

Schedule a Coaching Appointment (Handshake): Meet with a career coach for personalized guidance.

  • Book on Handshake: unlv.joinhandshake.com/edu 
  • Watch the Tutorial: How to Make an Appointment Video

Attend our Signature Events: Access in-person and virtual career development opportunities, employer insights, and hands-on skill building.

  • View Upcoming Opportunities: careerlaunch.unlv.edu/channels/signature-events/ 

Request a Workshop: UNLV faculty, staff, and RSOs can request presentations on career readiness topics.

  • Submit a Request: Presentation Request Form 

Contact the Career & Life Design (Coaching) Team: CareerLifeDesign@unlv.edu
summary: We are your source for resume building, interview preparation, and a wide variety of job and internship opportunities to get you started on your path to career success.

name: Philanthropy and Alumni Engagement
description: UNLV students are a part of a special tradition - a tradition of excellence in education, service as professionals, and support of future alumni. Wherever your life's work takes you - be it business, social service, engineering, teaching, law, the arts, nursing, or hospitality - your university is here for you.  The UNLV Division of Philanthropy and Alumni Engagement (PAE) is committed to fostering a culture of engagement and giving among our students, starting from the moment you step on campus. We firmly believe that a Rebel's journey with philanthropy and alumni engagement should not wait until graduation—it begins the moment you join the Rebels Forever community.
summary: Encouraging students to get involved through various initiatives. Follow to learn more about how you can give back to UNLV and make the most of your student experience.

name: Office of Undergraduate Research
description:
OUR Vision

UNLV’s Office of Undergraduate Research (OUR) seeks to elevate the status of undergraduate research at UNLV. OUR strives to increase direct undergraduate student participation in research and to enhance research education and learning experiences. We create rich and diverse research and funding opportunities and engage students with local, regional, national, and global challenges.

 

OUR Mission

We inspire, support, and nurture undergraduates in their efforts to discover, innovate, create, and experience research at UNLV. We help undergraduate students get research ready and connect them with faculty for successful and transformative research experiences. The research skills that students develop are transferable, ensuring continued success during the long journey of career development and professional life.

 

Services

We provide students from diverse backgrounds with ample opportunities to develop as scholars. All of these experiences serve as a real-world proxy to prepare students for graduate school, professional workforce, and life beyond college.

The specific programs and services we offer include:

  • Individualized research advising
  • Research funding opportunities
  • Research Skills Academy (RSA) with professional development and career training opportunities
  • Triannual research symposia: fall, spring, and summer
  • Monthly newsletter and research communications to highlight latest UNLV research news and opportunities for undergraduate researchers
  • Research-oriented instructional support for faculty through classroom presentations
  • A unique opportunity for undergraduate students to publish their research in SURJ: Spectra Undergraduate Research Journal
 

What is Research?

Research is a systematic process to discover, create, or contribute to generalizable knowledge. Definitions and categorization of research vary by professional field, academic discipline, method of inquiry, and objective.

 

What is Undergraduate Research?

The Council on Undergraduate Research defines undergraduate research as an “inquiry or investigation conducted by an undergraduate student that makes an original intellectual or creative contribution to the discipline.”

Undergraduate research is faculty-driven, student-centered, and institutionally supported and provides the combination of factors necessary for pedagogical effectiveness, enhanced learning outcomes, research productivity, and research program sustainability.

What are the Benefits of Undergraduate Research?

According to the Council on Undergraduate Research, active undergraduate student participation in research:

  • Enhances student learning through mentoring relationships with faculty
  • Increases retention and graduation in academic programs
  • Increases enrollment in graduate education and provides effective career preparation
  • Develops critical thinking, creativity, problem-solving, and intellectual independence
  • Develops an understanding of research methodology
  • Promotes an innovation-oriented culture
summary: The Office of Undergraduate Research aims to inspire and support undergraduates in their efforts to discover, innovate, create, and experience research at UNLV.

name: UNLV Honors Rebellion
description:
The UNLV Honors Rebellion is a team that meets on a weekly basis focused on our mission of building camaraderie, improving the health of students, and creating a safe space for personal growth for students. Our activities range from boot-camp style workouts, resistance training, hiking & trail running, and indoor rock-climbing! We look forward to having you join the team and perhaps run a Tough Mudder! HOORAH!

 

For more info about our workouts/times/general questions... join our discord: tinyurl.com/HCrebelliondiscord

 

Important Links (LinkTree)
summary: The UNLV Honors Rebellion is a team dedicated to building friendships as well as physical and mental strength. We train together on a weekly basis to build our endurance to complete obstacle course races as a team.

name: UNLV Rebels For Life
description: To raise awareness on the human rights issue of abortion, provide support and resources for women from different walks of life who are facing unplanned pregnancy- both during and after pregnancy, and to lead & participate in pro-life initiatives
summary: To raise awareness on the human rights issue of abortion, provide support and resources for women who are facing unplanned pregnancy- both during and after pregnancy, and to lead & participate in pro-life initiatives.

name: Neuroscience Undergraduate Research Organization
description: The Neuroscience Undergraduate Research Organization (NURO) is an interdisciplinary group dedicated to enhancing student pre-professional experiences through professional development, community involvement, and research opportunities. Affiliated with the UNLV Brain Health Department, NURO coordinates community walks and workshops while providing resources to help students prepare for various professional paths. In our research initiatives our organization actively informs members about research labs seeking assistants and provides adequate resources. Lastly, NURO  takes pride in promoting neuroscience and related fields through outreach to high schools partnered with NURO.
summary: The Neuroscience Undergraduate Research Organization (NURO) enhances student experiences at UNLV by offering professional development, research opportunities, and community involvement through workshops, resource coordination, and outreach.

name: Armenian Student Association of UNLV
description: Armenian Student Association is an organization dedicated to bringing Armenian students together in the Las Vegas community and promoting the Armenian culture and language at UNLV. Our goal is to provide a place for Armenian students to form friendships, receive mentoring, and take part in the growing community of Las Vegas.
summary: Armenian Student Association is an organization dedicated to bringing together Armenian students in the community and promoting Armenian culture and language at UNLV.

name: Hope at UNLV
description: Hope at UNLV is a Christian student organization affiliated with Hope Church Movement. We believe students can live on mission and change the college campus. The mission of Hope at UNLV is to give students a place to connect with others and explore their faith. We have frequent meetings on campus to discuss spiritual questions, as well as outdoor activities and competitions on and off-campus to build community.
summary: Hope at UNLV is a Christian student organization affiliated with Hope Church Movement. We believe that students can own their faith, live on mission, and lead and change our campus.

name: Rebel Report
description: The Rebel Report is a student-run digital sports media platform housed inside the Hank Greenspun School of Journalism and Media Studies at UNLV. The Rebel Report team covers sports all over Las Vegas, providing stories and content you won't find anywhere else and giving students opportunities as members of the sports media.
summary: Student organization reporting on sports in Las Vegas.

name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space
description: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS) empowers students to participate and make an impact in space exploration. SEDS helps students develop their technical and leadership skills by providing opportunities to manage and participate in national projects as well as to attend conferences, publish their work, and develop their professional network, in order to help students become more effective in their present and future careers in industry, academia, government, and education.
summary: SEDS UNLV is a science and engineering based student organization aimed at developing and participating in a variety of space-related projects and competitions. SEDS UNLV designed, built, and tested Nevada's first ever student led rocket engine!

name: UNLV Soccer Club
description: Our focus is to bring together UNLV students who want to play soccer. Our aim is to play once or twice a week in the SRWC. Sometimes we are able to play outside on the Intramural Fields. The games will be 5v5 including a goalkeeper. There will be 2-10 teams organized per session. We want everyone to play as much as possible so games will end either after 2 goals are scored by one team OR after a 5 minute timer expires. Our organization allows you to meet amazing new people with similar interests as well as get some good exercise. We'd love to have you join our club! If you have any further questions please email Alain or Mana. Their emails can be found below.
summary: We are an organization of students who love soccer. We hang out, chat, play soccer, and most of all, WE HAVE FUN!. We play 5v5 indoor soccer on the first floor of the SRWC weekly. We play Thursday nights at 7:30PM and Saturday's at 2:30PM.

name: UNLV Women's Volleyball Club
description:
Women’s Club Volleyball is designed for women at UNLV to compete competitively at a collegiate level.

The UNLV Women’s Volleyball Club provides an opportunity for women to continue to play volleyball competitively at the collegiate level. We provide the opportunity for bi-weekly exercise and wellness, along with allowing participants to establish strong social connections and school pride that enhance their college experience here at UNLV.

Our team plans to compete in several tournaments throughout the fall and spring semesters and remain active in the volleyball community throughout the year.
summary: UNLV Women’s Volleyball Club allows for women to continue competing at the collegiate level. At this club, we plan to build lasting friendships, teamwork, and leadership skills for all members through practices and tournaments that will last a lifetime,

name: UNLV Pre-Professional Advising Center
description:
Services

We are your one-stop shop for professional school help! PPAC offers the following services to students:

  • Assistance with navigating the application process for professional school
  • Providing direction on supplemental application pieces, attaining relevant experiences in community service, clinical experience, volunteering, research, and extra-curriculars
  • Comprehensive guide to what makes a well-qualified professional school applicant (personal attributes)
  • Advising appointments tailored to students’ individual needs
  • Guidance on academic requirements, curriculum, and policy information

**The pre-professional advising center is committed to serving our current UNLV students. Before making an appointment with the PPAC, you need to be admitted to UNLV. Thanks for your understanding!

***Please note: you will have two advisors as you complete your degree. Visit with your respective college academic advisor for all things related to your degree (course planning, petitions, change of majors, etc.). The PPAC will compliment your degree advising with guidance directly related to your professional school application.

To make an appointment with our advisors, please call or email:

(702) 895-2959
ppac@unlv.edu

 

https://youtu.be/uvGMoxuG83M
summary: UNLV Pre-Professional Advising CenterThe Pre-Professional Advising Center (PPAC) exists to serve UNLV students interested in pursuing a career in health-related professions. We will assist and support students through comprehensive advising, maximizing r

name: Student Affairs Strategic Planning & Assessment
description:
Student Affairs Strategic Planning & Assessment provides faculty and staff in Student Affairs departments with leadership and support for strategic planning, program review, and assessment processes.

Student Affairs Strategic Planning & Assessment achieves its mission by:

  • Aligning goals, strategic objectives, and outcomes with institutional priorities.
  • Fostering data-informed decision-making for the Division, as well as its departments, programs, and services.
  • Serving as a resource for Student Affairs departments’ research and assessment endeavors including national benchmark surveys.
  • Creating a culture of assessment by expanding faculty and staff knowledge about assessment, methods, and student learning.
  • Serving as a liaison with other institutional assessment and research entities.
  • Broadly sharing information gathered through Student Affairs assessment work.
summary: Student Affairs Strategic Planning & Assessment provides faculty and staff in Student Affairs departments with leadership and support for strategic planning, program review, and assessment processes.

name: College of Liberal Arts
description:
The College of Liberal Arts offers a rich variety of courses, majors and interdisciplinary programs in the humanities and the social sciences. Being well educated involves developing an array of intellectual skills, having the ability to use a variety of methodologies to understand our changing world, and having a broad base of diverse knowledge. Our courses and degree programs are designed to provide such a foundation and to create life-long learners who can adapt to the inevitably changing environments they will encounter.

 

The college includes outstanding faculty who thoughtfully balance their roles as creators of knowledge through original research and disseminators of knowledge as dedicated teachers. Many of our faculty have earned national and international reputations as a result of their published work in fields ranging from Anthropology to Women’s Studies. We encourage you to take advantage of the wealth of opportunity that our faculty offer at every level. We are also justly proud of our Wilson Advising Center. As you make decisions about your educational future, we encourage you to consult with Wilson Advising and to speak with any professor or chairperson in the College to plan your program of study. Whether you are taking courses in the College to fulfill core requirements or are majoring in one of our degree programs, we welcome you.
summary: The College of Liberal Arts offers students a well-rounded education in the humanities and social sciences, and provides students with a solid foundation for a lifetime of learning and discovery.

name: Office of the Vice Provost for Undergraduate Education
description: The Office of the Vice Provost for Undergraduate Education is comprised of general education, online education, academic program assessment, advising practice, and retention, progression, and completion initiatives.
summary: The Office of the Vice Provost for Undergraduate Education is comprised of general education, online education, academic program assessment, advising practice, and retention, progression, and completion initiatives.

name: Personal Training at the SRWC
description:
Personal Training is encouraged for everyone that exercises from beginners, advanced, or to competitive athletes. Personal training provides motivation, guidance, and consistency for healthy habits, weight management and a better quality of life!

If you are a UNLV student, faculty/staff, spouse or domestic partner, or community member with a paid Student Recreation and Wellness Center membership, you are eligible to sign up for personal training.

Get Started

  1. Stop by the Rebel Wellness Zone located on the 2nd floor of the SRWC to complete an interest form. We will match you with a personal trainer who best fits your availability and goals.
  2. A personal trainer will contact you to set up an assessment and consultation. You will need to purchase your package before meeting.
  3. Learn more about our trainers!

Get a Personal Trainer

Personal Training Levels & Rates

Meet the Trainers

Become a Trainer

 

Cancellations

If you are unable to make your appointment, you must call your personal trainer and the Rebel Wellness Zone at 702-895-4400 to cancel. The session must be cancelled 24 hours in advance, during which time you may reschedule your appointment. If you fail to call and you miss your appointment, it will result in the forfeiture of that session.
summary: Personal Training is encouraged for everyone that exercises from beginners, advanced, or to competitive athletes. Personal training provides motivation, guidance, and consistency for healthy habits, weight management and a better quality of life!

name: English Language Center
description: The English Language Center (ELC) provides tailored language support and helps students grow with confidence to communicate effectively in global contexts. Our caring, expert team of instructors help students accomplish their goals, whether they want to apply to a degree program at UNLV or improve their workplace English skills.
summary: We offer support to English language learners and multilingual students, faculty, and staff via classes, tutoring, and weekly GameDay events.

name: The Intersection
description: The Intersection: Academic Multicultural Resource Center is a comprehensive and sustainable resource center for students — particularly first-generation and students of color — faculty and staff, and our community at large. The Intersection provides access and linkages to research, people, information, and services. Here, all members of campus can discuss their differences, discover their similarities, and build a shared sense of belonging.
summary: We are a one-stop resource for UNLV’s highly diverse student body — a comprehensive multicultural center grounded in the academic life of our students. As an intersecting campus resource, we help ensure students, particularly first-generation and student

name: UNLV Rebel Robotics
description: UNLV Robotics strives to be a leader in competitive robotics at UNLV and the surrounding community while educating and improving member’s abilities throughout multiple disciplines in a competitive environment.
summary: UNLV Robotics is dedicated to improving the local robotics community by competing in various robotics competitions, specifically VEX U, in order to educate and improve member’s abilities in multiple engineering disciplines.

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Society
description:
Pre-Physician Assistant Society (PPAS) is a community established in 2019 at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. This organization focuses on preparing students for the Physician Assistant profession. Physician Assistants are medical professionals that work in collaboration or under the supervision of physicians, focusing on patient care and advocacy. We provide UNLV students with volunteer events, clinical opportunities, peer mentorships, GRE prep, and information to guide members on the pre-PA path. Additionally, our society hosts speaker events with PA schools and PA professionals that inform our members on potential PA schools, the CASPA application process, and PA school interviews.

 

If you are interested in joining, please fill out this Google Form

Come learn more about our society and the physician assistant profession at one of our general meetings!
summary: Our mission is to provide members the opportunities for clinical hours, mentorships, GRE practices, and other guidances in preparation for PA school.

name: Institute of Management Accountants Student Chapter
description:
Chapter Overview


UNLV’s IMA Student Chapter will provide members with opportunities to serve in the community, develop professional relationships, and build new skills. With the below-stated mission, vision, and core values, we hope to establish a foundation for students to learn, lead, and achieve.


Mission Statement - The IMA Student Organization works to prepare emerging leaders and future accountants by providing students with unique opportunities to network with professionals, strengthen their community engagement, and enrich their knowledge in business.


Vision Statement - The IMA Student Organization is aiming to become a portal of assistance to incoming accounting students. As the UNLV chapter, we hope to grow and be recognized as one of the western regions best accounting programs.


Core Values - The IMA Student Organization values integrity, service and learning. We are seeking members who want an opportunity to lead and expand their knowledge and skills.


Long Term Goals
● Have at least two students taking and passing the CMA exam each year
● Maintain a relationship with IMA student members after they graduate
● Have at least five students apply for the IMA scholarships every year
summary: The IMA Student Organization works to prepare emerging leaders and future accountants by providing students with unique opportunities to network with professionals, strengthen their community engagement, and enrich their knowledge in business.

name: Honors College
description:
We are a small, selective college within the university that offers an exceptional undergraduate core experience for highly motivated students. Honors College is an educational partner with the “discipline” colleges of the University in which Honors students pursue their academic majors. The combination of a central Honors intellectual core and specialized learning in a major discipline helps develop graduates who are fully prepared to succeed in graduate and professional schools and in rewarding careers.

Honors College is distinguished by a diverse student body, an innovative curriculum taught by outstanding faculty, personalized advising, multiple research and service opportunities, scholarships, priority registration, small class sizes, and special residential arrangements. Consequently, Honors College attracts excellent students from Nevada, across the United States and around the globe, joining dedicated faculty and staff to form a unique learning community supporting educational innovation and achievement.
summary: Honors College exists to channel the energies, refine the ideas, and ignite the passions of our remarkable students.

name: Department of Anthropology
description:
ANTHROPOLOGY OPEN HOUSE

 

October 24, 1:00-4:00pm

 

What do animal bones, spears, and your Thanksgiving meal have in common?  They are all part of Anthropology...the study of human society and culture.

 

Cultural-Biological-Archaeological

 

Tour the Department of Anthropology's 8 labs to see the interesting research done by faculty, graduate, and undergraduate students at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas! 

 

Use your EventPass QR code to check-in at each lab on October 24th from 1 - 4 pm! Pick up a schedule of activities, discussions and maps.

https://www.unlv.edu/sia/eventpass

 

Stop by the registration-welcome table at WRI-C 1st floor to learn how you can be a part of the Anthropology research team too!
summary: The UNLV Anthropology Department offers teaching and training in three broad aspects of Anthropology.
