summary: A cultural organization that promotes unity, spirit, and dignity through engaging our campus and community in academic enrichment, civic awareness, and leadership opportunities, in order to provide them support and guidance throughout college and beyond.

name: Girls Who Code College Loop
description:
Within our Girls Who Code College Loop, we aim to make technology a more diverse, equitable space to ensure that everyone who has a passion for coding gets a seat at the table. Our members hone their computer science skills through hands-on workshops, get matched with exclusive networking opportunities, build their professional knowledge and, of course, have lots of fun along the way! Students from any underrepresented group in technology, along with allies, are welcome to join. 

Learn more about Girls Who Code at: https://girlswhocode.com
summary: Girls Who Code is a place for students from underrepresented groups in technology to come together and form a community. We host workshops, socials, and more where you can learn more about programming, build relationships, and get career advice.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America, University of Nevada Las Vegas Chapter
description:
Young Democratic Socialists of America is the youth and student section of the Democratic Socialists of America, and a national organization of recognized campus chapters and several hundred activists. We are students organizing in our universities, colleges, and high schools to fight for the immediate needs of workers and students while building our capacity to fight for more radical and structural changes.

We form member driven unions that give real power to thousands of student workers. We organize to defend immigrants through campaigns for sanctuary campuses. We campaign to divest our schools from fossil fuels and Israeli apartheid. We run candidates for on and off campus elections: bringing class struggle to the halls of power. We do anti-poverty work through local mutual aid programs in our communities, and much much more.

YDSA fights for democratic socialism through active campaigns to improve the lives of working people.
summary: YDSA is the youth and student section of the Democratic Socialists of America. We are students organizing to fight for the immediate needs of workers and students while building our capacity to fight for more radical and structural changes.

name: Rebel Transfer Student Organization
description: The Rebel Transfer Student Organization (RTSO) aims to provide a home for all transfer students by helping them grow academically, socially, and developmentally. We promote a sense of belonging and support for transfer students. We know the more connected and involved you are at UNLV, the more it will directly relate to your success. Throughout the Fall and Spring semesters, the UNLV CSN Transfer Program (UCTP) and RTSO will plan and promote transfer students' events and opportunities to get involved and experience campus. Stay connected to RTSO by joining our mailing list and checking your UNLV email for our regular updates.
summary: "Helping Transfer Students Bridge The Gap To The Rebel Family." Aims to be Resource for the 2000+ Transfer students each semester. Join our Discord to stay up-to-date with Transfer News: https://discord.gg/MpQZYaFs5C

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America
description:
ALPFA UNLV is a professional business organization that promotes professional development, leadership and networking, through social events and workshops as well as professional business events. We are open to all UNLV students and all majors. 

 

ALPFA National

 

About ALPFA

ALPFA (Association of Latino Professionals For America) was the first national Latino professional association in the United States, established in Los Angeles in 1972. Throughout our 50-year history we have experienced steady growth and demonstrated consistent value.

 

ALPFA currently serves more than 100,000+ professional and student members across the country – a number we plan to grow aggressively to 125,000 within the next two years. In addition to networking and professional leadership development opportunities, our members have access to over 50,000 paid summer internships through hundreds of Fortune 1000 corporate partners. We aspire to be the business partner of choice for companies seeking to hire and develop Latino talent.

 

We promote a culture of philanthropy throughout the Latino community, encouraging our members to volunteer with, and engage on behalf of, local charities. We actively seek hands-on leadership building experiences for our members to engage your considerable talent to make a measurable impact for national charitable organizations.

 

Mission Statement

 

To empower and develop Latino men and women as leaders of character for the nation, in every sector of the global economy.

 

ALPFA's intended audience will gain knowledge and perfect their competencies on professionalism, resume and cover letter building, personal finance expertise, and networks with over 100,000 national Latinx professionals. In addition, members will have access to local chapter events, networking events, resume and cover letter critique, and even mock interviews.
summary: ALPFA UNLV is a professional business organization that promotes professional development, leadership and networking, through social events and workshops as well as professional business events. We are open to all UNLV students and all majors.

name: UNLV A Cappella Society
description: The UNLV A Cappella Society presents the Scarlet Tones--UNLV’s first and flagship collegiate a cappella ensemble. This student-led and faculty-advised group performs diverse and exciting repertoire, often arranged and edited by the singers themselves. Scarlet Tones performs on campus at music and other events and around the community at a variety of functions. The ensemble is a registered student organization, and students of any major may seek membership to audition as a performer. For more information about Scarlet Tones, please contact Britohn Blank at blankb1@unlv.nevada.edu or unlvscarlettones@gmail.com.
summary: The UNLV A Cappella Society is the host organization that regulates and maintains any and all A Cappella groups on campus. For more information, please contact Dr. Joe Svendsen, advisor.

name: American Medical Women's Association, Premedical Branch
description:
The American Medical Women’s Association (AMWA) is an organization which functions at the local, national, and international level to advance women in medicine and improve women’s health. We achieve this by providing and developing leadership, advocacy, education, expertise, mentoring, and strategic alliances. AMWA brings together those in medicine and health-related fields to promote women’s health and to support members’ professional and personal development.

The Premedical Branch of AMWA at UNLV emphasizes the importance of developing successful female leaders in medicine. Through leadership, collaboration, mentorship, and community engagement, UNLV AMWA Premedical Branch promotes academic achievement and opportunities for premedical students.
summary: The American Medical Women’s Association (AMWA) Premedical Branch at UNLV is an organization that functions at the local, national, and international level to advance women in medicine and improve women’s health through professional development.

name: Association for Women Geoscientists
description:
The Association for Women Geoscientists is an international organization devoted to enhancing the quality and level of participation of women in geosciences and to introduce girls and young women to geoscience careers. Membership is open to anyone who supports AWG's goals. Our members include:

  • professionals from industry, government, museums, academia, and K-12 teachers
  • students
  • retirees
  • others interested in supporting our goals

Our diverse interests and expertise cover the entire spectrum of geoscience disciplines and career paths, providing unexcelled networking and mentoring opportunities. Our membership is brought together by a common love of earth science and the desire to ensure rewarding opportunities for women in the geosciences.
summary: The Association for Women Geoscientists is an international organization devoted to enhancing the quality and level of participation of women in geosciences.

name: Rebel Anthropomorphic Works and Recreation
description:
Rebel Anthropomorphic Works and Recreation is a student organization for furries and people who are otherwise interested in the furry fandom, a fandom united by a common interest in anthropomorphic or "human-form" animals.

Our mission is to bring together enthusiasts of anthropomorphic animals in various media and art forms at UNLV, to educate both members and the general public on the creation of said arts, and to provide a safe space in which members are free to explore radical forms of self-expression.

Join our Discord!
summary: RAWR aims to unite enthusiasts of anthropomorphic animals at UNLV.

name: Troesh Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation
description:
The Troesh Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation operates as an administrative unit within the UNLV Lee Business School. Our primary purpose is to provide educational opportunities for students, faculty, and the community to learn about entrepreneurship and related activities.

Key activities include:

  • Overseeing academic curriculum in entrepreneurship
  • Conducting research in entrepreneurship
  • Providing learning opportunities such as certificate programs, speakers and events to expose students, faculty, and community members to entrepreneurship and generate awareness of UNLV Entrepreneurship programs

Mission Statement

The Troesh Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation partners with students, entrepreneurs, and leaders in the business community to foster entrepreneurship and support business activity, especially in the greater Las Vegas area.
summary: Operating as an administrative unit within the UNLV Lee Business School, our primary purpose is to provide educational opportunities for students, faculty, and the community to learn about entrepreneurship and related activities.

name: UNLV Mountain Club
description:
The UNLV Mountain Club is a group dedicated to making outdoor activities more accessible. Our primary mission is to foster a community interested in exploring the outdoors in any form, including climbing, hiking, backpacking, and so much more. We welcome students and friends of all abilities, from beginners to seasoned adventurers.
Website: https://unlvmountainclub.squarespace.com/
Instagram: @unlvmountainclub
Discord: https://discord.gg/Vz6aENDasV

 

Frequently Asked Questions

 

How do I get connected with the UNLV Mountain Club?
Follow us on Instagram or join our Discord for information on meetings and events. For any questions, contact us through our email or social media.

How do I become an active member?
1. Join our membership roster in the UNLV Involvement Center
2. Pay the yearly $25 membership due through embolt here
Once you’re a member, you’ll get access to the trip calendar and get notified about upcoming trips.

How do I participate in a trip?
All active members have access to the Member Trip Portal on our website. Here you can view the upcoming trip schedule. In the week before a trip, members will receive an email where they can sign up for the trip and contact the trip leader.

What comes with membership?
With membership, you will receive access to the Member Trip Portal, our Guidebook Library, Gear Room, and more!

What does my membership due go to?
Your membership due helps to fund trips as well as maintain the club’s gear closet.

Am I required to go on a specific number of trips every year?
No. You can join us on as many (or few) trips as you please!

 

2025-2026 Current Leadership:

Dax Whitaker - Club President

Bridget Morrill - Gear Manager

Lilly Czerwinski - Scheduling Liason

Devyn White - Community Liason

Katie Erickson - Club Treasurer

Tanner Dezarn - Trip Leader
summary: The UNLV Mountain Club is dedicated to making outdoor activities more accessible. Our mission is to foster a community interested in exploring the outdoors in any form (climbing, hiking, backpacking). We welcome students and friends of all abilities.

name: Nevada Esports Education League
description: Nevada Esports Education League (NEEL) is an organization that educates, empowers students through esports, video game design, streaming, coding, broadcasting, and computer skills. NEEL also encourages networking and helps students find volunteer opportunities within our diverse community.
summary: To provide services for the Esports Community in volunteering, education, and networking for Esports on campus. Also to provide a safe space for students to engage in Video Game Designing and Discussions.

name: Pre-Occupational Therapy Society
description: The Pre-Occupational Therapy Society is intended to allow students that are interested in pursuing a career in occupational therapy an opportunity to learn more about the profession, learn from fellow students about pre-requisites, application advice/guidance and to network with programs and with practicing occupational therapists for opportunities in shadowing, internships, pre-professional advice and more. Most meetings will have a guest speaker either from an occupational therapy school's program or from a practicing occupational therapist to learn about who they are, how they got to where they are and what specifications about the profession are of interest to the students.
summary: This organization is for students with an interest in pursuing a career in Occupational Therapy. There will be monthly meetings where members can network and meet other students as well as hear from occupational therapy students and professionals.

name: Lee Women in Business
description:
Lee Women in Business of the University of Nevada-Las Vegas (UNLV LWIB) is a student-run professional and personal development organization dedicated to empowering, educating, and elevating a network of future business leaders through workshops, collaboration, and learning opportunities.

Lee Business School students are encouraged to join, but we welcome students of all majors and identities.
summary: Lee Women in Business is a student-run professional and personal development organization dedicated to empowering, educating, and elevating a network of future business leaders through workshops, collaboration, and learning opportunities.

name: MBA Programs
description:
The MBA Program offers a rich variety of opportunities for anyone interested in the demanding world of business. The MBA degree provides students with the skills and knowledge necessary to succeed in the fast-paced, ever-changing world of business through innovative and dynamic programs and courses.

 

The MBA experience should be so much more than classroom theory. The MBA Program offers various opportunities to expand student’s professional networks, engage the unique Las Vegas industries, and contribute to UNLV’s status as “Most Diverse Campus in the US.

 

The MBA program is designed for professionals who are looking for the extra edge to make them stand out in the market. Courses are taught by our accomplished faculty and class sizes are small to encourage interaction between the faculty and the students. Students choose the UNLV Lee Business School MBA program due to its “location, flexibility and affordability”.
summary: The MBA degree provides students with the skills and knowledge necessary to succeed in the fast-paced, ever-changing world of business through innovative and dynamic programs and courses.

name: VAW Global Health Alliance - Medical Chapter
description:
VAW Global Health Alliances is a student run organization that serves to advance
interests in the field of medicine, global health, and international volunteer work though
the philanthropic promotion of health in countries across the world. Our focus is to provide
communities with access to medical treatment, medication, and health education, while
introducing students to real-life medical situations and the deprived state of certain
populations in the world. This unique abroad experience permits students to gain
invaluable first-hand knowledge of medicine and its practice in foreign countries.

 

VAW UNLV Discord

VAW UNLV Instagram

VAW Global
summary: Volunteers Around the World is a student run organization that serves to advance interests in the field of medicine, global health, and international volunteer work.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description: MAPS strives to give back to the minority community, ensures students’ preparation for the path to medical school, and offer personal and professional opportunities for growth while providing a supportive foundation to those seeking a career in medicine.
summary: MAPS strives to give back to the minority community, ensures students’ preparation for the path to medical school, and offer personal and professional opportunities for growth while providing a supportive foundation to those seeking a career in medicine.

name: Undergraduate Law Review
description: The purpose of the UNLV Undergraduate Law Review is to convene a group of undergraduate students who are interested in writing and publishing research articles on salient legal issues/topics. The Undergraduate Law Review is open to any student, regardless of major, and provides an opportunity for any pre-law students to participate and learn more about legal writing. If you'd like to join, please complete the Membership Application here: https://forms.gle/jT6gQvxcqNmRnF3o9
summary: The Undergraduate Law Review provides an opportunity for undergraduate students to write and publish law-related research articles that are of interest to them.

name: Association of Women Surgeons
description: AWS is a not-for-profit educational and professional organization whose mission is to inspire, encourage, and enable women surgeons to realize their professional and personal goals.
summary: AWS is a not-for-profit educational and professional organization whose mission is to inspire, encourage, and enable women surgeons to realize their professional and personal goals.

name: American Public Works Association, UNLV Student Chapter
description: APWA UNLV’s purpose is to connect students to the professional world of public works through networking opportunities, and chapter and national events. Our mission is to promote professional excellence and public awareness through education, advocacy, and the exchange of knowledge.
summary: The American Public Works Association UNLV Student Chapter is an organization that connects students interested in civil engineering, infrastructure, construction management, land survey, and more to the professional world of public works.

name: UNLV Archery
description: The University of Nevada, Las Vegas Archery Club is a student-run organization that provides a fun and safe environment for students of all skill levels to learn and practice the sport of archery. We offer a variety of activities, including practices, tournaments, and social events. The club is open to all students, regardless of experience. We are a friendly and welcoming group, and we would love to have you!
summary: UNLV archery club is for both new and experienced archers to connect on campus. We practice together and will work towards competing in local and national tournaments.

name: UNLV Office of Admissions
description: Whatever your aspirations—from running for office to running a hotel—we are here to help you every step of the way. With more than 230 majors, minors, and certificates to choose from, accessible faculty, and a supportive campus community, UNLV provides the foundation to set you on your path.
summary: We are excited to share information and upcoming events, help you connect with other future Rebels, and answer questions as you prepare to attend UNLV!

name: UNLV University Libraries
description:
In support of the University’s mission and shared values, the University Libraries contribute to and support learners as they discover, access, and use information effectively for academic success, research, and life-long learning.

 

Mission Statement

The University Libraries is a welcoming and inclusive place for learning. We foster innovation, knowledge creation and discovery, and academic achievement to enrich our UNLV and Southern Nevada communities.

 

Vision Statement

Graduate lifelong learners, inspire transformative research, connect diverse communities.

 

Values

We believe:

  • Everyone deserves a welcoming, inclusive, and equitable environment where they are treated with respect and dignity.
  • Holistic student success should be fostered.
  • Our work should be driven by the needs of our communities.
  • All users have the right to equitable and ready access to information.
  • Intellectual curiosity and lifelong learning should be nurtured.
  • Collaboration and diverse perspectives enhance our work.

 

Diversity And Inclusion Statement

The University Libraries is open to all people and affirms its commitment and dedication to diversity, inclusion, equity, and cultural awareness. We welcome everyone, including people of color, immigrants, adherents of all belief systems or religions and those that do not profess or practice a religion, people of all genders and sexual orientations, and all other members of marginalized communities or oppressed groups. We encourage discovery and learning in spaces where all people are respected and protected. Within our spaces, we seek to protect everyone from all forms of hostility and oppression, including sexism, misogyny, ableism, racism, classism, xenophobia, homophobia, transphobia, and religious persecution.

 

As a library, we defend intellectual freedom, oppose censorship, and uphold our commitment to the free and open exchange of ideas and viewpoints that is the very foundation of democracy and a part of our campus mission. We honor the voices and lived experiences of all members of our community. We see it as fundamental to the Libraries’ mission to foster a sense of belonging and to provide welcoming and inclusive surroundings where all people are treated with respect and dignity.

 

Our Libraries

Architecture Studies Library
Health Sciences Library
Lied Library
Music Library
Teacher Development and Resources Library
summary: In support of the University’s mission and shared values, the University Libraries contribute to and support learners as they discover, access, and use information effectively for academic success, research, and life-long learning.

name: Office of Study Abroad Programs
description:
The Office of Study Abroad Programs is committed to the development of global perspectives and intercultural understanding through high-quality study abroad opportunities and faculty and student programs which focus on international education. The Office of Study Abroad Programs will further the mission of the University of Nevada, Las Vegas by offering multidisciplinary programs which include opportunities for students and faculty to work cooperatively and develop skills that will serve Nevada in its role in the global community.

 

The Office of Study Abroad Programs supports the UNLV Mission Statement which affirms "Our commitment to the national and international communities ensures that our research and educational programs engage both traditional and innovative areas of study and global concerns. UNLV's distinctive identity and values permeate a unique institution that brings the best of the world to our region and, in turn, produces knowledge to improve the region and world around us."
summary: The Office of Study Abroad Programs is committed to the development of global perspectives and intercultural understanding through high-quality study abroad opportunities, faculty programs, and student programs that focus on international education.

name: UNLV Birding and Conservation Club
description:
Joining UNLV Birding and Conservation Club (Formally UNLV Audubon Student Conservation Chapter) is about great recreational and social opportunities. It’s also about making a difference on your campus and in your community. It’s about building your leadership skills, your knowledge, your resume, and your contact list. And it’s about having fun as you experience the wonder and joy of birds and nature.

Red Rock Audubon is the local club that supports UNLV Birding. All members are invited to attend Red Rock Audubon, check out their events here: https://www.redrockaudubon.com/calendar.html

The National Audubon Society is a nonprofit conservation organization that works across the Western Hemisphere to protect birds throughout their life cycles and all the special places they need along the way. Audubon’s work includes ambitious conservation and public policy programs, bird science leadership, and public engagement and outreach, all made possible by an unparalleled network of grassroots chapters, volunteers, advocates, and partner organizations.
summary: UNLV Birding events including birding in local areas, gardening to create bird and pollinator habitat, and community engagement.

name: Political Science Club
description: The Political Science Club is a non-partisan student organization open to students of all majors. We strive to promote civic engagement amongst the student body by providing a space to educate students and debate on current events and politics.
summary: The Political Science Club is a non-partisan student organization and open to students of all majors.

name: Student Organization of Latinxs
description:
The Student Organization of Latinxs (SOL) is a student organization created by and for Latinx students, their allies, and the UNLV campus community. SOL was founded on the belief that Latinx students at UNLV need a dedicated space and place for cultural representation on campus.

Our Mission

The Student Organization of Latinxs (SOL) was founded to create a supportive and unifying environment where Latinx students can learn, grow, and support each other. SOL is committed to equipping UNLV Latinx students with the essential tools to successfully navigate higher education while fostering a strong sense of community. Additionally, it strives to provide students with opportunities for both professional and personal development.

If you wish to learn more, please look below and connect with us on our social media!

UNLV SOL LinkTree

UNLV SOL Instagram

UNLV SOL Linkedin
summary: The Student Organization of Latinxs strives to educate, inform, and empower Latinx students to succeed in their UNLV careers while celebrating and sharing our diverse cultures.

name: UNLV Family Network
description: The UNLV Family Network is a support and networking program for parents & family members of UNLV students to connect with each other and the university through a variety of events and activities. You will receive a newsletter and learn about pathways to campus. When students transition to college, the Federal Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) takes effect. By federal law, all communication from the university must go directly through the student. This is an abrupt change from K-12 education, and parents may feel left out of the loop on key deadlines and paperwork. Family support is still a critical component of your student's success. With the UNLV Family Network, you'll have a resource to your answer questions and find information.
summary: Focused on providing resources to Parents and Family, the UNLV Family Network helps with Family Orientation, provides regular communication, and events throughout the year. This is where your family can build a connection to UNLV.

name: Disability Resource Center
description: The goal of the DRC is to provide students with effective accommodations based on law and current best practices, and to promote individual growth and self-determination. We are committed to supporting students with disabilities at UNLV through the appropriate use of advocacy, accommodations, and supportive services to ensure access to campus courses, services, and activities. Students can register with our office at www.unlv.edu/drc. Once they register they will need to submit disability documentation from a licensed provider. Our documentation review committee will review and determine eligibility and accommodations and if determined eligible, the student will meet with a disability specialist to discuss and activate accommodations.
summary: The DRC provides academic accommodations to students with documented disabilities

name: Aerospace Studies - UNLV Air Force ROTC
description:
The Air Force ROTC program provides Flying Rebels with excellent ways to jumpstart their careers. Some of these include: extensive academic funding, specialized military training, leadership development, fitness maintenance, professional development, and team-driven experiences. We are committed to ensuring that all who enter our doors will leave not only as a better individual, but as a different, daring, and diverse leader qualified to command some of the finest men in women in the world's greatest Air Force and Space Force.

 

About

AFROTC Detachment 004, University Nevada, Las Vegas, was established Aug. 2, 2004, with 54 enrolled students. Detachment 004 is the only AFROTC detachment in Nevada and covers Air Force Reserve Officer Training Corps recruiting for the entire state. Detachment 004 has commissioned numerous lieutenants since 2004 in various career fields, including cyberwarfare, intelligence, security forces, engineering, combat rescue, flying, space operations and more To enroll in the AFROTC program at UNLV, you must be a full-time student at CSN, NSC, or UNLV. AFROTC provides you the opportunity to become an officer in the U.S. Air Force or U.S. Space Force upon completion of your bachelor’s degree.

 

Mission

The mission of Air Force Reserve Officer Training Corps (AFROTC) is to develop quality leaders for the Air Force and the Space Force. As a cadet in Air Force ROTC, you'll be working toward a commission as a second lieutenant in the U.S. Air Force or U.S. Space Force. AFROTC focuses on teaching you the necessary leadership skills to become an Air Force or Space Force officer through a vigorous, multifaceted training program. As a freshman cadet, you will also attend AFROTC academic and leadership courses. These courses consist of:

- A weekly one-hour aerospace studies class where you'll learn about the Air Force and Space Force and how to become an effective leader.

- A weekly two-hour leadership laboratory for honing various military-specific skills and learning more about Air Force and Space Force leadership

- Two to three physical training sessions each week.

 

Scholarships

Various high school and in-college scholarships are available through AFROTC. Benefits include varying levels of tuition payment, a monthly nontaxable stipend based on college year, and an annual book stipend. Currently, freshman cadets receive $300, sophomores $350, juniors $450, and seniors $500. Freshman and sophomore cadets receive the stipend if they receive the AFROTC scholarship and become contracted. All cadets regardless of scholarship status receive the junior and senior stipends upon completion of field training and becoming contracted with the Air Force or the Space Force. For more information and to start the high school scholarship application process, please visit the U.S. Air Force ROTC website.

 

Base Visits

In addition to aerospace studies classes, leadership laboratory, and physical training, cadets can participate in base visits, Air Force ROTC summer programs, and additional cadet organizations. Base visits add diversity to the AFROTC program by allowing cadets to see how certain active duty units actually function.

 

Summer Programs

Summer programs focus on providing cadets additional training while giving cadets a glimpse of the active duty lifestyle. Alternatively, cadet organizations present more leadership opportunities and allow AFROTC students to plan, participate, and execute various service projects on and off campus. Some of these cadet organizations include:

- Honor Guard

- Arnold Air Society

The familiarity and knowledge gleaned from working in different organizations and participating in summer programs often gives cadets insight as to potential Air Force or Space Force career decisions. Although AFROTC is a very fun and exciting experience, it's important to remember that the focus of the program revolves around commissioning quality officers to lead the Air Force and Space Force.
summary: Our program teaches Flying Rebels the leadership skills required to excel as an officer in the United States Air Force or United States Space Force!

name: UNLV Medical Research Society
description:
Our current aims include:

  • Fostering the relationships between students and our research faculty
  • Coordinating opportunities to discuss up-to-date literature in medical research
  • Disseminating information on local and national research opportunities
  • Facilitating workshops focused on how to succeed in medical student research
summary: The UNLV Medical Research Society (MRS) at the School of Medicine was established to promote medical student research, identify local and national research opportunities, encourage community engagement, and provide professional resources to students.

name: Academic Success Center
description:
On a campus as big as UNLV, simply finding the right place to go for help can be challenging. Start at the Academic Success Center, and we'll guide you to the resources you need to be successful. The Academic Success Center will partner with the entire campus to both welcome and mentor students from pre-admission to first-year programs to a successful graduation. Students have full access to all of the Academic Success Center support resources including:

 

- Academic Advising, which is the home for Exploring Majors, undergraduate non-degree seeking students, and Major Pathways’ students. Our advising unit will help you plan your sequence of courses, provide career exploration, and declare a major!

 

- Academic Success Coaches partner with you to help you mature as a learner in the college environment by developing strategies for success. The collaborative effort focuses on soft skills that can be applied to the college experience as a whole. Some of the skills we focus on are Time Management, Goal Setting, Study Skills, and more!

 

- The Tutoring program provides content-learning support to students enrolled in historically difficult courses. Tutoring enables structured study time with peers, reviews of subject material and learning strategies to gain a deeper understanding of supported course content.

 

- Supplemental Instruction is a peer-led study group that partners with historically difficult courses and provides an interactive environment to assist you with course concepts and learning strategies.

 

- The Math Bridge Program is an online program designed to help students who are placed into preparatory math classes (Math 95/Math 96) or gateway math classes (Math 120/124/126) but require a different 100-level math course for their desired major. 

 

Academic Success Center video
summary: The Academic Success Center helps students do well academically and complete their studies on time. The ASC offers or will refer students to such programs and resources as tutoring, advising, skills testing, career exploration, and more.

name: Military & Veteran Services Center
description:
The Student Veteran & Military Family Services initiative was created in June 2009 to help UNLV's student veterans and active-duty military members successfully navigate the academic and administrative pathways of a college education. One of the primary outcomes of this initiative, aside from the birth of the Military & Veteran Services Center in 2012, is the partnering of several units on campus to provide simple, predictable administrative processes for student veterans and military family members. The members of this team are committed to outstanding support for this student population.

 

Our Programs

The Military & Veteran Services Center is staffed with veterans and VA Education Benefits-experienced staff to assist more than 1,800 veterans, dependents, active duty service members, National Guard members, and reservists. Our mission is to develop a welcoming, veteran-friendly campus environment that fosters academic and personal success. Our programs include:

- Nevada residency granted to all honorably discharged veterans within five years of separation from active duty/dependents within three years of their sponsor's discharge

- Priority registration for veterans/dependent VA education benefit users to improve the timeline for benefit payments

- Ranking as a Top 15% “Veteran-Friendly” University

- VetSuccess on Campus designation with a VA Benefits Counselor collocated within our office

- VA Yellow Ribbon program participation

- Nationally recognized Student Veterans of America chapter organization

- Veteran Career Fair with local employers ready to hire you

 

One Stop Shop

The Military and Veteran Services Center, located in the Student Services Complex A, Room 311, was created to provide you with all the support you'll need in one place. There, you'll find friendly faces, information, and transition support specifically tailored to your unique needs. As you begin to integrate into the UNLV community, be sure to get involved with the Student Veterans of America chapter on campus. In this group, you will find mentors and friends — all of whom have experienced military service. We hope you find a connection to UNLV's people and programs. If you find yourself to be missing that vital link, please contact us. We are here to help.
summary: We strive to provide responsive academic, social, and administrative support to student veterans, as well as active-duty military members and dependents using VA education benefits.

name: Writing Center
description:
The Writing Center is primarily a site for teaching and learning. We do not edit or proofread for writers. Instead, we consult with writers at any level of expertise to help them develop their own strategies for making informed choices about writing. Because writing is one of the chief ways we create, communicate, and critique knowledge, the Writing Center plays a central role in the life of the university. To support our community of learners, the Writing Center provides a variety of resources including synchronous and asynchronous consultations, writing workshops, and answers to quick questions. You can also visit our website to find resources for your formatting, style, and grammar needs.

 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fk0rRdKuefk&t=14s
summary: We work with writers in any discipline on any writing task (except for legal documents) to help them develop, improve, and transfer writing skills into their personal, academic, professional, and civic lives.

name: University Police Services, Southern Command
description:
University Police Services is a full-service police department responsible for providing police and security services to the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, College of Southern Nevada, Nevada State College, and Desert Research Institute, Las Vegas campuses and associated properties. We operate 24 hours a day, 365 days a year and are the providers and/or coordinators for all emergency and dispatch services on campus. Our commitment is to provide a safe and secure learning environment for our campuses by using a community-oriented policing model based on collaboration and professionalism. We also provide other services to the community, including fingerprinting, lost and found services, notary services, self-defense training, and student security escort services. We even give interested individuals the opportunity to participate in our ride-along program with an officer. Our department subscribes to the community policing philosophy, which guides our efforts in our interactions with our campus communities and the Las Vegas community in order to identify and solve problems.

 

To strengthen the ties and build a partnership with our faculty, staff, students, and residents, we encourage our community to participate in law enforcement events and engage with our officers. Our focus is on creating a safe educational and social environment through proactive policing and a commitment to community relations and outreach. Our department believes that education is the first step to prevention. Therefore, our staff personally give group lectures to students, faculty, and staff on a range of subjects, including the prevention of sexual assault, robbery, theft, and cybercrimes; active shooter safety; evening safety; and pedestrian safety.

Learn more at: www.updsouth.nevada.edu or by watching a short YouTube video about the department via tinyurl.com/y5b9ro5n
summary: UPD is the full-service police department for the UNLV, CSN, NSC, and DRI campuses.

name: UNLV Housing & Residential Life
description:
UNLV offers on-campus housing for traditional first-year students, all students seeking a traditional campus experience, and for students who meet the criteria for the First-Year Housing Requirement. UNLV is committed to filling the residence halls first, and to providing a first-year experience to students.

 

There are 4 residence hall complexes consisting of Dayton, Tonopah, South, and Upperclass Complexes. Residence hall living encourages students to develop friendships, join study groups and intramural teams, and develop and apply leadership skills. Residential life staff help residents set goals, make friends, resolve problems, and get connected in the community and around campus.

 

With a 24/7 on-site staff, utilities included, combined payment through your student account, and maintenance requests available online we worry about the details so students can focus on what matters most. The residence hall communities offer these helpful features:

 

- Attended lobby desks with extended hours

- Wireless Internet

- Premium cable TV included

- Computer labs

- Conference rooms

- Study/TV lounges and game rooms

- Free on-site laundry

- Steps from great dining options and markets
summary: Live in the heart of campus with a traditional residential experience in UNLV owned & operated residence halls.

name: Institute of Transportation Engineers
description:
ITE is an international educational and scientific association of transportation professionals who are responsible for meeting mobility and safety needs.

ITE facilitates the application of technology and scientific principles to research, planning, functional design, implementation, operation, policy development, and management for any mode of ground transportation.

The UNLV ITE student chapter aims to promote transportation knowledge of its members, helps them learn about the recent designs and technologies in the related fields, encourages education, and serves as a conduit for the exchange of professional information.
summary: The UNLV ITE student chapter promotes transportation knowledge of its members, helps them learn about the recent designs and technologies in the related fields, encourages education and exchange of professional information.

name: UNLV Comadres
description: UNLV Comadres is an interdisciplinary Latinx graduate student organization open to all graduate students who identify as Latinx mujeres and nonbinary Latinxs attending the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. UNLV Comadres are committed to creating space to share consejos, comunidad, and solidarity through collaborations, mentorship, and professional development opportunities that support the educational and professional aspirations of our academic familia.
summary: UNLV Comadres is an interdisciplinary Latinx graduate student organization open to all graduate students who identify as Latinx mujeres and nonbinary Latinxs attending the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.

name: UNLV Wine Society
description: Where our passion for wine is celebrated through educational sessions, curated tastings, and community engagement. Whether you're a curious beginner or a seasoned aficionado, our club offers a welcoming environment to explore the world.
summary: Wine can be daunting and confusing but with UNLV Hospitality's new club, The Wine Society, you can learn all about wine! Anything from the history of wine, how it's made, wine-tasting, and how to pair wine with food. Join us, as we endeavor wine!!

name: Student Occupational Therapy Association
description:
Our mission is to promote, advocate for, and elevate the profession of occupational therapy throughout the university and community by fostering leadership, service, scholarship, and professional engagement.

*This organization is open to all students who are interested in occupational therapy!*
summary:
To promote the profession of occupational therapy through out the university and community.

*This organization is for Graduate Students who are in the OTD program at UNLV.

name: The Care Center
description:
The Care Center offers confidential virtual support services, holistic healing workshops, campus education and awareness events to members of the UNLV, NSC, and CSN communities impacted by sexual violence, relationship violence, family violence, and/or stalking.

 

Advocacy & Case Management

The center operates from an anti-oppression framework to provide trauma-informed and healing-centered care, including one-to-one appointments and case management with a Care Advocate. The Care Advocates are available to hold space for our clients, offer emotional support, and help navigate the different options available to them on and off-campus. The Center also provides the Care Survivor Fund, which is an emergency fund available to our clients. Funding up to $1,000 is available for housing, food, off-campus therapy, and holistic healing services such as Reiki.  

 

Healing through Connections Programming

Due to the nature of our work, we recognize the impacts of trauma, people access healing in different ways, and that healing is an ongoing process. The Care Center offers Healing through Connections programming, including in-person and virtual workshops, outdoor retreats, and digital resources such as the Student’s Guide to Radical Healing zine. Regardless of where people are in their healing journey, the zine is a tool that people can access anywhere and in their own time. People can also find information about trauma, self-care, and healing via our social media accounts. We create and curate content that informs and validates the wide range of experiences of victim-survivors.  

 

Campus Education & Outreach

The Care Center strives to end various forms of power-based personal violence, and we do so by hosting campus-wide awareness engagements. We intend to create spaces where people can learn about the impacts of trauma related to interpersonal violence and partake in discussions that promote the needs and experiences of victim-survivors. 

 

If you’re interested in becoming a Care Advocate or to learn more about the Care Center’s services, programs, and events, visit our website unlv.edu/carecenter.
summary: The Care Center provides confidential advocacy services, case management, and holistic healing opportunities to students, faculty, and staff impacted by sexual assault, sexual harassment, relationship abuse, family violence, and/or stalking.

name: RebelSat
description:
Introduction

Welcome to RebelSat UNLV, a student-led initiative aimed at developing CubeSats for space-based research and development. What a Cubesat is, and why they are essential in the world of space exploration is detailed in the following.

  • CubeSats are small satellites that can perform specific functions at a relatively low cost.
  • They are fundamental to every university space program and play a significant role in advancing space research and technology.
  • We offer courses intended to further your understanding of CubeSat design and provide tips on best practices in this field
  • Real engineering projects are ongoing and allow members to get hands-on experience
  • Our program has had over 200 students from different majors working together, modeled after real engineering companies to provide the best experience possible for students.

Let's explore the RebelSat UNLV program in further detail and learn how you can be a part of this groundbreaking project.

 

Purpose

RebelSat was built around the students and pre-professional engineers who wish to join the Space/Satellite industry. Our club is tailored to those seeking experience and expertise in the complex world of satellite design, regardless of previous experience or degree. We offer experience to all areas, including non-STEM majors, within this industry. Some things you would learn and participate in are the following:

  • Creating an environment of learning for Engineering and Non-Engineering students
  • Develop the understanding and experience of interested individuals through offered courses and hands-on learning experiences
  • Provide opportunities to work on real and flight-worthy projects for both experience and an opportunity to participate in research
  • Provide leadership and professional development to aspiring students

Benefits to Membership

Joining RebelSat UNLV offers a unique opportunity to work on a groundbreaking project that has the potential to make a significant impact in the world of space exploration.

  • Gain hands-on experience in spacecraft design, testing, and operations, helping provide you with valuable skills for future career opportunities in the space industry.
  • Work alongside students from diverse majors and backgrounds, providing opportunities for collaboration as well as learning from a variety of perspectives.
  • Participate in all aspects of the project, from initial design to launch and mission operations, providing a well-rounded understanding of spacecraft development.
  • Modeled after real engineering companies and start-ups, the program provides an immersive and professional experience, preparing students for success in their future careers.
  • Develop new skills and gain a deeper understanding of the field of space engineering, promoting personal and academic growth.
  • Direct access to industry professionals from the likes of NASA, SpaceX, Lockheed Martin, etc.

 

We invite you to join RebelSat UNLV and be a part of the future of space exploration. Whether you are a student interested in engineering, science, business, or design, there is a place for you in our team. By joining RebelSat, you will gain hands-on experience in all aspects of the CubeSat development process and contribute to groundbreaking research in space technology. Plus, you will have the opportunity to network with industry professionals and gain skills that will benefit you in your future career. Don't miss out on this opportunity to be a part of something truly extraordinary. Email us at rebelsatunlv@gmail.com or join our discord here: https://discord.gg/FNTPm5e to get involved.

 

Website: https://www.rebelsat.com

Email: rebelsat@unlv.edu

Discord: https://discord.gg/FNTPm5e
summary: RebelSat UNLV's mission is to push the limits of CubeSat technology through student-led innovation, and to provide an accessible platform for space-based research and development in Southern Nevada.

name: UNLV Dining
description:
UNLV Dining is eating made easy on the UNLV campus. We offer a variety of dining options, treasures, and treats to all of our UNLV staff, students, and the open community. Our team of culinary and nutrition experts are dedicated to delivering well-balanced nutrition, specialized diet options, and complete menu transparency - because you have enough to worry about.

 

Have questions about our Dining Hall? On and off-campus meal plans? Dining Dollars? or maybe you just need to know what's open for your morning coffee run? Everything dining at UNLV is right here, made easy just for you. Study Hard. Eat Well, Rebels!
summary: UNLV Dining provides a range of delicious and healthy options with dining locations and meal plans that nourish your campus experience.

name: American Institute of Graphic Arts
description: UNLV's AIGA group consists of graphic design enthusiasts daring to take that first of many steps into the professional design world. We are a student chapter with AIGA Las Vegas meaning we have connections stretching across not only Las Vegas but across the nation. Our group aims to ensure that every member becomes well acquainted with the networks, skills, and professionals to land them the job of their dreams. We are steadfast planning to be active, fun, and engaging! Never a dull moment as we will have many student-led events from guest speakers, fundraisers, and contests.
summary: UNLV's very own student chapter with AIGA aims to connect the Graphic Design enthusiasts to professional organizations across Las Vegas! We gather, design, and create a future for all the future designers out there. Join us today!

name: Monarch Program
description:
The UNLV Monarch Program provides holistic, student-centered support for immigrant, mixed-status, DACA/TPS recipients, and others who identify as non-citizens. We are here to help students navigate the university, access resources, and build pathways toward academic and professional success.

 

Through individualized support and strategic referrals, we assist students facing complex personal, legal, financial, or systemic barriers that may impact their educational journey. Our team offers guidance grounded in care, confidentiality, and trust.

 

As part of The Intersection, the Monarch Program is committed to ensuring student success regardless of immigration status. We recognize how intersecting identities shape students’ experiences and remain dedicated to fostering belonging, empowerment, and opportunity across campus.
summary: The Monarch Program is committed to advancing equitable access to higher education by providing comprehensive support, meaningful resources, and increased visibility for students impacted by immigration.

name: NAMI On Campus
description:
Who is NAMI?

NAMI on Campus is part of the NAMI family, the nation’s largest grassroots mental health organization dedicated to building better lives for the millions of Americans affected by mental health conditions. NAMI advocates for access to services, treatment, supports and research and is steadfast in its commitment to raising awareness and building a community of hope for everyone.

What is NAMI on Campus?

Our club works to end stigma, to make it ok to seek help, to make sure everyone knows how to help a friend and to ensure our campus is a welcoming place for everyone.Whether you have a mental health condition or want to make a difference in the lives of others, join us as we create a positive, supportive environment for all students.

What do we do?

Our NAMI on Campus club hosts informative and fun events and works with other student and academic organizations on campus to promote mental wellness and reduce the stigma around mental health conditions. We raise awareness about mental health, its impact and add the student voice to the mental health movement.

Join our club and begin making a difference today! Work with us so that the campus community knows that mental health matters!

 

Follow our Instagram!

@Nami_at_UNLV

Join Our Discord!

https://discord.gg/Ffhc6NyP8V 

Email us!

nami@unlv.edu

 

Subscribe to our monthly newsletter!!

https://lp.constantcontactpages.com/su/JAGISOZ/UNLVNOC

 

Help us shape our meetings!

NOC Suggestions Form: https://forms.gle/qnxVa3mmUXL5FREu7

Share your story form: https://forms.gle/8G1NGWiYZMDZ9EWs9
summary: NAMI on Campus hosts events to promote mental wellness, raise awareness, and reduce the stigma around mental health conditions. Join us as we create a positive, supportive environment for all, and add the student voice to the mental health movement.

name: UNLV Medical Brigades
description: The mission of UNLV Medical Brigades is for aspiring professionals to build medical knowledge and cultural competency through skill-based volunteering, as they work to improve economic outcomes in under-resourced communities around the world.
summary: The mission of UNLV Medical Brigades is for aspiring professionals to build medical knowledge and cultural competency through skill-based volunteering, as they work to improve economic outcomes in under-resourced communities around the world.

name: Fostering Community
description: Have you experienced foster care or lived in kinship care? Fostering Community strives to support UNLV students with experience in foster care by bridging the gap between foster youth and resources, increasing awareness of the unique challenges that come with being a student who is a current/former foster youth, and engaging in advocacy efforts that advance the well-being and academic/career success of our youth.
summary: Fostering Community was created to support and connect students at UNLV who have experienced foster care. Our club seeks to build a sense of community, teach self-advocacy and leadership skills, and provide opportunities for civic engagement.

name: Latinx Graduate Student Association
description:
Mission

The University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV), Latinx Graduate Student Association (LGSA) mission is to provide the Latinx/a/o and Afrolatinx/a/o Graduate students with a comunidad . LGSA aims to provide a support system to Latinx/a/o and Afrolatinx/a/o Graduate students by providing mentorship, connections, and to assist with the needs of our community. We strive to make available resources for personal, professional, and academic success to the Latinx/a/o and Afrolatinx/a/o Graduate student body at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.
summary: As a community , the LGSA vision is to create a welcoming community to all Latinx and Afro-latinx graduate students, where they feel supported in their professional aspirations and engaged (included) in our community.

name: UNLV College of Fine Arts
description:
The College of Fine Arts, a creative nexus anchored within the vibrant and diverse culture of Las Vegas, boldly launches visionaries who transform the global community through collaboration, scholarship, and innovation. At UNLV we illuminate the power of the arts amidst breathtaking advancements in science and technology. In doing so, we are creating a global destination at the forefront of transforming arts and design. To accomplish this we encourage agency, inventiveness, problem-solving, and big-idea thinking in our students, faculty, and staff.

 

We make education relevant through curriculum and effective learning outcomes. We are passionate and compassionate, principled and supportive, joyful and kind. We believe in inclusion, invention, transformation, engagement, and rigor. These vital principles underpin all our efforts and guide us in fulfilling our mission.

 

We measure achievement of our mission through: Student success that outpaces our peer programs and institutions in recruitment, retention, graduation, and community impact; Creativity, scholarship, and learning accomplished by our students, alumni, and faculty whose impact is visionary and positive; Success in hiring and retaining nationally and internationally renowned faculty; Collaboration amongst our faculty, across our diverse programs, throughout UNLV and with the diverse communities we serve in Las Vegas and globally; and Recognition within the UNLV community and beyond for our efficacy, passion, principle, compassion, and joy.

 

https://youtu.be/Uw4CesyH5Ws
summary: Boldly launching visionaries who transform the global community through collaboration, scholarship, and innovation.

name: College of Sciences Advising Center
description:
The College of Sciences Advising Center seeks to provide academic guidance and support that will empower students to achieve their personal and professional goals.

Scheduling an Appointment

All admitted students in the College of Sciences are encouraged to meet with an advisor once each semester. Prospective students are encouraged to email the advising center for assistance. Students may schedule an appointment by calling the front office at 702-895-2077 or by self-scheduling online. All scheduled appointments will be completed using one of the following formats:

  • Virtual appointments through Zoom (screen and attachment sharing are available, and a camera is optional)
  • Phone appointments through your preferred phone number
  • In-person appointments at the advising center (MPE-A, Room 130)

Your advisor will contact you prior to your scheduled appointment to provide you with instructions on how to access your selected appointment format. Appointments may be scheduled for 30 minutes or 1 hour increments depending on the complexity of services requested. Students may request to meet with a specific advisor, or schedule with the soonest available advisor. Active students who have earned less than 30 credits will be encouraged to meet with the sciences First-Year Advisor.

Reasons to Schedule an Appointment

Some reasons to meet with an advisor are described below.

  • Discuss course requirements, sequencing, and prerequisites
  • Verify that your courses are applicable to your degree plan
  • Make changes to your schedule or graduation timeline based personal circumstances or preferences
  • Discuss applying for reinstatement 
  • Discuss the graduation process
  • Declare a science major or minor
  • Complete financial appeals, petitions, excess credit, veteran, and other miscellaneous forms
  • Discuss how your degree plan relates to your career goals
  • Discuss withdrawing from one or more courses
  • Review transfer credits or request an equivalency review
  • Learn about academic resources and involvement opportunities at UNLV
  • Learn about program, department, college, or university policies
  • Provide feedback about your experiences at UNLV

Call-Back Hours

Students who urgently need to meet with an advisor may request a return phone call during the following times: Monday through Thursday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. and Friday from 8 a.m. to 12 p.m. An advisor will contact you via phone on the same or next business day depending on your availability and time preferences. Call-backs are designed for quick questions and these calls run for approximately 10 minutes.

Email Assistance

Additional assistance for quick questions can be obtained via email. Please allow up to 2 business days for an email response (excluding weekends & holidays). Emails received from the sciences advising email account will be filtered and distributed to an available advisor. You may also directly reach out to the most recent advisor you have met. To expedite email responses, please ensure your email is sent through your Rebel Mail account. Detailed questions involving complex course planning or paperwork processes may be referred to a full advising appointment at the discretion of the responding advisor.
summary: The College of Sciences Advising Center seeks to provide academic guidance and support that will empower students to achieve their personal and professional goals.

name: Division of Health Sciences Advising Center
description: Welcome to the Division of Health Sciences Advising Center. Admitted undergraduate students should schedule meetings with their advisors once or twice each semester to review needed classes, discuss schedules, and evaluate plans for timely graduations.
summary: The Division of Health Sciences Advising Center provides academic guidance to all undergraduate students within the School of Integrated Health Sciences, the School of Public Health, and the School of Nursing.

name: College of Education Student Services Center
description:
The Education Student Services Center (ESSC) is your one-stop shop to success. We provide academic advising, career development and student success services, and field placement support for Education & Human Services majors. Our goal is to help you become the best Rebel teacher or human services practitioner that you can be! We share a commitment to our students’ personal, academic, and professional development by:

 

1) Amplifying student voices and honoring their experiences by creating a culture of respect for diversity.

2) Cultivating opportunities for students to build meaningful personal and professional networks and relationships by establishing and maintaining strong community partnerships.

3) Promoting students’ academic success through effective retention and progression initiatives, and timely completion through developmental and appreciative academic advising.

4) Committing to students’ personal and professional growth and development to help ensure well-rounded future educators and human services professionals.
summary: The ESSC provides academic advising, career development and student success services, and field placement support for Education & Human Services majors.

name: Lee Business School
description:
Our Mission

Our mission is also our ethos: we cultivate leaders who transform business. While our teaching is the most evident means through which we accomplish this mission, our research and service are also foundational to our teaching and our ability to foster such transformational change. Through our teaching, research and service, we transform the lives of our students and the communities we serve— Las Vegas, the state of Nevada, the United States and the world.

 

Accreditation

The Lee Business School is among just 181 business colleges and schools worldwide to hold international dual accreditation in business and accounting by The Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business.

 

Faculty

With about 85 faculty members, the school successfully recruits talented professionals from around the world, adding cultural richness and intellectual stimulation to the education experience.

 

Lee Business School Learning Objectives

Students at the undergraduate level will be able to demonstrate knowledge of core business concepts. Students at the undergraduate level will know how to communicate effectively in a business setting. Students at the undergraduate level will work effectively in a team environment. Students at the undergraduate level will recognize and analyze ethical and legal dimensions of business decisions and identify the probable outcomes and effects on stakeholders. Students at the undergraduate level will identify global and cultural issues that affect how a firm operates and describe how these issues affect business decisions.

 

Students

About 4,000 students, including 500 graduate students, call the Lee Business School home. We offer more than $350,000 of annual scholarship support for our talented students.

 

https://youtu.be/pOLGeMPrrYk
summary: At Lee Business School, we cultivate leaders who transform business in today's dynamic marketplace through skill development and experiential learning.

name: UNLV Summer Term
description: Earn your college credits quicker! Summer courses have the same academic content as Fall and Spring but move at a much faster pace. Many students enjoy the structure of attending courses five days a week for three to five weeks. This format enables students to immerse themselves in the subject material and advance their progress in obtaining their degree. Contact Summer Term with any questions: summerterm.unlv.edu (chat and video appointments), summerterm@unlv.edu, (702) 895-3711.
summary: Additional opportunities to earn college credit allows students to reap the benefits of attaining up to an additional eighteen credits for undergraduate students, and twelve credits for graduate students, ultimately fast-tracking their degree.

name: SFC Campus-Based
description: We are a student organization that provides a safe place for students to grow in their faith and to further the idea that God and the college experience can be one. Our goal is to make lifelong friendships and connections as we journey together in faith and learn about the Catholic faith. This is done through weekly hangouts, workshops, volunteer opportunities, and monthly kickbacks. No religious or Catholic background necessary! All are welcome!
summary: SFC-Campus Based provides a safe place for students to grow in their faith! Our goal is to make lifelong friendships and connections as we journey together. No religious or Catholic background necessary. All are welcome!

name: Office of Information Technology

name: From the Brush Arts and Literary Journal
description:
The From the Brush Arts and Literary Journal is a student-led organization established by UNLV's Honors College. Our mission is to provide an enriching outlet for honors students nationwide to have their creative works professionally recognized. The creative works published include fiction, nonfiction, visual media, and multimedia from students in a recognized National Collegiate Honors Council program. 

Our team is open to any interested students of the UNLV Honors College regardless of previous experiences in publishing or creative arts. Positions include executive editor positions, web and design managers, marketing managers, genre editors, copy editors, and an event coordinator.

description:
The UNLV Amateur Radio Club seeks to advance the amateur radio hobby by assisting students with attaining and upgrading licenses, learn operating procedures, establish an on-campus club station, participate in countless amateur radio activities, and more!

name: Frame by Frame: Animation Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a community for individuals who wish to immerse themselves in the art form of animation. Whether it be through the study and appreciation of the art form or the active creation of animated works, we embrace the beauty of animation. All are welcome, regardless of experience or knowledge of animation. If you’d like to watch some cool animated films, network with others interested in animation, or just learn something new, we are the RSO to join!
summary: We are an organization that intends to explore the intricacies of creating and analyzing all forms of animation.

'AAS Explained'
