name: College Republicans
description:
Forging leaders since 2022.

We identify, recruit, and train Las Vegas’ young professionals to become the next generation of public servants, business leaders, and elected officials.

The bench for the future of Nevada’s conservative movement starts with UNLV College Republicans, the Heart of UNLV’s Liberty.

We’re built by Rebels, for Rebels

UNLV College Republicans was founded with three goals:

1. Fostering Community: As a registered student organization, we host regular events for both College Republicans, prospective members of our RSO, and other community leaders to attend and network. We’re here to have fun, make friends, and develop ourselves as young professionals.

2. Professional Development: We host regular professional development and political training workshops, as well as special guest speakers. We also host a job board for our members to connect with prospective internships and employment opportunities for our members and alumni.

3. Winning Elections: Political parties, first and foremost, are organized to cultivate the grassroots to support common political efforts. UNLV College Republicans mobilizes our members to go into the community during election season and inspire our fellow students and neighbors to support quality Republican candidates by knocking doors, making calls, and helping with political events.
summary:
The official College Republicans chapter at UNLV, forging leaders since 2022.

We foster the moral and patriotic leaders of tomorrow at UNLV, one student at a time.

name: The Literate Earth Project at UNLV
description:
Our Mission

The Literate Earth Project (LEP) is a non-profit organization aimed to promote literacy and provide access to educational resources to underprivileged communities in both our local community and around the world. As a student-led chapter at UNLV, we aim to support LEP’s mission to make a positive impact on the lives of those who lack the resources and opportunities that many of us take for granted and lead a valuable impact by improving literacy within our communities and spreading awareness to others about the importance of equitable education.

How We Achieve Our Mission

BOOK DRIVES

The Literate Earth Project at UNLV collaborates with local community organizations to host various community projects and outreach efforts such as organizing book drives to collect books to fill libraries in underserved countries like Uganda and local underserved communities. For the Fall 2024-Spring 2025 term, our chapter aims to collect 3,000 books.

FUNDRAISERS

Through partnerships with local businesses and other student-led fundraising events, we seek to raise money to grow and fund the mission of The Literate Earth Project which goes to support building libraries in Uganda. Our goal is to raise $6,000 this year for our global literacy initiative.

Local Community Outreach

UNLV/CSUN PRESCHOOL

Our organization provides impactful service-based experiential opportunities by reading to the UNLV/CSUN Preschool. This opportunity promotes the values of our mission by fostering a meaningful connection between our members and the children that they read to. We seek to foster inclusive literacy experiences that both our members and those within our community hope to impact. 

LAS VEGAS RESCUE MISSION AND LIGHTHOUSE CHARITIES 

We seek to also provide inclusive opportunities to promote literacy in underserved communities in Las Vegas by donating books and educational resources to community partners such as the Las Vegas Rescue Mission and Lighthouse Charities
summary: The Literate Earth Project (LEP) is a non-profit organization that aims to promote literacy and provide access to educational resources to underprivileged communities around the world. We collect books and raise money to build libraries in Uganda.

name: Pre-Law Women of UNLV
description: The Pre-Law Women of UNLV serves as a community of womxn pursuing a career in the legal profession. PLW offers support, career development, networking, and encouragement for all types of womxn looking to connect with other legal-minded womxn. Our organization aims to encourage every womxn's confidence, assist in their professional development, offer resources for the pre law track, and serve as a platform for womxn to discuss issues that only affect womxn in law.
summary: The Pre-Law Women of UNLV serves as a community of women pursuing a career in the legal profession. PLW offers support, career development, networking, and encouragement for all types of women looking to connect with other legal-minded women.

name: LEE Business Connections
description:
LEE Business Connections is a club for freshman business students to connect with each other and make UNLV their home through fun and social activities/events.

Our goal is to introduce students to other members of the UNLV community and share information about opportunities based on their goals and interests. Ultimately, we want to provide a welcoming environment for students to kick start their college experience.
summary: A place for incoming and current Lee Business students to connect with one another.

name: UNLV Car Club
description: The purpose of the UNLV Car Club is to allow newcomers and long-time car enthusiasts a safe space to experience car culture. This club is meant to integrate all types of car culture and allow students to ask questions or share concerns without judgment.
summary: The purpose of the UNLV Car Club is to allow newcomers and long-time car enthusiasts a safe space to experience car culture. This club is meant to integrate all types of car culture and allow students to ask questions or share concerns without judgment.

name: African Student Alliance
description:
The African Students Alliance (ASA) at the University of Las Vegas is a student organization that aims to create a supportive and inclusive community for African students on campus. ASA provides a platform for African students to connect, share experiences, and promote African culture and heritage.

 

The alliance organizes various events and activities throughout the year to foster a sense of belonging and cultural exchange. These events may include cultural festivals, educational workshops, guest speakers, and social gatherings. ASA also collaborates with other student organizations and the university to promote diversity and inclusion within the campus community. ASA serves as a resource hub for African students, offering support and guidance on academic, social, and cultural matters. It provides a network of fellow students who can offer advice and assistance, especially to those who are new to the university or the United States.

 

By organizing events and promoting African culture, the ASA aims to raise awareness and appreciation for the diverse cultures and traditions of the African continent among the university community. The alliance seeks to create a welcoming environment where African students can thrive academically and socially while celebrating their cultural heritage.

Objectives:

  • Promote cultural exchange and understanding among students from diverse backgrounds. Through cultural festivals, workshops, and events, the association aims to provide a platform for African students to share their rich heritage, traditions, and experiences with the broader university community. By fostering cultural exchange, the ASA helps to break down stereotypes, promote diversity, and create a more inclusive campus environment.
  • Provide Support and Resources: The ASA aims to provide a strong support system for African students at the University of Las Vegas. It strives to assist students in their academic and personal journeys by offering guidance, mentorship, and resources. The association may organize workshops on topics such as time management, study skills, and career development, helping students navigate the challenges they may face during their university years. By providing a support network, the ASA helps African students thrive academically and socially.
  • Advocate for African Students: The African Students Alliance acts as an advocate for the needs and interests of African students on campus. The association works closely with the university administration to address issues that may affect African students' educational experience, such as access to resources, cultural sensitivity, and representation. The ASA may organize discussions, forums, and awareness campaigns to raise awareness about the challenges and contributions of African students, fostering a more inclusive and equitable campus environment.

Membership Benefits:

  • Exposure and access to other minority scholars, administrators, and professionals at UNLV and Las Vegas
  • Tools that can aid graduates and professionals in their studies and degree completion
  • Joining a network of scholars of African descent who are dedicated to serving the larger Black community
summary: The African Students Alliance (ASA) at the University of Las Vegas is a student organization that aims to create a supportive and inclusive community for African students on campus.

name: Origami Club
description: We teach the student body how to fold origami to foster patience and problem-solving. For more information, join our discord and follow our Instagram @OrigamiClubUNLV!
summary: Teaching the student body of UNLV the art of origami.

name: Rebel Start-Up
description: RSU aims to provide opportunities for students to share their entrepreneurial ideas and convert them into reality through mentorship, networking, collaboration, competition, and experience.
summary: RSU aims to provide opportunities for students to share their entrepreneurial ideas and convert them into reality through mentorship, networking, collaboration, competition, and experience.

name: A4 Zine Club
description:
What is a zine? A zine is essentially a DIY, self published magazine. Zines make use of different art styles and mediums, often featuring collages, essays, poetry, photographs, etc. Zines can be on any topic!

The A4 Zine Club hopes to educate people about the world of zines, help people create their zines, and be a supportive and inclusive community for zine lovers. We'll get together once a week to work on zines and host other zine related events as well.

Part of our club's mission is to help the Las Vegas Zine Library, hosted on campus at the Barrick Museum of Art, organize and archive the library to make it more accessible for the public. Members will have the opportunity to help with that project if they wish.
summary: The A4 Zine Club is a club where people can come together to create zines and support other zine creators.

name: Kirk Kerkorian Sports Club
description: As an organization, we aim to have students from the school of medicine engage in athletic activities in order to build closer relationship as well as enrich the medical school experience through stress-relieving athletic activities
summary: As an organization, we aim to have students from the school of medicine engage in athletic activities in order to build closer relationship as well as enrich the medical school experience through stress-relieving athletic activities.

summary: Mariachi Rebelde is an uprising student organization with the goal to unite students with a common interest in mariachi and introduce many others at UNLV with the extravagant culture that Mexico's pride and passion of music has to offer.

name: ReSpectacle
description:
Respectacle is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization that uses the power of the Internet to facilitate redistribution of quality, used eyeglasses to underserved communities worldwide.

The purposes of this organization shall be:

  • To facilitate glasses donations and ship them to ReSpectacle to be distributed to those in need
  • To assist in fundraising for ReSpectacle
summary: Respectacle is a nonprofit organization that uses the power of the Internet to facilitate redistribution of quality, used eyeglasses to underserved communities worldwide.

name: Nevada Youth Activist Alliance
description: NVYAA is a youth-led organization--partnered with the national non-profit, Advocates For Youth--that fights for reproductive justice on a local level here in Nevada.
summary: Associated with the non-profit, Advocates For Youth, the Nevada Youth Activist Alliance is a youth-led group that organizes young people to enact reproductive justice oriented policy in Nevada.

name: UNLV Badminton Club
description: Link to Discord
summary: For people who want to play badminton! Open to people of all backgrounds and all skill levels. Come join!

name: Associated General Contractors - Student Chapter
description: AGC is a place for construction management students, engineering students, and others interested in the construction industry to connect with each other and industry professionals. AGC is an organization with the goal of making our members more employable by providing guest speakers, job site tours, trainings, & participating in competitions. It also serves as a hub to find internships & scholarships. We also aim to make it a fun environment where you can make friends & socialize.
summary: Our student chapter serves as the hub for students to connect, learn, and collaborate with industry experts. We aim to bridge the gab between academic theory and practical experiences preparing our students for a successful career in construction.

name: Order of Omega
summary: The Order of Omega is an undergraduate Leadership Society.

name: Rebels R.I.S.E.
description:
At UNLV, we know that the college journey doesn’t stop after your first year. Each step—whether you’re a sophomore, junior, or senior—comes with new opportunities, challenges, and transitions. That’s where Rebels R.I.S.E. comes in!

Rebels R.I.S.E. is here to support you as you continue to grow personally, academically, and professionally. Our program is designed to help you explore majors and career paths, connect with faculty, staff, advisors, and peers, and discover the many resources available to help you succeed.

Through Rebels R.I.S.E., you’ll have opportunities to reflect on your strengths, set and accomplish meaningful goals, and envision the future you want to create. Most importantly, you’ll join a community of Rebels who are actively engaging on campus, pursuing scholarly opportunities, and making an impact in the Las Vegas community.

We’re excited to walk alongside you as you continue to R.I.S.E. throughout your UNLV journey!
summary: The Rebels R.I.S.E. scholar program provides annual cohorts of UNLV undergraduate students with opportunities to encourage meaningful learning experiences for students during their time at UNLV.

name: UNLV Campus Community Garden
description:
The UNLV Campus Community Garden is a vibrant space dedicated to growth and education, teaching individuals how to cultivate their own food using 100% organic gardening practices. The garden features 41 raised garden plots that are available for registration every fall semester. To become a UNLV Garden Member, groups of at least four members are required (limited to UNLV students, RSOs, staff, and faculty). UNLV members who become a part of the community garden have access to our communal outdoor composters, a free seed library, free communal garden tools, a communal herb garden, their own garden plot, and several fruit trees.

Solo gardeners can join the Rebel Roots Garden Club, a Registered Student Organization, which shares four garden beds among its members. Even if gardening isn't your passion, our volunteer days and events are always open to the public.

The UNLV Community Garden is located next to the UNLV Rebel Recycling Center right by parking lots: Lot T or Lot R. The best way to get to the garden is to travel east on Flamingo and take the first right before Claymont St. The entrance is by the Stan Fulton Building Parking Lot and next to the International Gaming Institute.
summary: The UNLV Campus Community Garden is a place of growing and learning, by teaching others how to grow their own food through 100% organic gardening practices.

name: UNLV Film Department
description:
Welcome to the Bachelor of Arts in Film - In UNLV's dynamic undergraduate film program, students learn the foundations of visual storytelling and critical skills that can be applied to any field. Our students are challenged with innovative training in directing; signature screenwriting approaches; the latest cinematography techniques; cutting-edge nonlinear solutions; and interactive courses in film history, theory, and criticism.

 

The Department of Film offers students the exciting opportunity to study film in the heart of Las Vegas, “The Entertainment Capital of the World.” Our efforts contribute to UNLV’s status as a premier metropolitan research university by adapting traditional film education values to meet the needs of individuals, communities, and industries in the 21st century. Once required courses are complete, students can select track electives to specialize in the area of their choice and prepare themselves to pursue careers as cinematographers, critics, directors, editors, historians, screen actors, producers, screenwriters, sound editors or designers, production designers, costume designers, or professional crew. The program also equips students for entry into top-tier graduate programs in production or research. (Total credits = 120-122)

 

Welcome to the MFA in Writing for Dramatic Media - Our Master of Fine Arts program takes a “conservatory” approach to screenwriting that is based on practice and repetition. As a student in the program, you will become closely acquainted with the process of pitching a story before writing it, employing critical and analytical approaches to filmmaking and screenwriting, and exploring multiple areas of screenwriting, including:

- Feature screenplays

- Short screenplays

- Television sitcoms

- Television dramas

- Telenovelas

- Sketch comedy

- Webisodes

- Mobisodes

Throughout the three-year program, you will work closely with expert faculty and a variety of guests from the industry as you create a significant body of feature motion picture and television scripts and hone them to the sharpest professional sensibility.
summary: Our degree programs combine the latest industry practices, traditional storytelling, and a scholarly film history context to help you build a strong foundation for graduate study and careers in the dynamic worlds of film, television, and digital media.

name: UNLV Vinyl Record Club
description: If you love music, you've probably noticed the comeback of vinyl records in recent years-  from casual fans that own one or two of their favorite albums on vinyl to hardcore collectors, this club is a great way to connect with other fans of the popular physical medium for music. Even if you haven't started a collection yet and are just interested in learning about records, you're welcome to join!
summary: A club for vinyl record fans and collectors, where we can listen to, talk about and share our favorite records.

name: Collegiate National Association for Music Educators
description: A collegiate music education organization that provides professional development, promotes advocacy and serves the community.  Any music education major can join. Dues will be minimal, based on the collegiate memberships. These will include membership to American Choral Directors Association (ACDA), National Association for Music Education (NAfME), Las Vegas Chapter of Kodaly Educators (LUCKE) and the Nevada Desert Valley Chapter (NDVC) of the American Orff Schulwerk Association (AOSA)
summary: A collegiate music education organization that provides professional development, promotes advocacy and serves the community.  Any music education major can join.

name: University of Nevada Las Vegas Powerlifting Club
description: The purpose of the UNLV Powerlifting Club is to promote strength training and competitive powerlifting at the highest level of collegiate competition. Regardless of powerlifting experience, people of any strength and background are welcome to join in.
summary: The purpose of the UNLV Powerlifting Club is to promote strength training and competitive powerlifting.

name: Color Business Association
description: The Color Business Association (CBA) at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, founded in 2022, is dedicated to connecting a diverse group of students to the corporate world through personalized professional development, workshops, and networking events. Emphasizing diversity, equity, and inclusion, the CBA prepares students for success in internships and jobs, empowering them to thrive in their careers.
summary: The Color Business Association (CBA) at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, founded in 2022, is dedicated to connecting a diverse group of students to the corporate world through personalized professional development, workshops, and networking events.

name: Asian Pacific Islander Alliance
description: Our mission is to create a supportive and welcoming community for all Asian and Pacific Islander graduate students at UNLV. We aim to spread awareness and build stronger representation of the diverse cultures in our group. Our focus is to empower students to share their stories and experiences to combat racial discrimination, invisibility, and biases perpetuated among the Asian and Pacific Islander community. In collaboration within the UNLV and Las Vegas communities, we aim to foster a service learning model by providing mentorship and support for the wellness of all students.
summary: The APIA group aims to create a supportive and welcoming community for all Asian and Pacific Islander graduate students at UNLV. We aim to spread awareness and build stronger representation of the diverse cultures in our group.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
description:
Only Boyd law students are eligible to join APALSA. All other students, including undergraduate students, will have their request to join denied.

The Asian Pacific American Law Student Association (APALSA) strives to promote Asian Pacific American topics and to contribute to the multicultural awareness at the William S. Boyd School of Law. APALSA is concerned with connecting Asian Pacific American law students with other professional organizations and the Asian Pacific American community. APALSA plans on organizing events such as guest speaker series, community service projects, cultural events, and professional and social opportunities to meet fellow Boyd students or practicing attorneys. APALSA membership is open to all interested law students and members are encouraged to become leaders and active participants in community issues.
summary: The Asian Pacific American Law Student Association (APALSA) strives to promote Asian Pacific American topics and to contribute to the multicultural awareness at the William S. Boyd School of Law.

name: UNLV Lee Business School Career and Professional Development Office
description: The Career and Professional Development Office in LEE works with undergraduate and graduate business students to prepare them to pursue their career goals. Students receive assistance and guidance to successfully pursue internships and full-time employment and are provided opportunities to connect with employers. It is our goal to prepare LEE students to find a job after graduation by providing a wide variety of career resources and events, and by delivering effective, individualized career assistance.
summary: We work directly with employers to help students network and find jobs or internships in their field.

name: Japanese Students Alliance
description:
The Japanese Student Alliance (JSA) is a student organization that welcomes all to learn and experience the history, tradition, and significance of Japanese culture.

JSAはUNLVの様々な学生が日本の歴史や伝統を学んだり、多用多種の日本人・日系人と交流できる場を目指している部活・或いはサークルです。一応、部員へ仕事の紹介も行ってます。

興味のある方は是非ご連絡ください。
summary:
The Japanese Student Alliance (JSA) is a cultural student organization that helps build strong relationships between Japanese students and non-Japanese students through language, culture and heritage. 
興味のある方は是非ご連絡ください。

name: Public Interest Law Association
description: PILA encourages law students to be involved with public interest work during law school and later as attorneys. We hope to influence the legal community to bring about meaningful change to meet the needs of the community through pro bono work. We also raise scholarship funds to support law students wishing to work in public interest over the summer.
summary: William S. Boyd School of Law Public Interest Law Association (PILA) is a non-profit, student-run organization that works closely with students, faculty, and members of the community to raise awareness about public interest work and opportunities

name: Rebel Trading Card Game Club
description:
The Rebel Trading Card Game Club is a friendly space to play and learn how to play different TCG games!

Times and locations for meetings may vary so please check our discord server for updates.

Our most to least played games and formats are:

- Magic the Gathering (Standard, Pauper, Pioneer, Modern, Commander/EDH)

- Yu-Gi-Oh!

- Pokémon (Standard)

- Weiß Schwarz

We welcome different games as well! 

Whether you're a newer or older player, feel free to come by and play a few games. We have plenty of decks of different power levels for anyone to use. Hope to see you there!

UNLV Address: 4505 S Maryland Pkwy, Las Vegas, NV 89154
summary: A welcoming and exciting atmosphere for all who are interested in playing and learning TCGs!

name: Council for Exceptional Children, University of Nevada, Las Vegas Chapter
description: The UNLV Chapter of the Council for Exceptional Children (CEC) is the premier organization for special education in Nevada. Headquartered within UNLV’s Department of Early Childhood, Multilingual, and Special Education, we mirror the international CEC mission: ensuring that children and youth with disabilities and gifts receive the support necessary to achieve their full potential. We are dedicated to bridging the gap between theory and practice through consistent professional development. By championing evidence-based strategies including culturally responsive teaching and inclusive practices, we challenge our members to provide the highest standard of support. Beyond the classroom, we serve as a vital community hub, providing essential resources and service opportunities for families and individuals with disabilities throughout the region. At our core, we serve as a professional incubator for teachers-in-training. By supplementing university instruction with specialized resources and networking, we develop seasoned professionals ready to provide immediate value to their schools. We invite all current and future educators to join us in our mission to empower the exceptional education community.
summary: Council for Exceptional Children is a dedicated student community advancing the success of individuals with disabilities & gifted talents. We equip current and future educators to lead through advocacy and networking.

name: Girl Gains UNLV
description:
Our mission is to promote female fitness, nutrition, healthy habits and empower women to feel strong and beautiful in a judgment-free community. Whether you’re a dedicated gym goer or a curious beginner, Girl Gains is a college campus-originated organization and an evolving online community here to support you in every stage of your fitness journey.

 

Join now: 

Discord Server

Instagram
summary: A global movement, inspiring women to dominate in and out of the fitness world.

name: Boyd Criminal Law Society
summary: The purpose of the Criminal Law Society is to provide legal information to the William S. Boyd School of Law community on pertinent issues in the field of criminal law. Additionally, to open a constructive discourse regarding current criminal law issues.

name: Food Tasting Club
description: Are you ready to eat and give back? In the Food Tasting Club, students at UNLV come together not only to explore various food items at restaurants in Las Vegas but also to make a meaningful impact on the community. We schedule weekly volunteer opportunities at food banks, shelters, and other places in need to combat food insecurity in Las Vegas. Additionally, we host biweekly events to dine out together and immerse ourselves in the local culinary scene. Monthly or bimonthly, we organize cultural events where we bring cultural restaurants to UNLV, complemented by speakers who provide a rich historical background. All students at UNLV are welcome to join and participate in the immersion of culture and location through the art of trying food. Our mission is to network, have fun, give back to the community, and deepen our appreciation of the art of being a connoisseur.
summary: The Food Tasting Club aims to create an inviting space for students to engage with UNLV, offering a platform for them to try an array of international cuisines, support local small businesses, and make meaningful connections with fellow students.

name: Environmental Law Society
description: The Environmental Law Society is a supportive community for law students interested in protecting the environment and pursuing careers in environmental & natural resources law. ELS strives to raise awareness about current environmental law and policy issues, facilitate conversations about environmental problem-solving on the state and national level, and provide members with pro bono research opportunities to address environmental justice issues affecting Nevada communities. Through community events—such as organized hikes and park clean-ups—ELS encourages law students to destress, connect with the outdoors, and foster a deeper appreciation for Nevada’s bountiful public lands.
summary: The Environmental Law Society is a supportive community for law students interested in protecting the environment and pursuing careers in environmental & natural resources law. ELS raises awareness about current environmental law and policy issues.

name: Dental Student Government Association
summary: The Dental Student Government Association (DSGA) was founded for the purpose of providing a collective voice to address the concerns and desires, to enhance the social activities, and achieve academic goals of the students of the UNLV SDM.

name: National Lawyers Guild
description:
Established in 1937, the National Lawyers Guild (NLG) is the nation’s oldest and largest progressive bar association and was the first one in the US to be racially integrated. Our mission is to use law for the people, uniting lawyers, law students, legal workers, and jailhouse lawyers to function as an effective force in the service of the people by valuing human rights and the rights of ecosystems over property interests. This is achieved through the work of our members, and the Guild’s numerous organizational committees, caucuses and projects, reflecting a wide spectrum of intersectional issues. Guild members effectively network and hone their legal skills in order to help create change at the local, regional, national, and international levels.

The NLG is dedicated to the need for basic change in the structure of our political and economic system. Our aim is to bring together all those who recognize the importance of safeguarding and extending the rights of workers, women, LGBTQ people, farmers, people with disabilities and people of color, upon whom the welfare of the entire nation depends; who seek actively to eliminate racism; who work to maintain and protect our civil rights and liberties in the face of persistent attacks upon them; and who look upon the law as an instrument for the protection of the people, rather than for their repression. The Boyd NLG chapter works with the Las Vegas NLG chapter of attorneys to effect legal change in the Las Vegas community.
summary: Established in 1937, the National Lawyers Guild (NLG) is the nation’s oldest and largest progressive bar association and was the first one in the US to be racially integrated. We are a radical, anticarceral coalition of attorneys and law students.

name: Romanian Student Association
summary: Romanian Student Association is bringing together the Romanian students of UNLV and anyone who wants to learn more about Romanian heritage and culture. RSA hopes to create a fun and social community for the Romanian culture to be shared.

name: ISACA Student Group
description: The goal of the ISACA Student Group (ISG) is to provide guidance and opportunities to students who are preparing for a career with a leadership role in all industries reliant upon and supported by information systems in such topics as cybersecurity, IT audit and risk.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to expand recognition of the Audit, Risk, Governance, Assurance and Security disciplines by extending education beyond the classroom in the areas of standards, practices and certification.

name: Jewish Law Student Association
summary: The Jewish Law Students Association seeks to provide a space for Jewish law students through networking, religion-based events, and activities. JLSA aims to facilitate a robust Jewish community at Boyd, to mutually benefit our students and the community.

name: UNLV Project Wellness
description:
Hello! We are Project Wellness, A student-led mental health initiative aimed to improve general mental wellness of the student body and allow our students to be the voice for how we as a university can promote student wellness and mental health services. 

Our organization has no requirements to join and no requirements to stay in the organization either. Project Wellness has Four committees, a Mental Health Committee, a Fitness Committee, a self-discovery committee, and an International/Transfer Student Committee!

For any information on how to join these committees, please join our Discord which is through our Linktree. Please follow us on Instagram and read our Newsletter! For any questions, please email projectwellnessunlv1@gmail.com
summary:
A student-led mental health initiative aimed to improve general mental wellness of the student body 
and allow our students to be the voice for how we as a university can promote student wellness and mental health services.

name: International Law Society
description:
The purposes of ILS are as follows:
1. To gain an understanding of international law through study and development.
2. To assist students in becoming internationally minded and gaining international
professional skills.
3. To promote the study and awareness of international law and encourage the cooperation
and understanding of different cultures and practices.
4. To assist and encourage international career resources and increase the opportunities for
students by:
     a. Offer opportunities to students to engage in relevant volunteer work
     b. Assist students with networking with attorneys who have international experience
5. To promote international understanding by providing law students with information about
study abroad programs and overseas internships and externships, as well as provide a
forum for those to share their international work experiences with the community.
summary: The International Law Society is an organization dedicated to providing a forum for international learning and connecting Boyd law students with the international legal community.

name: UNLV SIAM Student Chapter
description:
Join our Discord and Instagram!

 

UNLV SIAM (Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics) serves as an organization for STEM enthusiasts of all levels, with emphasis in math and its various applications. Expect collaborative events with other organizations and departments! 

 

The objectives of UNLV SIAM are to:

  1. Foster a sense of community in the student body,
  2. Educate students on the professional world of research, and
  3. Explore some of the many intricacies that math has to offer.

 

Regardless of your major or your mathematical background, you are welcome to join!
summary: UNLV SIAM (Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics) serves as an organization for STEM enthusiasts of all levels, with emphasis in math and professional development.

name: Organization of Psychology and Law
description: OPAL seeks to address the gap between the fields of law and psychology/behavior. OPAL seeks to educate members of the legal community about the intersection of the mind and the law, thereby creating better advocacy through empathy and understanding.
summary: OPAL seeks to address the gap between the fields of law and psychology/behavior. OPAL seeks to educate members of the legal community about the intersection of the mind and the law, thereby creating better advocacy through empathy and understanding.

name: Feminist Club
description:
The UNLV Feminist Club is a non-profit organization that has a focus on education and community building. It is also important to us that participation in the club is fun and entertaining! We hold events on-campus, collaborate with members of the wider Las Vegas community, and prioritize an engaging relationship with feminism for all of our members.

The feminism we practice in this club is completely intersectional. We intend to address issues in every marginalized community- we believe feminism can't be achieved unless everyone is included. Every person of every identity is welcome and encouraged to participate. Our only requirement is that you believe in feminism! Whether you want to strengthen your feminist community or have been an activist your entire life, we would love to have you there!
summary:
UNLV's Feminist Club has a focus on engaging with feminist theory, experiences, and activism in our local community.
With a mix of education, discussion and interactive activities- this intersectional club aims to be one which everyone can enjoy!

name: Sports and Entertainment Law Association
description: Must be a Boyd Law School Student! To provide career development in the area of sports and entertainment law. SELA is committed to establishing a reputable curriculum in this specific area of law and increasing student exposure to the industry. We will bring in guest speakers to give students opportunities to understand the many possible work options in this field, organize multiple networking events, and provide open discussions regarding the multitude of issues in Sports and Entertainment.
summary: Must be a Boyd Law School Student! To provide career development in the area of sports and entertainment law. SELA is committed to establishing a reputable curriculum in this specific area of law and increasing student exposure to the industry.

name: Español Club
description: Español Club is a space for conversation and listening in Spanish for all those native speakers of the Spanish Language and/or all those non-native speakers they wish to practice Spanish in a fun, diverse, and informal environment.
summary: Español Club is a space for conversation and listening in Spanish for all those native speakers of the Spanish Language and/or all those non-native speakers they wish to practice Spanish in a fun, diverse, and informal environment.

name: Dispute Resolution Society
description: The Dispute Resolution Society is a student-run organization committed to Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR). The vision of DRS mirrors that of the Saltman Center, and seeks to foster a better understanding of what Alternative Dispute Resolution is, the importance of resolving disputes as a part of the litigation process, and the positive effect Alternative Dispute Resolution has on the legal community. DRS will work in conjunction with the Saltman Center in facilitating discussion among students, faculty, and the legal community regarding issues related to ADR. DRS will also put on events, programs, activities, and engage in outreach in promoting all forms of Alternative Dispute Resolution.
summary: The Dispute Resolution Society is one of the oldest student organizations at UNLV and Boyd School of Law. We organize events focused on Alternative Dispute Resolution in the community and in concert with the Saltman Center for Conflict Resolution.

name: Chinese Corner
description:
Our club’s mission is to reinforce and expand Chinese language and cultural knowledge to both club members and non-members alike. 

Join our Discord for updates: https://discord.gg/tmWa9H5s.
summary: Our club’s mission is to reinforce and expand Chinese language and cultural knowledge to both club members and non-members alike. We hope to teach and share culture to create more well-rounded and knowledgeable global citizens.

name: The Wildlife Society
description:
The Wildlife Society at UNLV is an aspiring chapter of a much larger nonprofit organization that focuses on empowering people involved in wildlife and stewardship through science and education. TWS at UNLV is not only limited to students interested in wildlife, but also students wanting to build careers in conservation, natural resources, and management. Our goal is to expose members to opportunities such as workshops, field training, and networking connections to better prepare them for post graduation. 

 

Our chapter holds monthly meetings and frequent guest speaker presentations that allow students to connect with local and federal agencies and explore opportunities for career building. Additionally, we participate in stewardship events around the Las Vegas area.
summary: This organization exists for students with an interest in biology and wildlife sciences. Student will meet to discuss career options in the field, learn from professionals, and have opportunities to practice applicable career skills.

name: Music Teachers National Association
description:
For well over a century, MTNA has been the backbone for a collaborative community of music-teaching professionals, connecting both the seasoned educator and those at the beginning of their career.

MTNA membership does more than simply complement your career as a music professional—it supports, supplements and shapes it. It empowers music teachers nationwide to become better educators through networking, leadership, mentoring and educational opportunities.

Join today to make MTNA your partner in teaching!

MTNA Membership Delivers:

  • Peer Connections—a network of outlets, forums and channels for like-minded individuals in the music profession to collaborate and communicate, both locally and nationally.
  • Ongoing Education—conferences, events and advice on teaching tips and pedagogy, technology and web trends, master classes and informational sessions.
  • Invaluable Resources—access to countless benefits, from Certification, publications and consultation services to competitions, teacher enrichment grants and insurance.
summary: MTNA is an organization of musicians which seeks to:  (1) engage the public, (2) sustain the profession, and (3) inspire its members. At all levels, MTNA is devoted to nurturing the musical community and raising the standards of music education.

name: The William S. Boyd School of Law Chapter of The Federalist Society
description: The essence of The Federalist Society is open and honest debate, discussion, and decision-making. Many events held by the organization include debates between opposing speakers about important legal issues.
summary: The Federalist Society exists to promote the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, the separation of powers is central to our constitution, and that the duty of the judiciary is to say what the law is, not what it should be.

name: Armenian Law Students Association
description:
The Armenian Law Students Association (ALSA) is dedicated to advancing the educational and career aspirations of its members. Our key objectives include:

  • Community Building: Creating a welcoming environment for law students of Armenian descent and those interested in Armenian culture to connect, build relationships, and network with peers and alumni.

  • Academic and Career Support: Offering academic resources and mentoring to foster members' professional development and career advancement.

  • Community Service: Collaborating with Armenian-affiliated professional organizations to deliver legal services to underserved populations in the greater Las Vegas area.

  • Educational Programming: Hosting educational events such as lectures, panel discussions, and symposiums that address legal issues relevant to the Armenian community.

  • Law School Engagement: Organizing activities that enrich the William S. Boyd School of Law community and promote legal scholarship.
summary: The Armenian Law Students Association's mission is to foster goodwill among our members, assist and encourage each other in pursuing our legal education and careers, and generate interest in Armenian issues and culture.

name: UNLV Snow Club
description: Our club is a dynamic community of students passionate about skiing and snowboarding. Whether you're a seasoned pro or a beginner eager to learn, we welcome all levels of experience. Join us for exciting ski and snowboard trips where you can hit the slopes and have some fun.
summary: To help students participate and have fun in snow sports, regardless of their experience level.

name: UNLV Jazz Education Network Collegiate Chapter
description:
The Jazz Education Network is dedicated to building the Jazz and Commercial arts community by advancing education, promoting performance and developing new audiences. 

JEN advocates for the advancement of Jazz as an art form and the inclusion of Jazz pedagogy in all educational settings and in jazz artistry and business, promote Jazz and Commercial performance on a local and global basis, and lead the development of new and expanded Jazz and commercial audiences worldwide.

JEN is a community where inquiry, exploration and innovation are nurtured and encouraged to grow through education. JEN is committed to representing and cultivating diversity, equity, and inclusion in all areas of its operation including staff, board members, volunteers, presenters, clinicians, performers, and audiences. This commitment is present in all programs and decision making. We welcome and respect all backgrounds that reflect love and passion for jazz.
summary: The UNLV Jazz Education Network Collegiate Chapter is dedicated to promoting: jazz pedagogy, performance and expanding the jazz audience.

name: UNLV SDM Class of 2026
description: We are the council for UNLV SDM's Class of 2026
summary: We are the council for the class of 2026 at UNLV School of Dental Medicine. We oversee our entire class by acting as the liaison between the students and the faculty. We lead our classmates by ensuring that they are informed with a unifying message.

name: American Medical Association
description:
Founded in 1847, the American Medical Association (AMA) is the largest and only national association that convenes 190+ state and specialty medical societies and other critical stakeholders. Throughout history, the AMA has always followed its mission: to promote the art and science of medicine and the betterment of public health.

As the physicians’ powerful ally in patient care, the AMA delivers on this mission by representing physicians with a unified voice in courts and legislative bodies across the nation, removing obstacles that interfere with patient care, leading the charge to prevent chronic disease and confront public health crises, and driving the future of medicine to tackle the biggest challenges in health care and training the leaders of tomorrow.

The AMA’s system of governance and policy making include the board of trustees, House of Delegates, executive vice president, councils and committees, special sections, and AMA senior leadership and staff.
summary: The AMA’s system of governance and policy making include the board of trustees, House of Delegates, executive vice president, councils and committees, special sections, and AMA senior leadership and staff.

name: Cyber Clinic
summary: The Cyber Clinic strives to protect and educate small businesses while building the foundation for tomorrow’s cybersecurity professionals.

name: American Medical Student Association
description: The American Medical Student Association is an independent association of physicians-in-training in the United States. AMSA is a student-governed, national organization. This is the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV chapter.
summary: The American Medical Student Association is an independent association of physicians-in-training in the United States. AMSA is a student-governed, national organization. This is the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine at UNLV chapter.

name: Medical Students for Disability and Chronic Illness at UNLV
summary: Medical Students for Disability and Chronic Illness (MSDCI) at UNLV is a student-led group committed to enhancing accessibility of resources and providing a support system to students with disabilities and chronic illnesses.

name: Immigrant Rights Coalition
description: The purpose of the Immigrant Rights Coalition (IRC) is to defend and advocate for the rights of immigrants in our community through outreach and education. Our members serve the immigrant community of Las Vegas by volunteering and providing legal resources and assistance. IRC helps facilitate a dialogue in both the law school and the community at large about how best to provide aid and information to protect the rights of vulnerable immigrants. IRC works to inform local communities and leaders about the plight of immigrants in an effort to correct the common immigration misconceptions that exist today.
summary: The Immigrant Rights Coalition is a law student organization dedicated to community service, as well as defending and advocating for the rights of immigrants in our community!

name: UNLV Collegiate 100
description:
The Collegiate 100® (“C100”) has an important role to play on the college campus. The effective execution of that role requires that the Chapter be a responsible, productive, and cooperative segment of 100 Black Men of America, Inc. and the total college community.  

 

Membership into the Collegiate 100® is drawn primarily from African–American male and female college students through Chapters on university campuses across America.   The primary purpose of the Collegiate 100® program is to provide an avenue for 100 Black Men of America, Inc. chapters to continue their one-on-one and group mentoring to students as they matriculate from high school to college; thereby supporting the Mentoring The 100 Way Across a Lifetime initiatives.  Members of the Collegiate 100® will assist the local 100 Black Men Chapter (Sponsoring Chapter) with its programmatic initiatives that support the development of social, emotional, educational, and physical needs of youth who have few or no positive role models in the communities in which they live.  The Collegiate 100® program is not intended to create a separate functioning group that operates independently from the local Chapter of 100 Black Men of America, Inc.  The right to use intellectual properties must be approved by the local Chapter through authorization from the National Headquarters.

 

The Collegiate 100®, therefore, is not fraternal in nature and does not include the process of pledging or hazing to gain affiliation or membership.  Rather, the Collegiate 100® adds value by serving as a core of service volunteers to help carry out the mission of the 100 Black Men of America, Inc. and support the activities of the Sponsoring Chapter.

 

It is expected that those chosen as members of the Collegiate 100® will gain a greater appreciation for the mission and goals of the 100 Black Men of America, Inc.
summary: The primary purpose of the Collegiate 100® program is to provide an avenue for 100 Black Men of America, Inc. chapters to continue their one-on-one and group mentoring to students as they matriculate from high school to college.

name: La Voz, Latino Law Student Association
description:
La Voz is the Latino Law Student Association at the William S. Boyd School of Law. Our mission is to empower and support traditionally underrepresented students, aiming to increase representation in the legal profession. One of the key ways we accomplish this is through our nationally acclaimed mentorship program, Huellas.

 

Huellas, meaning "footprints" in Spanish, is an award-winning program that pairs a local attorney or judge with a Boyd law student, an undergraduate student, and a high school student for mentorship and support. This four-tier mentorship initiative is designed to create a pathway to the legal profession for underrepresented students, allowing each mentee to follow in the footsteps of their mentors.

 

Through Huellas and other initiatives, La Voz serves as a unifying and informative organization that connects the legal community and functions as both an educational and cultural resource for students, faculty, and staff.
summary: La Voz is the Latino Law Student Association at the William S. Boyd School of Law. Our mission is to empower and support traditionally underrepresented students, aiming to increase representation in the legal profession.

name: UNLV OUTLaws
description: The mission and purpose of OUTLaws is to serve the local community by programming, sponsoring, and otherwise providing information, events, and philanthropic opportunities related to issues of sexuality, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, and queer theory as they relate to the law and legal practice. OUTLaws shall also support lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, non-gendered, non-binary, questioning, intersex, asexual, and allied students at the William S. Boyd School of Law at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.
summary: Serving and supporting LGBTQIA+ and allied students at the William S. Boyd School of Law and beyond.

name: Books, Beverages & Bites
description:
The purpose of Books, Beverages, and Bites (BBB) is to foster personal and professional growth through self-improvement, leadership, and hospitality. We explore the hospitality industry to inspire, challenge, and transform our approach to service and leadership.

 

We are excited to see you at our next book discussion!

  • IG: unlv.bbb
  • E: bbb@unlv.edu
summary:
A Wellbeing & Hospitality Reading Club dedicated to savoring the joy in the journey, mindful living, and comfortable growth.

Curiosity. Community. Conversations.

name: International Student Organization
description: The International Student Organization (ISO) at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) is dedicated to providing international students with community and support. Our goal is to create a welcoming and inclusive environment where students from all over the world can connect, share and learn from one another. In addition, we organize various events, activities, and programs that promote cultural exchange and understanding. Through our efforts, we aim to help international students thrive academically and socially while they pursue their studies at UNLV.
summary: The International Student Organization(ISO) connects international students, fostering a welcoming & inclusive environment. Promoting cultural exchange & understanding through events dedicated to creating a sense of community among international Students

name: Minority Serving Institution Student Council
description: UNLV is a dual-designated Minority-Serving Institution (MSI). Serving as a part of the MSI Task Force, the MSI Student Council focuses on enhancing the positive impact of historically underrepresented minority student groups including, but not limited to, Native American/Alaskan Native, Asian, Black/African American, Hispanic, and Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander.
summary: The MSISC is taking steps to ensure student-facing programs, service models, and policies exist to help historically underrepresented students thrive at UNLV.

name: UNLV Women's Wrestling Club
description: UNLV Women's Wrestling is a collegiate women's wrestling team that competes in the National Collegiate Wrestling Association (NCWA). UNLV Women's Wrestling became the first Nevadan collegiate Women's Wrestling team when they stepped on the mat in early 2023. Now, our next goal is the win the NCWA West Coast Conference, and then the NCWA National Championships.
summary: UNLV Women's Wrestling is a collegiate women's wrestling team that competes in the National Collegiate Wrestling Association (NCWA).

name: If/When/How - Lawyering for Reproductive Justice
description: We are If/When/How: Lawyering for Reproductive Justice, a student organization which works to transform the law and policy landscape through advocacy, support, and organizing so all people have the power to determine if, when, and how to define, create, and sustain families with dignity and to actualize sexual and reproductive wellbeing on their own terms.
summary: Fighting for people to have the power to determine if, when, and how to actualize sexual and reproductive wellbeing on their own terms.

name: Hearts for the Homeless
description: Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) International is a nonprofit organization with chapters established globally. Our chapter aims to improve the health outcomes of the unhoused population in Las Vegas by providing free blood pressure screenings and other healthcare services, promoting heart health education, and engaging in community service. Our organization provides pre-health students with the opportunity to gain relevant clinic experience, knowledge in public health and preventative health measures, and leadership experience for professional school.
summary: Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) UNLV is a chapter of an international organization that is dedicated to contributing to the health and well-being of those who are experiencing homelessness.

name: Medical Supplies Recovery
description:
TOGETHER, WE CAN MAKE A DIFFERENCE
Does your clinic have excess or expired medical supplies taking up valuable clinic space?
Consider donating unwanted supplies and equipment to Medical Supply Recovery (MSR)! Our
organization collects donations free of charge and distributes them as needed to free clinics
both locally and internationally.

RECOVER

Our organization works with
various local care providers
to collect useable medical
surplus before supplies are
disposed of.

REDISTRIBUTE
Recovered supplies are
redistributed upon request
to fulfill critical supply needs
in free clinics across the
globe.

REPURPOSE
The redistribution of these
supplies serves to promote
improved global health and
greater environmental
awareness.
summary: Serving the local Las Vegas medical community by repurposing medical supplies

name: UNLV Chemistry Club
description:
Welcome to the UNLV Chemistry Club! We host multiple fun events throughout the semester, including special events on National Chemistry Week and Mole day! We also provide our students with volunteering opportunities through the American Chemical Society (ACS). As well, we encourage our members to also attend the Chemistry Seminars hosted by UNLV Chemistry department. 

We have 1 general meeting a month to discuss upcoming events with our members. 

No Specific Major is required to join! Please email us with any questions and we would love to have you join our Chemistry Family!
summary: A club for UNLV students who are interested in Chemistry. All majors welcome! :)

name: Gamma Iota Sigma
description: Gamma Iota Sigma is an international professional fraternity with 100 Chapters Nationwide with 5,000+ members organized to promote, encourage, and sustain student interest in insurance, risk management, and actuarial science as professions. Gamma aims to encourage high moral and scholastic attainments and to facilitate the interaction and cooperation of educational institutions, industry, and professional organizations by fostering research, scholarship, and improved public relations.
summary: Gamma Iota Sigma is an international professional fraternity with 100 Chapters Nationwide with 5,000+ members organized to promote, encourage, and sustain student interest in insurance, risk management, and actuarial science as professions.

name: UNLV Triathlon Club
description: The UNLV Triathlon Club meet regularly to train and compete in the sport of triathlon. UNLV is a part of the West Coast Collegiate Conference and competes with other schools within the conference as well as local races.
summary: The UNLV TRI Club is a team of students that will exercise and compete together in the triathlon sport, which includes swimming, biking, and running.

name: Transfer Student Association at the William S. Boyd School of Law
description: Transfer Student Association (at the William S. Boyd School of Law) ("TSA") is a student organization dedicated to supporting transfer students. The TSA aims to facilitate integration, foster community, provide resources and mentorship, and organize relevant events for these students. It is governed by an Executive Board, comprised of a President, Vice-President, Secretary, and Treasurer. Members, predominantly transfer students, are allowed to participate in the organization's events, activities, and decision-making processes. General and board meetings are held periodically.
summary: The Transfer Student Association at the William S. Boyd School of Law is an organization made to help transfer students integrate into their new environment.

name: The Veterinary Association
summary: Our club focuses on bringing together those with a passion to love and help animals. This club also aims to prepare those in the pre-vet studies for professional school and help others find multiple opportunities working with animals.

name: UNLV Rebel Divas
description:
UNLV Majorette Dance Team. Spread social and community Awareness and Interaction

Provide African Americans with an excellent opportunity to express themselves, celebrate heritage, and demonstrate talent. Infusing jazz, West African, and hip-hop dance styles
summary: UNLV Majorette Dance Club/Team. Provide African Americans with an excellent opportunity to express themselves, celebrate heritage, and demonstrate talent. Infusing jazz, West African, and hip-hop dance styles

name: Fright! Club
summary: FRIGHT! CLUB is a horror film club that embraces, celebrates, and analyzes various aspects of the genre through screenings, open discussions, games, and much more!

name: Child Care Access Means Parents In School
description: This organization is for student participants in the Child Care Access Means Parents in School (CCAMPIS) program to support students that are parents toward successful degree completion.
summary: This organization is for student participants in the Child Care Access Means Parents in School (CCAMPIS) program to support students that are parents toward successful degree completion.

name: UNLV Dreamers Movement
description:
UNLVDM is an on-campus organization that unites individuals who share a passion for movement. More than just a club, UNLVDM is a welcoming community. Our primary goal is to foster a safe learning environment for all students to explore movement, express themselves through art, and have fun. For those who are interested in teaching, we provide the opportunity to broaden pedagogical skills by leading a class.

 

Every month, we conduct a team meeting to handle scheduling logistics, which includes selecting the instructors for the next month. All other meetings take the form as dance classes. 

 

Whether you’re a life-long dancer or eager to try something new, if dance is your passion, our club is the perfect place for you. We’re a judgment-free space dedicated to bringing like-minded individuals together to share in the joy of moving. We value each person as a unique individual and are here to support and uplift one another!
summary: UNLVDM is a dance club for everybody! All members have the opportunity to teach and take classes from other members in several styles including hip hop, tap, jazz, modern, ballet, improvisation, pointe, contemporary, and combinations, etc.

name: UNLV Ping Pong Club
description:
Provide a space for students to socialize with others who share the interest of ping pong.

Learn the skills of awareness, hand-eye coordination, & reaction time

Improve both your physical and mental health, reduce stress, and have a great time while doing it.

Whether you swing by for a casual rally or a competitive match, you belong!
summary: Provide a space for students to socialize with others who share the interest of ping pong.

name: UNLV Color Guard
description: The UNLV Color Guard has a long history of supporting UNLV with ceremonies requiring the posting of the United States and Nevada State Flags. These events include Commencement Ceremonies, football games, UNLV Veterans Association events, and many more. External to UNLV, the organization supports local high schools, Las Vegas Raiders football team, Veterans Associations, and many more.
summary: The UNLV Color Guard is an avid participant in ceremonious events that require the presentation of the US and State Flag while the National Anthem is playing. The organization supports other organizations on campus and in the state of Nevada.

name: Y-Elements @ UNLV
description:
We meet once a week on-campus as an extension of American Missionary Church - Las Vegas, a small part of the United Denominations (UD-OLGC) within Dag Heward-Mills Ministries (DHMM).

Currently, we meet on Thursdays at 6:30 PM in the Student Union Art Gallery (Floor 2). Come by for a laid-back hangout after a tiring day.

 

To find out more, text Joshua at 702-201-2773 or join us on Discord!

https://discord.gg/tA2uts7vdC 

 

Welcome to AMC Las Vegas!
summary:
Two goals: soul-winning and loving the Lord.
We preach the Gospel (Mat 28:19-20) and make disciples who seek God's presence (Isaiah 58:11).
You are never too young to love the Lord!

name: Runway Rebelz
description: In this club there will be many events that our club members can attend to get a better understanding of the fashion industry. Not only that but get the chance to gain exposure and someday be apart of an amazing and creative indusrty. Our club will incorporate sewing, drawing and modeling. All these amazing club meetings will prepare everyone for the end of semsester Runway(date TBA).
summary: This organization is meant to be a creative outlet for students with a passion in fashion, and give students the platform to start a future in fashion. Club members will collaborate and bring new ideas towards the fashion industry.

name: American Society for Microbiology - UNLV Student Chapter
description: Welcome to the American Society for Microbiology, UNLV Student Chapter. Our mission is to promote science as well as microbiology within Southern Nevada. Some of our objectives include: promoting student interest and further improving the quality of the Microbiology program at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas; (2) promoting elementary and high school student interest in microbiology; (3) promoting high school student attendance at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas; (4) providing aid for students in Microbiology at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas with respect to the academic curriculum, research, employment, and (5) promoting student participation in local, state, and national ASM meetings. These goals are achieved through our monthly meetings, public outreach events, joint ventures with public health facilities and other scientific societies, symposia, and information provided through our website. We welcome all students with interests in microbiology and related sciences. We offer our members networking opportunities to help foster professional camaraderie and prepare budding scientists to interact with the local scientific community, learning to overcome the future challenges in microbiology.
summary: ASM UNLV is an organization dedicated to promoting microbiology within Southern Nevada. We offer our members networking and outreach opportunities, and foster a scientific community for budding and experienced scientists.

name: Women in Mathematics
description: We will host events and gatherings where we will discuss how to successfully move through undergraduate studies in math and science. Events where you will invite female professors and scientists to give talks about their experiences and successes. We want create a community of support for women in mathematics at UNLV. The members do not have to be just women, everyone is welcome to join the events.
summary: Women in Mathematics empowers and advances students in mathematics at UNLV.

name: Asian & Pacific Islander Student Dental Association
description: UNLV SDM API celebrates and honors the rich tapestry of Asian and Pacific Islander cultures and heritages. We strive to foster understanding and appreciation, bridging communities through shared stories and traditions.
summary: UNLV SDM API celebrates and honors the rich tapestry of Asian and Pacific Islander cultures and heritages. We strive to foster understanding and appreciation, bridging communities through shared stories and traditions.

name: Tradition Keeper Medallion Program
description: • As we embark on this exciting journey, we come together as one community, committed to preserving the cherished traditions and legacies that have shaped our college into the extraordinary place it is today. Like the intertwined threads of a tapestry, traditions form an integral part of the fabric that binds us together, transcending generations and uniting us in shared experiences and values. They are the living bridges that connect the past, the present, and the future, carrying the essence of our history, pride, and aspirations through time. The Tradition Keeper Medallion Program stands as a testament to our unwavering commitment to safeguarding and nurturing these unique and time-honored practices. From the raucous cheers at sporting events to the solemn ceremonies of academic achievement, from the heartwarming rituals that mark the beginning and end of each academic year to the inspiring traditions that foster camaraderie and community spirit, every aspect of our campus life holds a story worth preserving. By documenting, promoting, and passing down these traditions, we ensure that they remain an integral part of our college experience for generations to come. The Tradition Keeper program serves as a compass that steers us toward continuity, reminding us of the values that have always set us apart.
summary: The Tradition Keeper Medallion Program is a unique way for the UNLV community to document their memories and be rewarded in the process.

name: Hindu YUVA at UNLV
description: The mission of Hindu YUVA at UNLV organization is to bring together both Hindu and non-Hindu students to raise awareness about what Hinduism is. As one of the largest and oldest religions in the world, we believe it is necessary that there be an organization on campus to represent the interests of the Hindu community present here. We hope to create a comfortable atmosphere where students can come and celebrate the Hindu culture. We hope to achieve these goals by having team-building games, yoga and meditation, lively and exciting debates, and presentations at our weekly meetings. All in all, we want to build a small Hindu community within UNLV so that students can come and discover a home away from home.
summary: The mission of Hindu YUVA at UNLV organization is to bring together both Hindu and non-Hindu students to raise awareness about what Hinduism is. As one of the largest and oldest religions in the world, we believe it is necessary.

name: Boyd Game Club
description:
Boyd Game Club is a social club for Boyd Law Students. The club hosts game events to allow students to socialize through board games, card games, videogames, chess, TTRPG's, and other forms of entertainment.

The club hosts meeting monthly including a lunch time event, an after-school on-campus event, and a Friday event at a local board game cafe, as well as partnering with other organizations and events to host events with games.
summary: Boyd Game Club is a social club for Boyd Law Students. The club hosts game events to allow students to socialize through board games, card games, videogames, chess, TTRPG's, and other forms of entertainment.

name: Society for Black Scientists
description:
The Society for Black Scientists (SBS) is dedicated to increasing Black student representation in STEM fields. Founded with the aim of dismantling barriers faced by underrepresented students in STEM, the SBS strives to create a supportive and empowering environment that fosters academic and professional success among black students at UNLV.

 

One of the SBS’s primary objectives is to encourage increased representation of Black students in STEM disciplines. This goal will be pursued  through various initiatives, such as mentorship programs, networking events, and outreach activities.

 

A key focus of the SBS is to cultivate a sense of belonging among Black students in STEM. Recognizing the importance of community and support networks, the SBS provides a platform for students to connect with like-minded peers, mentors, and professionals. This inclusive environment encourages collaboration, innovation, and personal growth, ensuring that Black students feel valued, empowered, and equipped to overcome challenges encountered during their educational and professional journeys.

 

In addition to providing tutoring in some key courses for undergraduate students, the SBS aims to address the unique needs and aspirations of this demographic by forging partnerships with academic institutions, industry leaders, and community organizations. These steps ensure its members have the necessary tools and guidance for academic, professional, and personal growth.

Meeting Minutes
summary: The Society for Black Scientists is a dynamic and inclusive organization committed to promoting involvement of Black student population in STEM by providing dedicated support to underrepsented students in the College of Sciences at UNLV.

name: Christians on Campus
description: Christian on Campus is a vibrant and inclusive campus club dedicated to creating a welcoming and nurturing environment for students of all backgrounds who are interested in exploring the Christian faith, deepening their understanding of the Bible, and embracing Christian culture through learning and hymn singing. Our aim is to foster spiritual growth, build a supportive community, and promote a culture of love, compassion, and understanding. Our community is built on respect, openness, and a desire to learn and grow together. Whether you're a lifelong Christian, curious about Christianity, or simply looking for a supportive community, you are warmly invited to join us.
summary: Christian on Campus is a vibrant and inclusive campus club dedicated to creating a welcoming and nurturing environment for students of all backgrounds.  https://christiansoncampusunlv.com/

name: New Hope Ocean Club
description: Through fishing and the knowledge of the ocean resources, NHOC encourages students to engage with professors and professionals to address global hunger and other social issues. And develop broad world view to become well rounded future leaders.
summary: Through fishing and the knowledge of the ocean resources, NHOC encourages students to engage with professors and professionals to address global hunger and other social issues. And develop broad world view to become well rounded future leaders.

name: National Association of Black Accountants, Inc.
description:
We engage, empower, and educate Black business leaders and institutions by providing a
platform to enhance skills and elevate Voice. Our 5 Es: engage, empower, educate, enhance, and elevate.

Doing so, we’re “lifting as we climb.”

(a)To assist and encourage members of minority groups in entering the profession of accounting;

(b) To stimulate acquaintance and fellowship among members of minority groups;

(c) To provide opportunities for members of minority groups to increase their knowledge of accounting, finance, and related business practices and methods to improve or enhance their individual capabilities.

(d) To unite, through membership in the Association, persons interested in enhancing opportunities for minority groups in accounting, finance, and related businesses.

(e) To provide education and training opportunities to its members.
summary:
We engage, empower, and educate Black business leaders and institutions by providing a
platform to enhance skills and elevate Voice. Our 5 Es: engage, empower, educate, enhance, and
elevate. Doing so, we’re “lifting as we climb.”

name: Screen Acting Club
description:
At the Screen-Acting Club, we envelope ourselves in all things film. With a focus on acting in front of the camera, we run workshops, screenings, and make films based on our members interests. If your major isn’t film related, don’t worry! This club is for any student even remotely interested in film.

 

⇏ CURRENT 2024-2025 CORE MEMBERS ⇍

 

President

Shelby Graham

unlvscreenacting@gmail.com

 

Scheduling Liaison

Aatreya Maktal

liaison.unlvsac@gmail.com

summary: Screen Acting Club is a way to explore different creative techniques. The essence of SAC is to work with or without experience in a fun environment. This club is a safe place, no pressure only instinct. How you talk/interact, is the dependent.

name: J. Reuben Clark Law Society, UNLV Student Chapter
description:
The J. Reuben Clark Law Society (JRCLS) is a multi-faith organization whose purpose is to affirm the strength brought to the study of law by a student’s personal religious convictions. Membership in the JRCLS is open to all students currently enrolled at the William S. Boyd School of Law. The JRCLS will also provide a welcome environment for JRCLS members, all students of the School of Law, and the family and friends of both.

The mission of the JRCLS aligns with the international J. Reuben Clark Law Society mission statement: “We affirm the strength brought to the study of law by a lawyer’s personal religious conviction. We strive through public service and professional excellence to promote fairness and virtue founded upon the rule of law.”

To accomplish its mission, the JRCLS at UNLV will: (1) encourage its members to regard the study of law as preparation to serve the courts, clients, and society; (2) promote and protect the rule of law; (3) provide members with opportunities for continuing dialogue on legal topics of significance; and (4) offer opportunities for members to use the legal skills they acquire to provide service to the community.
summary: The J. Reuben Clark Law Society is a global society of faith-based lawyers and law students who are working together to make a difference in the world.

name: SGI Buddhism
description: This club will serve as a gathering for the means of introducing Buddhism to others. We will discuss Buddhist concepts and writings, as well discuss societal issues and impacts in relation to Buddhism such as war, nuclear weapons, and injustice. Our goal is to create an environment with profound dialogue that builds a bridge of understanding for Nichiren Buddhism and a society for peace.
