summary: Soka Gakkai International (SGI) was founded on January 26, 1975, as a worldwide network of socially engaged Buddhists dedicated to a common vision of a better world through the empowerment of the individuals through Buddhist Humanism.

name: Undergraduate Student Association of Research
description:
The Undergraduate Student Association of Research’s mission is to continuously foster an interest in research and spread awareness of opportunities available at the collegiate level for all undergraduate students regardless of background or prior experience. We also strive to create an environment for students to share their research ideas, interests, successes, and failures.

The resources and opportunities we provide include (but are not limited to) networking events, lab tours, community events, research workshops, etc.

If you would like to get involved, you can contact us through our email u-star@unlv.edu or social media @ustarunlv
summary: U-STAR is a student-led support network for undergraduates interested in or currently participating in research at UNLV.

name: Pickleball Club
description: The Pickleball Club is a student-led organization revolving around the inclusion of our college community. We provide a welcoming environment for players of all skill levels to enjoy this sport with one another. Please follow our Instagram to be updated on upcoming meetings and events!
summary: The Pickleball Club is a student-led organization revolving around the inclusion of our college community. We provide a welcoming enviroment for players of all skill levels to enjoy this sport with one another.

name: Biostatistical and Epidemiological Applied Methods for Students Society
description: The purpose of BEAMSS is to promote a conducive environment for BEAMSS members to increase EAB knowledge and experience among UNLV students; promote and foster innovative, student-led research; and collaborate with faculty to learn about EAB research methods and applications. By encouraging students to interact with each other and build a learning community, and gain valuable skills in their research outside of coursework, the club can help to foster collaboration, creativity, and networking opportunities among its members.
summary: The purpose of BEAMSS is to promote a conducive environment for BEAMSS members to increase EAB knowledge and experience; promote and foster innovative, student-led research; and collaborate with faculty to learn about EAB research methods.

name: American Railway Engineering and Maintenance-of-Way Association UNLV Chapter
description: This organization is a place for students, faculty, and industry leaders to connect over their excitement about the rail industry in either a professional or personal manner. Past field trips include visits to the Railroad Museum in Boulder City and the Classification Yard in Barstow. Seminars on railroad will be hold regularly. Membership in the National AREMA organization is encouraged to take full advantage of benefits, but not required.
summary: UNLV's AREMA chapter connects students enthusiastic about rail transportation with each other and members of the railroad industry.

name: The Ludomusicology Society
description: Our goal as a group is to build a community for video game music and understand what makes the genre so great. We also strive to develop a reputation for video game music and Ludomusicology in the academic world! We give presentations on music theory and music in video games, and every semester, we have a Smash Bros Tournament and a Mario Kart Tournament. We also love to play Towerfall and Jackbox!!
summary: A club dedicated to discussing and appreciating video game music!

name: Middle Eastern Law Students Association
description:
The goal of the Middle Eastern Law Students Association (MELSA) is to encourage unity among a diverse student body and promote an understanding of various Middle Eastern cultures. We strive to connect our members and the legal community to encourage expression of different backgrounds and views. MELSA seeks to provide a platform for our members to speak about their backgrounds and advocate for ideas important to them. 

MELSA will connect the Boyd community with various Middle Eastern cultures through opportunities such as fundraisers, socials, speaker events, and other causes that our members are passionate about. We invite all students from all backgrounds to become a part of MELSA.
summary: The goal of the Middle Eastern Law Students Association (MELSA) is to encourage unity among a diverse student body and promote an understanding of various Middle Eastern cultures.

name: Trust and Estate Planning Society
summary: A student organization for the William S. Boyd School of Law that will spread awareness on the field of trusts and estate Planning.

name: UNLV Poetry Club
description:
UNLV Poetry Club is a new RSO aiming to create an inclusive and welcoming space for everyone who shares a passion for poetry! ��📖 We plan to hold themed workshops, invite guest speakers, and organize poetry readings. We meet on Tuesdays from 5:30-6:30pm, inside the Black Mountain Institute located inside RLL. 

Join our Discord here��

Follow us on Instagram here��
summary: UNLV Poetry Club is a brand new RSO aiming to cultivate a supportive community for students who love poetry!  We plan to hold themed workshops, invite guest speakers, and organize an undergraduate reading every year.

name: UNLV School of Dental Medicine Class of 2027
summary: UNLV School of Dental Medicine Class of 2027 student council committee

name: UNLV Grad Pride Alliance
description:
UNLV Grad Pride Alliance, or GPA, works to educate fellow Graduate students about LGBTQIA+ experiences, encourage current and future professional and personal allyship while creating a social space for LGBTQIA+ grad students.

Our organization holds social events throughout the year, and is looking to network with other student groups and academic departments to best serve UNLV and its LGBTQIA+ population.
summary: UNLV Grad Pride Alliance, or GPA works to educate fellow Graduate students about LGBTQIA+ experiences, encourage current and future professional and personal allyship while creating a social space for LGBTQIA+ grad students.

name: Business In Action
summary: Business in Action is a philanthropic student organization within Lee Business School that hosts outreach events each semester partnering with other student organizations on campus and within the community to make a difference for those in need.

name: UNLV BIOS
description: Welcome to UNLV BIOS. Our mission to help foster community amongst biology graduate students. Some of our objectives include: (1)hosting community events for graduate students to make friends and decompress from graduate school stresses,(2) communicating job/internship opportunities, (3) helping graduate students to integrate mental/ physical wellness into their weekly schedules, and (4) helping with graduate recruitment events by putting on graduate poster sessions, and building tours to help highlight what it means to be a graduate student in SOLS. Our organization offers graduate students the opportunity to be a part of the UNLV graduate community and helps students to make UNLV feel like home.
summary: UNLV BIOS is a biology graduate student organization that aims to foster community, communicate job/ internship opportunities, and inform graduate students of important information needed for graduation.

name: Corporate Law Society
description:
Corporate Law Society's mission is to inform and educate law students about corporate law and its diverse work opportunities, as well as help network students with attorneys and law firms in and outside of Las Vegas.

CLS will: 

  1. Host events that inform and educate students about all the different practices associated with corporate law. 
  2. Host panels or discussions with attorneys, professors, and/or professionals associated in the corporate work field, or corporate law field.
  3. Actively promote the William S. Boyd School of Law in order to attract a more diverse student body.
summary: Corporate Law Society's mission is to inform and educate law students about corporate law and its diverse work opportunities, as well as help network students with attorneys and law firms in and outside of Las Vegas.

name: WiCyS Student Chapter
description: WiCyS is an organization dedicated to bringing together women in cybersecurity, from academia, research and industry, to share knowledge, experience, networking, and mentoring. Our mission is to help build a strong gender-diverse cybersecurity workforce by facilitating recruitment, retention, and advancement for women in the field.
summary: Women in Cybersecurity (WiCyS) is a community that promotes women’s education, participation, and leadership in the field of cybersecurity.

name: William S. Boyd School of Law
description:
The William S. Boyd School of Law is the sole law school in the state of Nevada. We are a young law school and we had the privilege of building this school from the ground up: imagining the ideal, modeling best practices, creating an outstanding curriculum, and assembling our fine faculty. We've worked hard to ensure that the ideal we first envisioned has become a reality. Our students are exposed to real-world challenges, professional interactions, and public clinics from the very beginning, and community service is an integral part of their education and experience.

This page is intended for undergraduate prospective students to use to contact us as well as view upcoming recruitment events.
summary: The William S. Boyd School of Law is the only Law School in the state of Nevada.

name: UNLV Rebel Media Group
description:
Rebel Media Group gives students the chance to gain invaluable hands-on experience in a real media agency setting and create exciting work for diverse clients. Student learning opportunities include:

  • Social media management and strategy

  • Content creation, including graphic design, photography, and videography

  • Project management

  • Team collaboration

  • Client relations

  • Leadership

By working with our motivated team of social media managers, community builders, and content creators, students can take pride in their ability to tell compelling stories. At the same time, clients receive unique, out-of-the-box work that pushes the boundaries of social media.
summary: Rebel Media Group is a full-service digital media agency located in UNLV’s Greenspun Hall.

name: Fostering Scholars
description: Mission Our mission is to empower and uplift students who have experienced foster care by providing a holistic campus-based support program at UNLV. Through personalized coaching, engaging workshops, and tangible resources, we aim to foster a nurturing environment that enables these resilient students to thrive academically, socially, and emotionally, ultimately paving the way for their success in higher education and beyond. Purpose The purpose of Fostering Scholars is to address the unique challenges faced by students with foster care histories by offering tailored resources and services that help them thrive in their academic pursuits. Fostering Scholars is increasing the awareness among current and former foster youth that higher education is an attainable goal due to the funding resources and support available at UNLV. The program aims to improve retention rates, increase graduation rates, and promote overall well-being for students who have experienced foster care. Vision The UNLV Fostering Scholars Program is a campus-based community support network with dedicated programming to support students who have experienced foster care in pursuing a degree from UNLV. This program is student-led based on best practices and is the first of its kind in the state of Nevada. The UNLV Fostering Scholars Program draws together resources from across the institution, the local community, Clark County, and the state of Nevada to help address the educational, social, emotional, physical, and financial needs of UNLV students with a history of foster care experience. The UNLV Fostering Scholars Program acts as a single point of contact for students. The UNLV Fostering Scholars Program partners with the Clark County Department of Family Services, Clark County Social Services, NSHE, Children’s Advocacy Alliance, iFoster Jobs, and other state and local agencies to raise awareness of UNLV staff and other community partners regarding the needs of students who have a history of foster care experiences. Fostering Scholars is also working to increase the awareness among current and former foster youth that higher education is an attainable goal financially due to the funding resources for former foster youth, as well as the support available to them at UNLV by the Fostering Scholars program.
summary: Support program for students who have experienced foster care after the age of 13 while at UNLV

name: Basic Needs Program
description: The Basic Needs Program, housed within the office of Service Learning and Leadership (SLL) supports students experiencing housing, food, and any other basic needs insecurity by offering in person guidance and connections to community and campus resources. The program offers educational workshops to connect students to various resources and involvement opportunities. Additionally, students can access daily necessity items such as hygiene items, school supplies, nonperishable foods, clothing, childcare, and household items free of charge both at our monthly Take What You Need events and in between events by coming by our office.
summary: The Basic Needs Program supports students experiencing any form of basic needs insecurity, from one on one support to providing daily necessity access.

name: Youth Voice of Nevada
description:
The Youth Voice of Nevada was founded with the belief that young people are an important and valuable part of our community, and that their voices and perspectives should be heard and taken into account when making decisions that impact their lives.

 

Through community service projects, advocacy efforts, and leadership development programs, we strive to give young people the tools and resources they need to make a positive impact in their communities and beyond.

 

Our organization is made up of dedicated young people from all over Nevada who are passionate about making a difference. They come from diverse backgrounds and perspectives, but they are united by a shared commitment to creating a brighter future for themselves and their communities.
summary: The Youth Voice of Nevada is to empower and inspire young people to become active and engaged members of their communities, and to provide a platform for youth to have their voices heard and to make a positive impact on their community

name: UNLV, eh?
description:
UNLV, eh? is a club where Canadians can join together and stay connected while away from home, especially during the holidays when we can't go home, like Thanksgiving.

This club is open to Canadians, those interested in Canadian culture or just for people to come, hang out and connect with other UNLV students!

 

Follow us on Instagram @unlv_eh
summary:
Are you Canadian? Interested in Canadian culture? Wanting to celebrate Thanksgiving in October? 
Come by and hang out! Open to Canadians and non-Canadians alike.

name: UNLV School of Nursing Student Body
description: Our objective is to cultivate a strong loyalty towards the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, while advancing our members' social, educational, and cultural growth. We acknowledge that these goals are best achieved in an environment characterized by academic excellence, philanthropy, and strong bonds of friendship
summary: We are the UNLV School of Nursing Student Body, also known as the UNLV Future Nurses! Our mission is to foster a profound sense of loyalty to the UNLV, while advancing the social, educational, and cultural growth of our student nurses.

name: The Ware House Theatre
description: We're the champions of the undergraduate voice in theatre. Our mission is to develop a network of theatrically minded individuals providing the undergraduate student body with training and opportunities through workshops, showcase events, playwriting festivals, and full theatrical productions.
summary: We are the champions of the undergraduate voice in theatre. We're developing a network of theatre lovers, providing training and opportunities, with workshops, showcase events, playwriting festivals, and full theatrical productions.

name: UNLV American Indigenous Business Leaders
description: The mission of UNLV AIBL is to increase the representation of American Indians and Alaska Natives in business and entrepreneurial ventures through education and leadership development opportunities.
summary: The mission of AIBL is to increase the representation of American Indians and Alaska Natives in business and entrepreneurial ventures through education and leadership development opportunities.

name: Bridge Las Vegas
description:
Bridge Las Vegas is UNLV's BridgeUSA Chapter, an organization that is present at over 73 educational institutions in 40 different states and involves over 10,000 students. 

We're a conversation club, not a debate club: Bridge aims to reduce political polarization by fostering healthy and positive discussions and helping promote strong communication and open-mindedness - regardless of topic or gravity.

87% of today's youth are tired of political division, and 60% are worried about democracy's future entirely.

Join our discussions today and help us come together to build a bridge for a better future.
summary: Bridge Las Vegas aims to reduce political polarization by fostering healthy and positive conversations and helping promote strong communication and open-mindedness in both politics and other topics of discussion.

name: The Cinematographers Society
description: The Cinematographers Society at UNLV is a community focused on furthering understanding of the art of photography for the screen. Our aim is to organize opportunities for film students to gather and discuss the technical features of professional cinematographers. Gatherings will cover topics on the skills and tech surrounding the film industry along with information about cameras, lensing, lighting, production methodologies and all things filmmaking.
summary: The Cinematographers Society aims to enhance education in the arts and techniques of cinematography through discussions with invitees, demonstrations and further learning.

name: Club Management Association of America UNLV Student Chapter
description: UNLV CMAA is for UNLV Hospitality looking for networking and education about the private club industry. This club will tour private club properties and network with industry professionals. Private clubs include Country Clubs, Membership-Based Golf Clubs, Yacht Clubs, and athletic clubs. We work With CMAA National to create educational opportunities for students at UNLV.
summary: UNLV CMAA is for UNLV Hospitality students looking to network and learn about the private club industry. We host club tours at various membership-based clubs, host guest speakers, and industry panels.

name: Alpha Phi
description: Alpha Phi is the UNLV Chapter for Pi Theta Epsilon (PTE). Pi Theta Epsilon is a specialized honor society for occupational therapy students and alumni. PTE recognizes and encourages superior scholarship among students enrolled in accredited educational programs. Alpha Phi recognizes students from UNLV's Occupational Therapy Doctoral program.
summary: Alpha Phi is the UNLV Chapter for Pi Theta Epsilon (PTE). Pi Theta Epsilon is a specialized honor society for occupational therapy students and alumni. PTE recognizes and encourages superior scholarship among students enrolled in accredited educational p

name: Native American Law Students Association
summary: The purpose of the Native American Law Students Association is to articulate and promote the professional needs and goals of Native American law students and their allies in Native American law pursuing a legal education at the Boyd School of Law.

name: Students for Salt
description: Students for Salt is a group of students who work together to help Salt Company reach, serve, and influence the University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) campus. The Salt Company (TSC) is a college ministry of King’s City Church that seeks to bring Good News to the Rebels.
summary: Students for Salt is a college ministry that exists to create a Christ-centered community on UNLV’s campus.

name: Perfumery Club
summary: Perfumery club is a safe and fun social space where everyone is taught how to create perfume.

name: Youthphoria Dance Club
description: "Youthphoria, where the rhythms of youth collide" Our club's main goal is to connect students with a dancing passion from all backgrounds. Whether you are a seasoned dancer or just discovering your groove, join us for energetic, laughter, and unforgettable memories. This club will allow you to increase your confidence, build friendships, and boost your physical and mental health. Let's create moments of musical youthphoria together!
summary: Our club is to gather people who love dancing. Together we will dance, perform, and have fun!

name: Dental Anesthesiology Club
summary: The Dental Anesthesiology Club aims to educate pre-doctoral students about the specialty and assist in applying to residency programs.

name: Integrative Mental Health Pact
description: An affinity and reading group for students of color interested psychology and mental health. We meet once a month to read academic articles, participate in group activities, and engage in discussions to enhance our experiences as minority students interested in mental health.
summary: An affinity and reading group for students of color interested psychology and mental health.

name: AI and Data Science Club
description:
Introduction

Welcome to UNLV's AI and Data Science club, a passionate community of students and professionals of all skill-levels (first-year to PHD) aiming to learn more about machine learning, data science, and artificial intelligence. 

Objectives

1. Education and skill development through interactive workshops, seminars, courses, study groups, and competitions.

2. Community and Networking with professionals in the industry and other students.

3. Encourage and support Research and Project Development related to AI and Data Science.

4. Career Development via access to professionals in industry, resume reviews, mock-interviews, and job placement opportunities.

Membership Benefits

- Access to learning resources and a community of passionate learners.

- Opportunities to work on exciting projects and gain practical experience.

- Networking and job-placement through access to professionals in the field.

- Additional member benefits are listed on our discord in the Join Us section.

Join Us

discord: https://discord.gg/9M7UryuXnh

website: https://aiclub.cs.unlv.edu
summary: The AI and Data Science club is dedicated to fostering a community of driven students and professionals passionate about machine learning, artificial intelligence, and data science.

name: UNLV AWS CLOUD CLUB
description: The club aims to provide students with opportunities to learn about AWS services, cloud computing concepts, and hands-on experience through workshops, projects, and networking events. Members can gain valuable skills relevant to careers in cloud computing and related fields.
summary: The UNLV AWS Cloud Club is a student organization at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) focused on Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud computing technology.

name: UNLV Battery Workforce Challenge
description: The Battery Workforce Challenge collegiate competition is a three-year engineering competition that challenges North American universities and their community college partners to design, build, test, and integrate an advanced EV battery pack into a Stellantis vehicle. Our first year started in the Fall of 2023 and we intend to bring in students expressing a strong interest in learning technical or non-technical traits. From our range of battery hardware, software testing, and CAD designing, we are also looking for those who can market, assist with communications, and spread the word about us.
summary: The UNLV & CSN Battery Workforce Challenge is a collaborative program designed to cultivate a supportive and engaging learning environment for students seeking careers in the rapidly growing electric vehicle battery industry.

name: College of Education Alumni Engagement
description:
UNLV COLLEGE OF EDUCATION ALUMNI ENGAGEMENT

In 1957, UNLV set forward its first graduating class. You are part of a special tradition - a tradition of excellence in education, service as professionals, and support of future alumni. Wherever your life’s work takes you, we hope UNLV and the College of Education will always feel like “home” to you.

 

With an alumni base of more than 24,000 and growing each year, we encourage our graduates to take advantage of the variety of services and opportunities available to support them and their families. UNLV alumni are encouraged to support the values of higher learning through advocacy, involvement, and giving.

 

More info on alumni activities as the College of Education.

 

 

UNLV COLLEGE OF EDUCATION ALUMNI CHAPTER

The UNLV College of Education Alumni Chapter Board of Directors is the driving force behind a strong relationship between UNLV’s College of Education, its alumni, and its friends. Through outreach, engagement, and philanthropy, our goal is to benefit the greater UNLV community and next generation of professionals. Our members represent many aspects of the educational field at all levels - classroom teachers, counselors and psychologists, human services professionals, researchers, subject specialists, and more.

 

More info on the COE Alumni Chapter and it's current directors.
summary: We offer programming and services to COE alumni, students, faculty, staff, and friends. #rebelsforever #unlvcoealumni

name: Generative Artificial Intelligence and Legal Technology Club
description:
The UNLV William S. Boyd School of Law Generative AI and Legal Technology Club is a
student organization formed under the auspices of the UNLV William S. Boyd School of
Law, dedicated to exploring the intersection of artificial intelligence, legal technology,
and the law. The Club aims to promote critical thinking, innovation, and dialogue on how
emerging technologies can transform legal practice, education, and policy. The Club is
committed to a technology-neutral stance, encouraging diverse perspectives on the
ethical, practical, and legal implications of AI and legal tech in society.
summary: Generative AI and Legal Technology Club is a student organization formed under the auspices of the UNLV William S. Boyd School of Law, dedicated to exploring the intersection of artificial intelligence, legal technology.

name: Student Support Club for Academic Success
summary:
Student Support Club for Academic Success aims to provide a space where students
can connect other students to academic resources and services and share their own
experiences using these valuable campus resources.

name: UNLV Study Abroad Club
summary: The University of Nevada Las Vegas Study Abroad Club aims to encourage and educate students about the opportunities and benefits of studying abroad as well as be an avenue for connection and community for study abroad alumni.

name: UNLV Music Business Club
description: The UNLV Music Business Club (UMBC) is an organization that aims to provide a space for musicians on campus to learn, network, and collaborate. Club activities include, but are not limited to, guest speakers from the industry, resume/mock interview workshops, songwriting and production contests, and club outings, among other activities. The organization will fill gaps in the music business discipline on campus whilst allowing students to grow and learn about the industry.
summary: UMBC aims to provide a space for musicians on campus to learn, network, and collaborate through guest speakers and workshops, as well as opportunities to show their talent through songwriting and production contests.

name: Latinx Faculty and Staff Alliance
description: The UNLV Latinx Faculty and Staff Alliance’s mission is to support the Latinx communities on and off- campus and enhance diversity initiatives at UNLV. Through the interdisciplinary assembly of its members, our affinity group aims to mentor, promote community-building events, and provide an ever-growing network to elevate student, staff, and faculty success. Finally, the group aims to offer a space, place, and voice to Latinx students, staff, and faculty, by communicating Latinx interests at the University level.
summary: An affinity group for Latino/a/x and Hispanic Faculty and Staff at UNLV.

name: UNLV Cosplay Club
summary: The purpose of this club shall be to provide a community for individuals interested in cosplay, costume creation, and related activities. The club aims to foster creativity, skill development, and camaraderie among its members.

name: UNLV Wind Orchestra to France 2025
description: This school year, we are changing our aim from achieving the best experience musically and professionally on a trip to France for our performance in the Festival d'Anches, to providing the best possible musical experience to the highest qualified musicians who auditioned and earned a spot in the internationally recognized Wind Orchestra; The UNLV Wind Orchestra.
summary: This organization's primary goal is to raise and obtain funding for the UNLV Wind Orchestra's 2025 France trip.

name: Student Organization for Learning and Research
description: We are a group of graduate students enrolled in the EPHLE department of the College of Education. Our focus is in supporting each other in the completion of our studies as we learn together and build our research proposals. Mentoring undergraduates and new Master's/Doctoral students by more senior students is highly encouraged as the shared experience is a highly valuable experience for all participants.
summary: Graduate student organization supporting Master's, Doctoral, and undergraduate students enrolled or interested in Educational Psychology, Learning Sciences, Higher Education, and other degree programs within the College of Education.

name: Las Vegas Iranian Students Association
description: The Las Vegas Iranian Students Association (LVISA) at UNLV aims to foster a deep appreciation of Iranian culture within the university community. We promote education, cultural exchange, and collaborative events to inspire a diverse and vibrant community that celebrates Iran's rich heritage. Open to all students and faculty, LVISA focuses on encouraging diversity and introducing Persian foods to the UNLV community. Through cultural events, educational programs, and culinary experiences, we strive to enrich the university experience and promote inclusivity. Join us to explore, celebrate, and share the richness of Iranian culture!
summary: The Las Vegas Iranian Students Association (LVISA) at UNLV aims to foster a deep appreciation of Iranian culture within the university community. We promote education, cultural exchange, and collaborative events to inspire a diverse community.

name: Sexual Health and Reproduction Education
summary: To provide comprehensive sexual health education to underserved communities and vulnerable populations in Las Vegas. We strive to focus on medically accurate, age-appropriate, and evidence-based information to reduce disparities in sexual health outcomes

name: UNLVPT Student Organization
description: We are a collection of the current doctoral UNLVPT students. Our mission is to enhance human health and quality of life through education, scholarly activity, and community service.
summary: We are a collection of the current doctoral UNLVPT students. Our mission is to enhance human health and quality of life through education, scholarly activity, and community service.

name: Actuarial Science Club
description:
For free study material for exam P and FM visit (https://www.theinfiniteactuary.com/).

we would love to have you stop by for a meeting to introduce yourself. 

Looking forward to seeing you!
summary: A place for STEM students to get together and study, to meet people who share the same interest's. Love all things STEM. (OPEN TO ALL)

name: Narcare
description:
Narcare at UNLV is a student organization that is dedicated to utilizing community empowerment to address the opioid crisis and substance abuse in the UNLV area, Las Vegas, and parts of Nevada. We want to provide harm reduction tools for substance use disorders in the form of education and Narcan distribution.  
⁣
Our efforts are guided by the fact that overdose deaths are preventable. Our goal is to provide everyone, especially students, with more information about the opioid epidemic. We hope to achieve this mainly through community involvement and discourse, which is a unique and novel method of fighting the opioid crisis.
⁣
Therefore, we will collaborate with organizations that are addressing these issues, such as local and state government agencies, universities, health clinics, homeless shelters, and many more. We hope to counter the despair and apathy associated with the seemingly inexorable opioid overdose crisis by providing a platform for students to share their feelings and experiences.
⁣
By empowering students, community members, businesses, and organizations with Narcan and overdose prevention awareness, we can reduce this needless loss of lives.
summary: Narcare is a student organization that is dedicated to community empowerment to address the opioid crisis and substance abuse in the Las Vegas area. We aim to provide education for students about effective harm reduction tools and Narcan use.

name: Percussion Society
summary: Working in association with the UNLV Percussion Studio, we aim to introduce students to the world of the percussive arts.

name: English Department Student Association
summary: The English Department Association (EDSA) are a group of graduate students in the English Department at UNLV working together to collectively advocate for all English graduate students in addition to community oriented events and peer-to-peer support.

name: Plaintiffs' Law Student Association
description: An association of law students dedicated to Plaintiff's law. This association hosts events which educate law students about the field of Plaintiff's side law, holds workshops designed to sharpen law students' professional skills, and create an environment where law students can foster professional relationships with current plaintiff's attorneys.
summary: An association dedicated to preparing the next generation of plaintiffs attorneys for professional excellence.

name: Rebel Recreational Volleyball Club
description:
RRVC is a community-oriented recreational volleyball club dedicated to fostering a love for the game in players of all skill levels. Our mission is to provide a fun, inclusive, and supportive environment where members can enjoy the sport, improve their skills, and build lasting friendships.

 

We offer a variety of programs, including weekly pick-up games, seasonal leagues, and skill development clinics, catering to everyone from beginners to seasoned players. Our club values sportsmanship, teamwork, and the joy of playing volleyball, making it a perfect place for both casual players and those looking to take their game to the next level.

 

Join us to stay active, meet new people, and share your passion for volleyball!
summary: RRVC offers a fun, inclusive environment for players of all levels. Enjoy weekly games, leagues, and clinics focused on skill-building and teamwork. Join us to play, improve, and connect!

name: UNLV Gaming Law Journal
description: The GLJ is a journal of legal scholarship dedicated to analyzing the law and policy implications of gaming case law, legislation, administrative regulations, and important gaming legal events. The GLJ is compromised of second-year, third-year, and fourth-year students, who are interested in gaming law.
summary: GLJ is published by the students of Boyd School of Law at UNLV in conjunction with the International Masters of Gaming Law.

name: UNLV ACF
description: Adventist Christian Fellowship (ACF) is the official organization for Adventist campus ministry on non-Adventist college and university campuses within the North American Division (NAD) of Seventh-day Adventists, including the US, Canada, Bermuda and Guam. Currently ACF is a network of over one hundred chapters across the North American Division.
summary: Reach the campus – Change the world

name: Intellectual Property Law Society
summary: IPLS aims to empower future legal professionals by providing resources and opportunities to engage with key issues and innovations shaping the field.

name: Division of Research
description: The Division of Research is committed to supporting and promoting a culture of excellence and integrity in research and creative/scholarly activity that benefits the university, community, and state. The UNLV Division of Research offers a variety of support services to our students, faculty, partners, and community through the following offices: Office of Sponsored Programs Office of Clinical Trials Office of Research Integrity Office of Research Infrastructure and Safety https://www.unlv.edu/research Read our strategic plan: https://www.unlv.edu/sites/default/files/media/document/2024-08/DOR-Strategic-Plan-Final-2024-Reduced-Approved.pdf https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEAA4DjqzXA
summary: The Division of Research is committed to supporting and promoting a culture of excellence and integrity in research and creative/scholarly activity that benefits the university, community, and state.

name: Student Life
description:
Mission Statement

Student Life serves and enriches the experience of students through student advocacy, inclusiveness, programming, holistic development, and its infrastructure.

Vision

Our vision is to provide every student with a sense of belonging, affirm diverse identities, and prepare students to be engaged leaders in a global society.
summary: Student Life includes programs and facilities that focus on the student experience — housing, recreation, student union, student diversity, student activities and organizations. It also has a Business Services Office.

name: BABE VOTE UNLV
description: BABE VOTE UNLV is a Gen-Z voter advocacy group focused on engaging young people in the political process and getting them to vote, but we don’t only want young people to participate in democracy, we also want them to lead the charge to fix democracy!
summary: BABE VOTE UNLV is a Gen-Z voter advocacy group focused on engaging young people in the political process and getting them to vote, but we don’t only want young people to participate in democracy, we also want them to lead the charge to fix democracy!

name: Hospitality Themed Housing Community
description: This community is designed for hospitality majors wishing to live and work with others who are also enrolled in the William F. Harrah College of Hospitality. It is a community of peers who have some of the same courses or who assist each other in studying for courses they have previously taken. Participants interact with faculty members in the hospitality college through programs that include mentoring opportunities, finding employment in the hospitality industry, internship opportunities, study groups, social events, and other leadership programs.
summary: It is a community of peers who have some of the same courses or who assist each other in studying for courses they have previously taken. Participants interact with faculty members in the hospitality college through programs that include mentorship

name: Engineering Thematic Housing Community
description: In partnership with the Howard R. Hughes College of Engineering Floor, this community has its very own computer lab outfitted with commonly-used software programs, such as CAD and C++, which can be accessed at any time. The Engineering theme community provides a cohort of future engineers to live, learn, problem solve, and socialize in a supportive environment.
summary: In partnership with the Howard R. Hughes College of Engineering Floor, this community has its very own computer lab outfitted with commonly-used software programs, such as CAD and C++, which can be accessed at any time.

name: First Year Experience Thematic Housing Community
description: This community offers a focused space to get and stay connected with the First-Year Experience (FYE) at UNLV. The FYE Team will work closely with students on this floor to facilitate a personalized experience aligned with personal and academic goals. The team will offer opportunities for students to connect with peer leaders, faculty and engage in structured and meaningful experiences available to support overall success in the first year.
summary: This community offers a focused space to get and stay connected with the First-Year Experience (FYE) at UNLV.

name: Stonewall Suites Thematic Housing Community
description: This community is designed to provide a safe, comfortable, and educational living and learning environment for LGBTQ+ students and their allies as well as being an inclusive environment for students who identify as any gender. All suites on this floor are gender inclusive and students can room with anyone of any gender. The floor has a large lounge space where students do many programs. Events include celebrating National Coming Out Day as well as marching in the Las Vegas Pride Parade.
summary: This community is designed to provide a safe, comfortable, and educational living and learning environment for LGBTQ+ students and their allies as well as being an inclusive environment for students who identify as any gender.

name: UNLV Harvard Latina Empowerment and Development Chapter
description:
The Harvard LEAD Conference was founded by Bianca Caban (Harvard Class of 2009) gathered a team of committed leaders within Harvard's Latina community and organized a conference dedicated to facilitating the entrance of young Latinas into the professional world. Latina Empowerment and Development Conference (LEAD) is a crucial conference for promoting development, expanding one's professional network, introducing mentoring opportunities, and inspiring womxn to reaffirm their commitment to their futures and goals.

The UNLV Harvard LEAD Chapter was founded in 2024 by Nicole Garcia-Contreras with the mission of empowering and creating a path of opportunity for Latina students at UNLV. The chapter’s mission is to recruit and prepare Latina students to attend the annual Harvard LEAD Conference. Throughout the year, we host workshops and activities designed to help members navigate professional environments during and after college. We also equip students with practical skills in fundraising, networking, and travel planning ensuring they are prepared for opportunities both on and beyond campus. The chapter is committed to creating an environment that promotes Latina leadership and empowerment.
summary: The UNLV Harvard Latina Empowerment and Development (LEAD) Chapter is an extension of the Harvard LEAD organization, based at Harvard College in Cambridge, Massachusetts. Our mission is to foster professional development, fundraising and travel.

name: Rebel Dance Club
description:
Our mission is to create a vibrant community at UNLV and in the larger Las Vegas area where members can learn, practice, and enjoy a wide range of partner and social dances, such as Bachata, Salsa, Merengue, Line Dance, Country Swing, and more.

We aim to promote student involvement through high-quality instruction, social dance events, and engaging activities, all while fostering an environment that is fun, safe, and inclusive. No partner or prior experience is required — everyone is welcome!

How To Join

✅ Click "Join" at the top of our Involvement Center page (this page)
✅ Fill out our Membership Form so we can get to know you better
✅ Follow us on our Instagram @rebeldanceclub for updates on our upcoming events
summary: The Rebel Dance Club is a fun, safe, and inclusive community where everyone can learn, practice, and enjoy partner and social dances like Bachata, Salsa, Merengue, Line Dance, Country Swing, and more. No partner or prior experience is required!

name: Recovery Evolved: Validating Individual Values and Experiences
description:
REVIVE aims to provide a comprehensive support system for students interested in exploring recovery by addressing their unique needs and promoting a balanced, healthy lifestyle, all while navigating their academics and identity as a college student. The program will offer a range of resources, workshops, and community-building activities designed to validate and support each individual’s values and experiences.

Get in touch with our organization through group me or email!

https://groupme.com/join_group/105507061/9h5E5z5h
summary: This RSO intends to create a supportive community for students who are interested in or are working on changing, reducing, or moderating their substance use and unwanted habits.

name: The Create for Kids Chapter at UNLV
summary: The UNLV Create for Kids Chapter strives to foster a nurturing environment for pediatric patients through art. UNLV Create for Kids assembles various kits, makes cards, and hosts donation drives to give to children in local hospitals and schools.

name: Policy and Legislation Society
description:
Our purpose 

  • Promote and educate law students about the importance of policy and legislation; 
  • Foster and encourage professional competence in Policy and Law; 
  • Improve the relationship between practicing attorneys, attorneys in office and law students;
  • Providing a platform for law students to pursue careers in Policy and Legislation; and 
  • Do all things necessary and appropriate to accomplish these purposes. 
  • Plan, coordinate and organize annual making the law competition.
summary: Our main mission is to support our members (law students) in the development of their legal careers and to provide opportunity in Policy, Law, Lobbying and Legislation. PALS plans, coordinates and organizes the annual making the law competition.

name: UNLV Association of Black Journalists
description: We, the members of the Association of Black Journalists (ABJ), who are striving for credible journalism that comprehensively portrays the voices and experiences of African Americans and people from the Black diaspora for a society and world that values them, do enact and establish this constitution for the governance of our members.
summary: The National Association of Black Journalists (NABJ) is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization that provides innovative, quality programs and services to its members and advocates on behalf of Black journalists and media professionals in the U.S. and worldwide

name: Office of the Vice President For Student Affairs
description: • The Division of Student Affairs is a dynamic environment characterized by shared leadership that fosters learning and human development. Working in partnership with the university and surrounding communities, Student Affairs is a service-oriented division that strives to establish a safe and secure learning environment that is diverse, dynamic, and student-centered. Areas of responsibility include: development and external relations, CAEO, Student life, Student Support and Advocacy, the UNLV Bookstore, UNLV Eats, and Student wellness
summary: Led by Vice President of Student Affairs Keith Rogers, the Division of Student Affairs supports students through the following departments and services: The Dr. William W. Sullivan Center for Academic Enrichment and Outreach, Student Life, ect.

name: Pre-Nursing Student Organization
description: This organization is open to all pre-nursing students at UNLV and/or students that might be interested in pursuing a nursing degree.
summary: This organization has been formed to engage pre-nursing students at UNLV to share information about the School of Nursing admissions process, share invites to events, hear from guest speakers, etc.

name: Rebel Trinkets
description: The Rebel Trinkets Club is dedicated to bringing together students at UNLV who share a passion for collecting, creating, curating, and celebrating meaningful items, regardless of size. Through trading and crafting unique trinkets, we foster creativity, community, and connection among students of all backgrounds.
summary: Rebel Trinkets at UNLV is a creative space for students to trade, collect, and make unique items. Join us to explore new treasures, connect with others, and express your creativity! All are welcome—no experience needed.

name: African-Americans In Gaming
description:
OUR VALUES:

• Collective Community: Build and nurture our community together, and collectively increase the strength, profile and opportunities for all. • Self- Reliance: Define ourselves, name ourselves, create for ourselves and speak for ourselves.

• Determination: Remain focused and driven in all that we do.

• Equality: Break through industry barriers to create more accessible opportunities and growth.

• Data Advocacy: Use data as evidence to demonstrate the impact of African Americans on gaming.

• Self-Advocacy: Use our voices, resources and influence to help others in all ways that we can.

• Positivity: Reflect a positive, optimistic and welcoming spirit with each other and our allies.
summary: Provide a sense of community, support and opportunity for African Americans in the gaming industry.

name: The Society of Health and Medical Physics
description: The organization aims to promote education in the field, create a space to share ideas, network with other medical physicists, and educate members on the professional aspects of the field
summary: The Society of Health and Medical Physics (SHMPS) is an organization for students interested in radiation physics and the medical uses of radiation. All graduate students are welcome to join.

name: UNLVPT Neurology Club
description: The UNLVPT Neurology Club seeks to promote education, awareness, and development in the field of neurological physical therapy through collaboration, guest speakers, field trips, workshops, and community outreach. Prospective members must be enrolled in the Doctorate of Physical Therapy degree track and be in good standing with the UNLV Department of Physical Therapy.
summary: The UNLVPT Neurology Club seeks to promote education, awareness, and development in the field of neurological physical therapy through collaboration, guest speakers, field trips, workshops, and community outreach.

name: Human Trafficking Prevention Club
description: Our mission is to raise awareness, educate, and advocate for the prevention of human trafficking within our community and beyond. We strive to empower students and faculty through knowledge and resources, fostering a culture of vigilance and compassion. We aim to create a safe and informed environment where every individual understands the signs of trafficking and feels empowered to take action against it.
summary: Working to educate and understand how and why human trafficking happens and how we can work towards ending our state being the highest rate of human trafficking.

name: The Las Vegas Punk Coalition
description: Welcome!! We are the Las Vegas Punk Coalition. We are dedicated to studying the antiracist, antifascist, and antibigotry roots of the punk movement and studying honoring the black forefathers of our movement. Without black musicians, without spirituals and blues, the punk movement and punk could NOT EXIST. The Las Vegas Punk Coalition is also dedicated to honoring the forefathers of our movement and our community with acts of service. In addition to karaoke and battle jaclet sewing, we will be performing regular food and clothing drives, as well as performing self-defense classes and teach ins designed to educate the public on history, music, and social justice.
summary: We are a student organization dedicated to the study of the antiracist and antibigotry roots of the punk movement, as well as dedicating ourselves to the study of music and to community service.

name: The American Concrete Institute
description: ACI is an international organization with networking and technical opportunities for students and professionals working with Concrete and related technologies. Civil and construction majors are encouraged to join us, but all concrete lovers are welcome! Some potential learning opportunities include learning to design concrete mixes, problem solving within the design process, developing connections within the construction industry, and understanding different technologies within the concrete industries and their uses.
summary: ACI is an international organization with networking and technical opportunities for students and professionals working with Concrete and related technologies. Civil and construction majors are encouraged to join us, but all concrete lovers are welcome!

name: Professional Convention Management Association
description:
The Professional Convention Management Association (PCMA) is a premier organization for students pursuing careers in event planning, hospitality, tourism, and business management. As a collegiate chapter, PCMA provides members with valuable industry exposure, hands-on experience, and professional development opportunities. Through networking events, workshops, guest speakers, and participation in national conferences, students gain insight into the dynamic world of meetings, conventions, and event management.

PCMA fosters leadership, teamwork, and career readiness by connecting students with industry professionals and offering real-world applications of event planning principles. Members have access to mentorship programs, internships, and exclusive resources that enhance their knowledge and skills. By joining PCMA, students build a strong foundation for success in the hospitality and events industry while forming meaningful connections with peers and professionals.
summary: A leading organization for students interested in event planning, hospitality, and business management. PCMA provides members with networking opportunities, hands-on experience, industry insights, professional development and leadership skills.

name: Campus Thrifts
description: Our general members join us for monthly meetings where we inform on sustainable brands, sourcing clothing, and even ways to make your own pieces. We also have "thrift meetups" around the city and introduce our members to small second hand businesses - they get to know the business' story and shop at the store. Our main involvement at UNLV is our weekly fundraisers! All members can volunteer for the fundraisers and profit from the fundraisers go to our events, sourcing our clothing, and at the end of every month we do a large donation to local non-profits.
summary: Campus Thrifts is a student-run thrift club that brings the second hand community to our college campus. Sustainable and high quality clothing is important. We have info-sessions, thrift meetups, and community involvement!

name: Mercy and Care Club
description:
The nonprofit Nevada Mercy and Care works to provide: employment placement, rental and utility assistance, housing placement, transportation assistance, provide food and clothing, social work services, mental health (psychiatric) services, primary care services, immigration services, translation services, ESL classes,  and many more services that are specific to each case and may vary depending on the needs of the individual. Majority of the individuals that we serve include but are not limited to people of refugee status. We serve anyone who comes to us for help regardless of socioeconomic status, race, religion, etc. We started out by serving 48 families in 2019, and now serve well over 100 families in 2024. 
 
Mercy and Care Club is essentially a chapter of the nonprofit Nevada Mercy and Care, in which members of the club can gain a tremendous amount of volunteer/ community service experience, as well as patient care hours as well. Personally as a pre professional student myself, I know first hand how hard it is to find places that allow you to gain patient care experience and volunteer hours as well. I would like for members of the club to be able to gain those experiences through our primary care, mental health, and ESL classes departments. Members can gain patient care and shadowing experiences on the days we do primary care services by taking the vital signs and blood pressures of our patients (training for this can be done in a vital signs workshop at one of our first meetings). Members can also gain unique volunteer experiences via participating in and possibly even teaching our ESL classes as well as our clothing and food distribution days. Overall, the club should serve as a unique way for students to gain experiences that will make them stand out on any graduate application, while also serving the community in a great and meaningful way!
-Laila El-Badry (President)
summary: A chapter of the nonprofit Nevada Mercy and Care, Mercy and Care Club (MCC) provides shadowing, patient care, and community service opportunities while being a part of a mission to help refugees and members of the community to have a better life!

name: Global Surgery Student Alliance
description:
For a very long time, surgery was not included in the discussion of how healthcare could be improved worldwide, especially in the most remote corners of the earth. Only recently has surgery been brought to the table, being called the “forgotten step-child” of global health by Paul Farmer and others. At the core, global surgery is "a field that aims to improve health and health equity for all who are affected by surgical conditions or have a need for surgical care, with a particular focus on underserved populations in countries of all income levels, as well as populations in crisis, such as those experiencing conflict, displacement, and disaster.” Important to this effort is all aspects of surgery, including anesthesia, OB/Gyn, and nursing.

​

Currently, 5 billion people around the world do not have adequate access to basic surgical care. Worse yet, the disparity in surgical care is stark, with the poorest 30% of the world receives only 3-6% of operations, whereas the richest 30% receives almost 75%. 17 million people die and 77 million DALYS are lost each year because of this, greater than HIV/AIDS, malaria, and TB combined. Our global economy could lose 21 trillion dollars between 2015 and 2030, with 12.3 trillion lost by low- and middle-income countries, so the lack of surgical care worldwide is also a significant detriment to the progress of humanity. 2.2 million more surgeons, anesthetists, and OB/Gyns (SAOs) and an investment of $350 billion dollars is needed to prevent this from happening, with a goal to raise the operative volume of countries to 5,000 per 100,000 population, meaning an increase of 143 million more surgical procedures per year. It can be easy to get lost in the numbers, but ultimately, health is a human right, and no one should have to live with a condition that would have been treated had they been born somewhere else.

​

So the bigger question becomes how we address this need for surgical care. In the past, this has almost exclusively involved efforts such as week-long surgical missions and the provision of money/resources in order to acutely work on the problem. However, the field has evolved with the creation of the Lancet Commission on Global Surgery, which called for a more in-depth analysis. Their major report from spring 2015 outlined an approach through investigation, innovation, and implementation, pointing out that research, technology, and capacity-building efforts were necessary to advance the field. In addition, they did a thorough economic analysis which showed that upscaling the number of surgeons and operations alone will not work - an economic approach that helps decrease catastrophic health expenditure for patients and their families is necessary. As the Lancet Commission encompasses almost 110 different countries, efforts have now been set on working at the highest level - with governments, health ministers, and financial officers - to enact changes in policy and infrastructure for whole countries to improve people’s access to surgical care.

​

Ultimately, whether you are interested in surgery/anesthesia/OBGyn, global/public health, economics/finance, policy, technology/engineering, education/empowerment, statistics/research, or any combination of these, you have a very significant contribution to make to this growing field. And no matter how young or old you are, no matter how little or how much you can contribute, your involvement in global surgery is incredibly necessary and valuable.

*Reference: Meara, J, Leather A, Hagander L, et al. Global surgery 2030: Evidence and solutions for achieving health, welfare, and economic development. Lancet. 2015;386:569-624.
summary: The Global Surgery Student Alliance is an organization created to increase awareness of global surgery needs, improve global surgery outcomes, and give advocacy to public health projects across the globe.

name: Artificial Intelligence in Business
description: The Artificial Intelligence Business Association (AIB) is dedicated to bridging the gap between academic learning and real-world AI applications, fostering an inclusive and dynamic community for students. By connecting members with faculty, industry professionals, and the Lee Business School administration, AIB provides a strong support network that extends beyond the classroom. With a focus on the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence, the organization offers expert-led workshops, guest lectures, and hands-on projects to equip students with practical AI knowledge and business applications. Through mentorship, career guidance, and networking opportunities, AIB prepares students to navigate the complexities of AI, enhancing their marketability and long-term value in an increasingly AI-driven world.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence in Business (AIB) Association equips students with practical AI knowledge and industry connections to apply AI-driven solutions in business through workshops, mentorship, and hands-on projects.

name: Project Management Association
description: The Project Management Association (PMA) is a student-driven organization dedicated to bridging the gap between academia and industry, equipping students with the skills, knowledge, and professional connections necessary for success in project management. PMA provides hands-on experiences, industry-recognized certifications, and exposure to cutting-edge project management practices, ensuring that members gain practical insights before graduation. Through networking with industry leaders, alumni, and faculty, students receive mentorship and career guidance that extend beyond the classroom. The organization also facilitates access to job and internship opportunities, hosting workshops, guest lectures, and career development events to prepare students for competitive roles in the field. By fostering a collaborative and professional learning environment, PMA empowers future project managers to lead with confidence and innovation.
summary: The Project Management Association (PMA) is a student-driven organization dedicated to bridging the gap between academia and industry, equipping students with the skills, knowledge, and professional connections necessary for success in project management

name: Off the Shelf: A Banned Book Club
shortName: Off the Shelf: A Banned Book Club
description: Off the Shelf: A Banned Book Club is a graduate student organization committed to fostering critical dialogue around banned, challenged, and censored books. Through regular discussions, events, and collaborative programming, we create an inclusive space for intellectual freedom, diverse perspectives, and the exploration of literature that has faced suppression. We aim to challenge censorship, amplify marginalized voices, and empower our academic community to engage deeply with the social, political, and cultural contexts of banned works.
summary: Off the Shelf: A Banned Book Club fosters dialogue on banned, challenged, and censored books. We champion intellectual freedom, amplify marginalized voices, and explore the cultural impact of literary censorship through events and discussion.

name: Black Social Workers Society
description:
We host meetings and events about:

- Social work/welfare history and information

- Black/African-centric history, culture, and social issue(s) education

- Information about UNLV's BSW and MSW programs

- Networking with the Black social work community of UNLV and Las Vegas

- Community service and volunteering initiatives

- Career preparation and education
summary: The Black Social Workers Society aims to connect and elevate students, particularly of the African diaspora, who are interested in social work and social justice. We provide opportunities for education, career preparation, service, and social connection.

name: The Hospitality Collective
description: We are a student-led community united by a passion for all facets of hospitality. Our mission is to foster a collaborative environment where students can learn, grow, and innovate together. Through direct engagement with industry professionals—via mentorship, networking events, workshops, and real-world insights—we aim to bridge the gap between academic learning and industry practice. By cultivating meaningful connections and embracing continuous learning, we empower the next generation of hospitality leaders to drive excellence and shape the future of the industry.
summary: A student-driven community dedicated to learning, collaboration, and innovation in hospitality—connecting with industry professionals to inspire excellence, foster growth, and shape the future of the industry.

name: Raising Our AANHPI Rights
description:
UNLV ROAR (Raising our AANHPI Rights) is a coalition of Asian-American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander student organizations representing the diverse AANHPI students on campus. ROAR dedicates its mission to empowering students with the leadership opportunities and social networking required to give their cultural heritage a voice in UNLV and Nevada affairs. This mission involves, but is not limited to, providing collaborative efforts among student organizations for networking, leadership & career development, mentoring, encouraging university awareness and education of AANHPI issues, and providing meaningful experiences for personal growth.

UNLV ROAR is committed to empowering students by providing opportunities to explore diverse career fields and connect with both emerging and established professionals. We aim to unify AANHPI-oriented registered student organizations on campus to foster a tight-knit, collaborative community that supports cultural identity, shared goals, and collective impact.

Through engaging events, advocacy initiatives, and leadership development, our organization strives to build an inclusive environment where every student feels welcome and inspired to make a difference.

 

Want to take the next step? Get involved by applying to one of our four dedicated committees:

Events & Programming Committee

Advocacy & Outreach Committee

Public Relations Committee

Career Development & Leadership Committee
summary: ROAR (Raising Our AANHPI Rights) dedicates its mission to empowering students with the leadership opportunities and social networking required to give their cultural heritage a voice in UNLV and Nevada affairs. Join us on our bi-weekly meetings!

name: The Forward Party at UNLV
description:
The Forward Party at UNLV is a nonpartisan student organization committed to fixing our broken political system through collaboration, civic engagement, and local action. We bring together students who care about making politics more accessible, less divisive, and more solutions-focused.

Our club brings together students, local leaders, and changemakers to tackle one of the biggest issues in politics today: voter disengagement and misinformation. Through weekly meetings, events, and campus-wide initiatives, we’re making politics more accessible and even fun. We host guest speakers, watch parties, debates, and open forums designed to empower students and spark real conversations.

Our mission is to fight misinformation, boost voter education, and push for long-term systemic reform—like ranked-choice voting, open primaries, and public campaign financing. We do this through weekly meetings, campus events, and a student-run local news initiative that breaks down Nevada politics in a way that’s simple, unbiased, and engaging.

Whether you’re interested in journalism, advocacy, debate, policy, or just want to be more informed, Forward is a space to learn, lead, and make a difference. No party loyalty required—just a passion for democracy.


Next Meeting: 
Thu Sep 11 @ BEH 222
Mon Nov 17 @ SU 207
Mon Dec 15 @ SU 207
Mon Jan 12 @ SU 218
Mon Feb 9 @ SU 205
Mon Mar 9 @ SU 207
summary: The Forward Party at UNLV is a nonpartisan student organization committed to fixing our broken political system through collaboration, civic engagement, independent media, and local action.

name: Rebel Career Ambassadors
description: The purpose of Rebel Career Ambassadors is to build a robust ecosystem of career-ready students by offering career readiness training and workshops; providing networking opportunities with peers, professionals, employers, and alumni; and fostering a supportive environment where students can practice, fail, explore, and grow professionally. The organization also offers peer-led support to help students navigate job searches, interviews, and the transition into the workforce.
summary: Rebel Career Ambassadors aims to prepare students for the workforce by offering career readiness training, peer support, and networking opportunities with professionals, employers, and alumni. We foster a supportive space for all.

name: Business Law Association of UNLV
description:
Business Law Association (BLA) of UNLV is a student-led organization dedicated to building a supportive and dynamic community for undergraduates interested in the intersections of law and business. Our mission is to connect aspiring legal and business professionals with industry experts, alumni, and current law students, providing members with valuable opportunities to grow academically and professionally.

We emphasize freedom of expression, the exchange of ideas, and mutual support among like-minded individuals who are committed to realizing their personal and professional potential. BLA offers access to law school preparation resources—including LSAT prep and internships—and fosters meaningful relationships within the professional legal network in Las Vegas. Together, we empower our members to thrive on their journey toward academic achievement and career success.
summary: The Business Law Association (BLA) at UNLV builds a community for undergrads interested in law and business, connecting them to resources, professionals, and networks to help them achieve academic and career success.”

name: UNLV Extended Realities
description: We are a student organization that is striving to share and showcase the development of Extended and Virtual Realities! We are hoping to showcase the magic that is XR/VR/AR!
summary: We are a student organization that is striving to share and showcase the development of Extended and Virtual Realities! We are hoping to showcase the magic that is XR/VR/AR!

name: Maternal Child Health Student Organization
description:
The UNLV Maternal and Child Health Student Organization (MACHSO) is an interdisciplinary student-led group committed to advancing maternal and child health in Southern Nevada. Open to all actively enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at UNLV, MACHSO also welcomes participation from alumni, faculty, and community members. The organization provides a collaborative platform to promote leadership, mentorship, education, and advocacy around maternal and child health issues.

Rooted in values of accessibility, equity, holistic care, and ethical, evidence-based practices, MACHSO organizes events, community service activities, and educational opportunities throughout the year. Members are encouraged to participate in at least two meetings annually and one special event per semester to remain active.

MACHSO is governed by an executive board of dedicated student leaders who coordinate the organization’s events, communication, finances, and outreach efforts. The group works closely with a UNLV faculty advisor and operates in full compliance with university policies. MACHSO empowers students to make a meaningful impact on public health while fostering professional growth and community engagement.
summary: MACHSO is a student-led organization at UNLV promoting maternal and child health through education, advocacy, service, and leadership. It welcomes students and partners to engage in community-based efforts to improve health outcomes in  Southern Nevada.

name: Miracle Oral Health Initiative
summary: Miracle Oral Health Initiative is a student-led organization committed to improving oral health outcomes through education, prevention, and access. We aim to empower underserved communities with oral hygiene knowledge and supplies, while also providing m

name: The Lee Advisory Board
summary: The Lee Business School Advisory Board (LAB) connects student organizations, college leadership, and alumni to foster collaboration, leadership, and engagement across the undergraduate business community.

name: Anatomy Academy
description: Anatomy Academy is a college student–led outreach program designed to inspire curiosity and learning in anatomy, physiology, and healthy living. Our volunteers visit elementary classrooms to deliver fun, interactive, and age-appropriate lessons about the human body and wellness. Through hands-on activities, movement, and discussion, we aim to foster excitement for science and encourage lifelong healthy habits—especially in schools that may have limited access to STEM enrichment opportunities. Each visit lasts one hour and is led by trained college mentors who work in small groups with students to maximize engagement and learning.
summary: Anatomy Academy is a college student-led outreach program where students will visit elementary schools once a week for an hour and teach a basic interactive anatomy/wellness lesson to get kids excited about the STEM field.

name: Pocket Battle Bots
description: Pocket Battle Bots is an organization that abides by Southern California Attack Robots (SCAR) rule set. Our organization is centered around the design, construction, and competition of fairyweight (1LB) combat robots comprised of 3D printed components. We assist our members in the CAD design, electrical assembly, and operation of their robots to compete in scheduled events against fellow students.
summary:
The purpose of this organization is to promote hands-on engineering, creative
design, and friendly competition through the construction and battling of small scale
3D printed combat robots to compete in sanctioned battles in an enclosed arena.

name: Interfaith RSO
description: Interfaith RSO will work to foster understanding, appreciation, and respect of religions, spiritual paths, and cultures represented at UNLV through creating safe and respectful conversations, learning about our similarities and differences, and addressing shared community issues with the ultimate goal of building bridges and sharing our goals of supporting thriving communities at UNLV and beyond.
summary: Interfaith RSO works to foster understanding, appreciation, and respect of religions, spiritual paths, and cultures represented at UNLV.

name: Rebel Freestyle
description: Rebel Freestyle is a student club for anyone interested in freestyle footbag (hacky sack tricks) and/or freestyle football (soccer ball tricks). We create a fun, supportive space to practice, learn new skills, and grow together in these dynamic sports. No experience necessary, all skill levels welcome! Come kick it with us!
summary: A student club for anyone interested in freestyle footbag (hacky sack tricks) and/or freestyle football (soccer ball tricks). A fun, supportive space to learn and practice. No experience necessary, all skill levels welcome! Come kick it with us!

name: Papa Hana No'eau
summary: Hana No'eau means to strive for skillfulness and talent. Papa is a class to learn these talents. Hawaiians used it to help master arts and crafts, but we hope to learn more than just Hawaiian arts & incorporate all of Polynesia.

name: Alpha Lambda Delta
description:
• Alpha Lambda Delta is a national honor society.

  • It recognizes academic excellence among first-year college students.

  • Founded in 1924 at the University of Illinois.

  • Over 280 chapters exist across the United States.

  • More than one million members have been inducted.

  • Membership is by invitation only.

  • Eligibility requires a 3.5 GPA or higher during the first full-time term.

  • ALD promotes intellectual growth, leadership, and service.

  • Members gain access to scholarships, networking, and leadership opportunities.
summary: Alpha Lambda Delta is a national honor society recognizing academic excellence in first-year college students. With 280+ chapters and 1M+ initiates, ALD promotes scholarship, leadership, and meaningful career growth.

name: BioLaw & Health Policy Forum
description:
Our mission is to bridge the gap between medicine and law by promoting student-led publishing alongside ethical dialogue focused around health policy, medical law and bioethics.

If you're interested in joining, please complete the following google form! https://forms.gle/23kfYatA99q42EwU9
summary: Our mission is to bridge the gap between medicine and law by promoting student-led publishing alongside ethical dialogue focused around health policy, medical law and bioethics.

name: Catholic Medical Association
summary: Novus Medicus is a community of aspiring and early-career medical professionals, who strive to be faithful Catholics and excellent in their medical craft. Members support each other in growing professionally, spiritually and personally.

name: International University Bible Academy
description: The International University Bible Academy (IUBA) is a Christian organization dedicated to promoting positive engagement through Bible studies, seminars, and community service initiatives. IUBA also supports its members and other students to excel in scholastic activities.
summary: The International University Bible Academy is a Christian organization focused on organizing positive activities, bible studies, and seminars for students, as well as engaging in community service.

name: Christian Medical Dental Association
description: The Christian Medical Dental Association exists to form and support current and future physicians, empowering them to live and promote the principles of the all forms of Christian Faith in the science and practice of medicine.
