Additionally, it strives to inspire physicians to imitate Jesus Christ as the model of authentic healing and service. The Christian Medical & Dental Associations (CMDA)'s mission is to educate, encourage, and equip Christian healthcare professionals to glorify God. They achieve this by encouraging professionals to follow Christ, serve with excellence and compassion, care for all people, and advance Biblical principles within the Church and throughout the world. The CMDA also seeks to "change hearts in healthcare" be reinforcing the need to address spiritual needs for patients within all stages of healthcare.
summary: The Christian Medical & Dental Associations® (CMDA) is made up of the Christian Medical Association (CMA) and the Christian Dental Association (CDA). CMDA provides resources, networking opportunities, education and a public voice for Christian healthcare

name: ACM SIGGRAPH @ UNLV
description: Our ACM SIGGRAPH student chapter is a community of learners, creators, and innovators passionate about computer graphics and interactive technologies. We aim to provide opportunities for students to explore cutting-edge topics in visual computing, animation, rendering, XR, and digital art. Through workshops, guest talks, competitions, and research initiatives, we foster technical growth, creative expression, and professional development. Our mission is to connect students with industry and academic leaders while cultivating a space where ideas, creativity, and technology come together to shape the future of interactive media.
summary: A student-led ACM SIGGRAPH chapter focused on exploring computer graphics and interactive technologies through research, collaboration, and creative practice, fostering innovation and community engagement in visual computing.

name: Rebel Sketch Comedy
description: Rebel Sketch Comedy is UNLV’s student-led comedy club dedicated to writing, producing, and screening original sketch content. Each week features collaborative writing sessions, producing sketches, and improv! Anyone is always welcome to drop in!
summary: Rebel Sketch Comedy is UNLV’s student-led comedy club dedicated to writing, producing, and screening original sketch content. Each week features collaborative writing sessions, producing sketches, and improv! Anyone is always welcome to drop in!

name: Rebels for Randi
summary: Rebels for Randi is an organization for all UNLV students to come together in hopes of creating a new UNLV mascot, Randi the Red Rock. Through our support of Randi, we will support each other’s personal and professional development so that we can all bec

name: Black Screen and Stage
description: Black Screen and Stage is a student-led organization that uplifts and empowers Black creatives at UNLV through screen acting, filmmaking, and theatrical performance, fostering a community rooted in expression, collaboration, and representation.
summary: Black Screen and Stage is a student-led organization that uplifts and empowers Black creatives at UNLV through screen acting, filmmaking, and theatrical performance, fostering a community rooted in expression, collaboration, and representation.

name: Bit to Byte
summary: Bit to Byte is focused on helping students gain professional software development skills, while working with development teams using industry standard procedures. We also offer software services such as site development, and maintenance.

name: National Association of Hispanic Journalists @ UNLV
description:
NAHJ @ UNLV is dedicated towards supporting Latinos in media within the UNLV community. Members will be exposed to career opportunities within Las Vegas that will boost their professional life. 

As a part of NAHJ @ UNLV, you will:

- Build a team: Meet journalists, students, and other media professionals who can open doors to internships, jobs, and collaborations.

- Learn and grow: Be part of a national and state organization that offers workshops, scholarships, and training that can help you.

- Thrive on campus: NAHJ @ UNLV helped provide media coverage of a Raiders event in the spring, with more to come!

Members do not need to be of Hispanic descent to join NAHJ @ UNLV — if you're passionate about making a positive impact in the community, we’d love to have you with us!
summary: NAHJ @ UNLV supports Latino students pursuing a career in media. Members will sharpen their professional life by working in a team and getting a hands-on experience in the journalism field.

name: Ultimate Frisbee
description:
UNLV Ultimate Frisbee wants to grow the sport of ultimate frisbee at UNLV and across the Las Vegas Area. We work closely with Las Vegas Ultimate (LVU) to fulfill this mission.

More information about spirit of the game can be found here: https://usaultimate.org/spirit-of-the-game/

More about ultimate frisbee can be found here: https://usaultimate.org/discover-ultimate/
summary: Ultimate Frisbee at UNLV is dedicated to growing the sport of ultimate frisbee at UNLV by providing students with the opportunity to play at all levels. We welcome players of all skill level!

name: Fine Arts Thematic Community
description:
For students passionate about the arts, the College of Fine Arts has created an exclusive community just for you. This community provides the opportunity to meet, study, and connect with like-minded students who share similar interests in Architecture, Film, Theatre, Art, Dance, Music, and Entertainment Engineering. The CFA offers a unique and innovative artists' community. Here, members will be nurtured as well-rounded artists who learn, appreciate, and understand all art forms rather than limiting their learning to a single discipline or specialization. Participants will engage with a diverse array of art forms, viewpoints, ideas, emotions, and creative expressions from peers, faculty, and professionals across various disciplines. This exposure will enhance your awareness, expertise, skills, and creativity, allowing you to seamlessly integrate theories, practices, and insights from multiple art disciplines into your own creative work or performance. As a result, you will emerge as a well-informed and more experienced artist with multifaceted abilities and perspectives. The environment will be caring, energetic, and vibrant, where every student feels a sense of belonging and is empowered to reach their full potential as a human being and a unique artist.
Don't wait any longer; join this community and experience the joy of living the Fine Arts way!
summary: In partnership with the College of Fine Arts, this floor provides collaborative spaces and a supportive community of like-minded peers, where students can cultivate their talents and engage in artistic expression.

name: Drone Club
description:
The goal of our club is to host educational meetings to learn how to build and fly drones. We aspire to partner with other groups and sponsors to host races and other competitions. Everyone's ideas are accepted and all skill levels are welcome!

 

📆 Semester Roadmap

Month 1 – Kickoff

  • First Meetup (Intro + Flight Day)

    • Location: UNLV open field

    • Agenda: intros, safety briefing, free flight, group photo/video.

  • Poll + Officer Sign-ups (use Discord reactions).

  • Set Regular Meeting Time (biweekly or monthly).

Month 2 – Skill & Community

  • Workshop #1: Drone Basics & Safety

    • FPV goggles demo, FAA rules, battery care.

    • Run by advanced members for beginners.

  • Club Social / Study Night

    • Casual hangout to build community.

  • Fundraiser #1: FPV Demo Booth on Campus

    • Let students view FPV goggles.

    • Accept donations / small ticket fee.

Month 3 – Showcase & Fundraising

  • Workshop #2: Cinematic Drone Flying

    • Smooth shots, framing, editing basics.

  • Fundraiser #2: Drone Photoshoot Day

    • Offer aerial group/grad photos.

    • Market to UNLV students.

  • Collab Event

    • Join with another club (engineering, film, gaming) for cross-exposure.

Month 4 – Growth & Next Steps

  • Club Flight Jam / Mini Race

    • Fun freestyle race or obstacle course.

  • Fundraiser #3: Merch or 3D-Printed Keychains

    • Use Makerspace to create drone-themed merch.

  • Officer Check-In

    • Review what worked, prep for next semester.

Ongoing Monthly Goals

  • Social media posts (clips from meetups).

  • 1 educational workshop or demo.

  • 1 community/fundraising activity.

  • Consistent flight meetups.
summary: We build, fly, and try not to crash drones. Racing drones, cinematic, and toy-grade, etc. Everyone's ideas are accepted and all skill levels are welcome!

name: Womxn of Color Collective - Las Vegas
description:
The Womxn of Color Collective is dedicated to creating a safe space and a community within the William S. Boyd School of Law where the unique experiences, opinions, challenges, and successes of womxn and womxn of color are shared.  

We are a collective of individuals who identify as womxn, womxn of color, or allies. We are open to all people ready to engage in an open and honest dialogue about race, gender and the law. Our mission is to promote the empowerment, inspiration, and personal and professional development of womxn and people of color in the legal field. 

We begin from the perspective that the institution of law does not understand, acknowledge, or make room for the experiences, opinions, and world-views of womxn and people of color. It further actively defines what it means to be a womxn and a person of color in a way that does violence to those identities.  We seek to re-frame what it means to be a womxn and a person of color both from within and outside of the law.  

We spell womxn with a "x" as opposed to the common way of spelling it with an “e” because we want to assert ourselves and our views as womxn, consciously rejecting the patriarchal norms that permeate the legal field and society.
summary: The Womxn of Color Collective is dedicated to creating a safe space and a community within the William S. Boyd School of Law where the unique experiences, opinions, challenges, and successes of womxn and womxn of color are shared.

name: Flintknapping Club
description: A relaxed club to practice hands-on methods of stone tool manufacture with current and past technologies.
summary: A relaxed club to practice hands-on methods of stone tool manufacture with current and past technologies.

name: Las Vegas Voices of Victory
description:
The Las Vegas Voices of Victory's mission statement is as follows:

We exist to share the love of Christ through the Power of Gospel Music, to create a vibrant and supportive community, and to provide a joyful space for the expression of faith, unity, and spiritual growth.
summary: This organization will function as a UNLV community choir! We aim to create a safe space and promote artistic freedom through music.

name: Howell Town Theme Community
description: Named after John Howell, the first African-American to own land in Clark County, Nevada, Howell Town aims to create an environment that focuses on the history, prosperity, and enrichment of the African and Black Diaspora communities. Through the use of faculty, programming, and campus resources students will learn through personal and group discovery around issues of identity development, race, class, gender, and social norms. Howell Town is open to students of all backgrounds and identities.
summary: Howell Town aims to create an environment that focuses on the history, prosperity, nad enrich ment of the African and Black Diaspora communities.

name: Diamond Campus Ministry
summary: Christian based club set out to build a community, create friendships and strengthen campus culture.

name: Rebel Quantitative Finance Association
description:
The Rebel Quantitative Finance Association (RQFA) is a student-run association at the Lee Business School created to connect UNLV’s Master of Science in Quantitative Finance (MSQF) community with the broader Las Vegas business ecosystem. Our mission is twofold: (1) showcase the analytical rigor and industry relevance of the MSQF program to local employers, and (2) equip students from all majors who are passionate about data-driven finance with the practical skills and professional network needed to thrive in quantitative roles.

To achieve this mission, the club pursues four core objectives:

  1. Industry Outreach – We promote the MSQF program and its talent pipeline by hosting joint events with Las Vegas companies, arranging site visits, and collaborating on applied research or capstone projects that demonstrate the value of quantitative finance in real-world decision making.
  2. Skill Development – Through hands-on workshops and coding labs, members gain proficiency in programming (Python, R, MATLAB), statistical modeling, machine learning, and computational finance techniques that align with current market demands.
  3. Expert Engagement – The club invites portfolio managers, quants, FinTech founders, and academic researchers to deliver guest lectures, lead case studies, and mentor project teams—bridging classroom concepts with professional practice.
  4. Networking & Community – Regular socials, panel discussions, and résumé-review sessions foster peer-to-peer learning and help members build the confidence and connections necessary for internships and full-time placements.

By blending rigorous technical training with active industry engagement, RQFA positions UNLV students to become job-ready quantitative finance professionals while enhancing the visibility of our program across Southern Nevada’s growing financial and technology sectors.
summary: The RQFA is a student-led organization at UNLV that connects the MSQF program and any student interested in data-driven finance to Las Vegas employers, as well as all the other employers across the US.

name: The UNLV Fandom Fellowship
description:
The UNLV Fandom Fellowship celebrates popular culture! Each month we host a discussion, a study & screening session, and an interactive activity - allowing us to foster community, creativity, and connection through shared fandoms.

The UNLV Fandom Fellowship is a student organization devoted to the celebration of popular culture, mainstream media, and interactive community engagement. Rather than centering exclusively on specific media franchises, the club embraces a broad range of themes - spanning from childhood favorites, gaming culture, iconic film and television, and other cultural phenomena! Each month, the organization strives to unify and balance both recreational enjoyment and beneficial enrichment. We do this by three primary means:

  • A Discussion Forum: a guided conversation or debate on a topic related to the monthly theme.

  • Study & Screening Session: a quiet, collaborative environment combining academic study with a film or episode screening.

  • Interactive Activity: a hands-on, participatory event such as crafts, trivia, or some sort of miscellaneous activity.

Additional meetings and socials will likely follow, fostering a consistent rhythm of engagement throughout the academic year. The UNLV Fandom Fellowship’s mission is to cultivate a welcoming and inclusive space where students can connect through shared cultural experiences while maintaining opportunities for productivity and creative exploration. We provide an outlet for students to strengthen community bonds and celebrate the joy of fandom in all its diverse forms!
summary: The UNLV Fandom Fellowship celebrates popular culture! Each month we host a discussion, a study & screening session, and an interactive activity - allowing us to foster community, creativity, and connection through shared fandoms!

name: UNLV Orientation
description: New Student Orientation will help you make a successful transition to college. You'll connect with future classmates, learn about the services available to help you succeed, and explore opportunities to get involved. We look forward to supporting you along the way to ensure you are ready for your first semester at UNLV.
summary: Orientation department oversees the transition of all incoming students, whether they are first year, transfer, or online only students, to their journey as a UNLV student.

name: The Coaching Spot
description: The Coaching Spot is the central hub for Student Success Coaching, a free resource available to all undergraduate students. Our mission is to support students in achieving their academic, personal, and professional goals through one-on-one coaching and skill development. Our trained student success coaches are here to help you build effective strategies and strengthen key soft skills that lead to long-term success. Whether you're looking to improve your time management, enhance study habits, set achievable goals, stay motivated, or overcome procrastination, we're here to guide and support you every step of the way. We’re also proud to be home to the Rebels Rise program, where students can connect not only with a coach but also with a peer mentor. This added layer of support helps students build community, stay accountable, and feel more connected throughout their academic journey. But The Coaching Spot is more than just a place for academic support—it's a welcoming space designed to help you feel encouraged and focused. While you're here, take advantage of our free ramen bar and snack station, available to all students at no cost. Whether you’re meeting with a coach, working on assignments, or just need a break between classes, The Coaching Spot is a space designed with your success in mind. Stop by, grab a snack, and let’s work together to build the path toward your success.
summary: The Coaching Spot is home to Student Success Coaching—free support for all undergrads! Get help with study skills, time management, goal setting & more. Plus, free snack station & ramen bar while you work on your success.

name: Korean Social Club
description: The Korean Social Club at UNLV is a student organization dedicated to celebrating Korean culture through food, music, language, and community events, creating friendships and cultural exchange on campus.
summary: The Korean Social Club at UNLV is a student organization dedicated to celebrating Korean culture through food, music, language, and community events, creating friendships and cultural exchange on campus.

name: Class Action Student Education Society
description:
The Class Action Student Education Society (CASES) gives Boyd Law students the opportunity to get involved in the field of complex litigation, with a focus on class actions and mass torts. Our mission is to provide students with meaningful opportunities to learn about this practice area through speakers, events, and community engagement. By connecting students with practitioners and faculty, CASES fosters both education and involvement, helping prepare future advocates to understand and contribute to large-scale litigation and its role in advancing justice. 

Every CASE Matters—Because Justice Is a Class Act.
summary: The Class Action Student Education Society (CASES) gives Boyd Law students opportunities to explore complex litigation. The society educates future practitioners about class actions and mass torts through speakers, events, and community engagement.

name: UNLV Student Athlete Advisory Commitee
description: We strive to amplify the student-athlete voice in the development of policies, cultivate a united athletics community across all teams, and represent student-athlete interests in campus-wide initiatives. Through organized community service, active participation in legislative discussions, and collaboration with university leadership, we are committed to promoting a positive and impactful student-athlete experience and image both on campus and in the broader community.
summary: The UNLV Student-Athlete Advisory Committee is dedicated to fostering open communication between student-athletes, campus and athletics administration, ensuring the timely dissemination of information and providing meaningful feedback to athletes.

name: UNLV SDM Class of 2028
summary: UNLV School of Dental Medicine's Class of 2028 student council.

name: Rebel Clay
description: Interested in joining a group of passionate ceramicists dedicated to expanding their artistry and learn more about the world of clay? Rebel Clay is the RSO for you! Rebel Clay is a student organization that aims to provide a healthy learning environment and creative outlet for people of all skill levels at UNLV to learn about and work with clay. During our meetings we provide hands - on clay time, mini demonstrations and workshops, creative social time, and so much more to foster an artistic and educational setting for those interested in the art of ceramics.
summary: Rebel Clay is an open - major student organization for anyone interested in honing in on their skills working with clay, learning pottery and building techniques, and exploring the art of ceramics!

name: Love Yourself Foundation
description: The Love Yourself Foundation (LYF), at its core, is about health and wellness. LYF believes that loving yourself is fundamental to achieving mental wellness. When you love and accept yourself, you cultivate a positive relationship with your mind and body, and this enhances your overall well-being. Our programs and workshops (e.g., Love Your Self-Expression open mic nights, sound bath meditations, mix and mingles, live painting events, etc) aim to equip individuals with the necessary tools and knowledge to develop self-compassion, self-awareness, and self-care practices. Additionally, we recognize that human beings are social creatures and that our relationships with others are crucial to our wellness. LYF fosters a community that supports and uplifts one another, where individuals can connect and build meaningful relationships. Thus, we strive to create a safe space for people to share their experiences and stories, and to learn how to support others on their mental health journeys.
summary: The Love Yourself Foundation (LYF) promotes self-love practices to help those searching for personal enhancement through art, science, creativity and more.

name: St. Thomas More Society of Boyd Law at UNLV
description:
The St. Thomas More Society of Boyd Law at UNLV is a Catholic-focused, faith-welcoming organization open to all law students interested in the relationship between faith and the law.

The purpose of the Society is to encourage Catholic and Christian law students, as well as those of other beliefs, to live out their vocation as future legal professionals through service. To that end, the Society sponsors the annual Red Mass to bless legal professionals, organizes frequent service events where law students can earn pro bono hours, and connects students with Catholic and Christian practitioners in the community.

Together, we ask God to bless, strengthen, and enlighten all servants of the law, so that in solidarity and mutual trust we may work for justice and serve the common good.
summary: The St. Thomas More Society of Boyd Law at UNLV is a Catholic-focused, faith-welcoming group open to all, dedicated to service as future legal professionals through pro bono work in the community.

name: Literacy for Youth
summary: Literacy for Youth is a student-led, non-profit organization that seeks to introduce and strengthen life skills to students of all youth ranges, through interactive meetings, hands-on service events, and engaging community activities.

name: UNLV Chapter of SkillsUSA
description:
Ready to level up your college experience? 🚀UNLV Chapter of SkillsUSA is where passion meets opportunity. We’re a national organization that helps students turn their skills into careers, their leadership into impact, and their connections into lifelong friendships.

Here’s what you’ll get when you join:
🔥 Compete in exciting state and national competitions that put your talents to the test.
🔥 Travel to conferences across the country and represent UNLV on the big stage.
🔥 Meet industry leaders, recruiters, and students from all over the U.S.
🔥 Build leadership skills, boost your resume, and stand out after graduation.
🔥 Be part of a supportive, driven community that celebrates your wins.

No matter what your major is, there’s a place for you here. UNLV Chapter of SkillsUSA isn’t just another student org… it’s a launchpad for your future
summary: SkillsUSA UNLV is where passion meets opportunity! 🚀 Compete, travel, lead, and connect with students + industry leaders nationwide. All majors are welcome! This is your launchpad to sharpen skills, boost your résumé, and join a winning community.

name: UNLV Cricket Club
description:
The UNLV Cricket Club is a vibrant and inclusive community for students passionate about the game of cricket, whether you're a seasoned player or just getting started. We provide opportunities for competitive matches, skill development, and social engagement throughout the academic year.

Our club participates in Nevada Cricket Association leagues, friendly fixtures, ensuring year-round cricketing action. Regular friendly games will be played on every weekend with a focus on fitness, technique, and team strategy.

Beyond the pitch, the club offers a strong social environment with events, watch parties, and networking opportunities, creating lasting friendships among members from diverse backgrounds.

Join us to learn the game, improve your game, and be part of a team that values sportsmanship, teamwork, and a shared love for cricket.
summary: The UNLV Cricket Club is a vibrant and inclusive community for students passionate about the game of cricket, whether you're a seasoned player or just getting started. We provide opportunities for competitive matches, skill development, and social.

name: Lōkahi Theme Community
description:
Deepen your understanding and appreciation of Pacific Islander history, traditions, languages, and customs to promote cross-cultural respect and inclusivity. Foster a supportive community where Pacific Islander students can connect with their heritage and feel a strong sense of belonging while encouraging broader cultural appreciation among residents.

While you must be a UNLV student to join, you do NOT need to be an on campus resident to join and participate.
summary: Welcome to all, this community is dedicated to honoring, celebrating, and sharing the Pacific Islands’ rich heritage with pride to foster a united, supportive, and inclusive environment for those who are close and far from home.

name: UNLV Kendo
description:
Kendo is the modern practice of Japanese swordsmanship, blending martial discipline and mental focus. Using bamboo swords, we train and develop precision, timing, and respect through structured drills. Eventually, practitioners can expect to wear protective armor and engage in full-contact sparring.

Our club welcomes all skill levels! No gear, or experience needed. Be sure to get in contact with us over on Discord!

Practice times:
SRWC room 2040
Tuesday & Thursday 4:30-5:45

SRWC room 2025
Saturday 5:00-7:00
summary: Sharpen your spirit and body through Kendo, the modern art of Japanese swordsmanship. Build focus, confidence, and control through disciplined practice and sparring. Beginners are welcome!

name: UNLV Animation Club
summary: The UNLV Animation club is a place where students can discuss both art and animation. We plan on organizing and working on club-made films, working together to advance our skills in art and animation.

name: K-pop Club
description:
K-pop Club is a space where fans of K-pop can make new friends, participate in activities, and have interesting discussions. There are also opportunities to learn choreographies and film K-pop covers. This organization aims to create a strong and welcoming community for all students through a shared interest in K-pop. Join our BAND: 
https://band.us/n/a7a5b41bZ2R23
summary: K-pop Club is open to all students looking to meet people with similar interests. It is a space where K-pop fans can come together, have fun, and build friendships!

name: Family, Career and Community Leaders of America
description: Family, Career and Community Leaders of America (FCCLA) is a national Career and Technical Student Organization dedicated to developing youth leadership, career readiness, and community engagement through Family and Consumer Sciences education. Members gain practical skills, participate in competitive events, and engage in service initiatives that prepare them for success in college, careers, and beyond. Watch our inspiration-packed promo here:
summary: FCCLA is a national Career and Technical Student Organization that prepares youth for leadership, careers, and community service through Family and Consumer Sciences education, personal growth, and hands-on experiences.

name: UNLV School of Dental Medicine Sports Club
description: The UNLV School of Dental Medicine Sports Club is engaged in introducing and fostering sports of all varieties to the student, faculty, and alumni populations at the UNLV School of Dental Medicine. The club shall commit to providing reasonable (financially, logistically, socially) outings to build rapport between said populations and should offer an opportunity for physical fitness amongst the demanding requirements of dental school.
summary: The UNLV SDM Sports Club is committed to fostering connections between students, faculty, and alumni at the School of Dental Medicine. By providing sports opportunities for these populations, we hope to promote physical fitness into our busy schedules.

name: Tau Sigma National Honor Society Kappa Eta UNLV Chapter
description:
Tau Sigma National Honor Society, Kappa Eta Chapter honors exceptional transfer students at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas who have demonstrated academic excellence, resilience, and commitment to their educational journey.

Grounded in the values of scholarship, leadership, service, and fellowship, the chapter cultivates a strong and inclusive community while providing meaningful opportunities for leadership development, campus involvement, and service. Tau Sigma empowers transfer students to grow, connect, and make a lasting impact at UNLV and beyond.
summary: Tau Sigma National Honor Society Kappa Eta Chapter recognizes and supports high-achieving transfer students at UNLV through leadership development, academic excellence, and community engagement.

name: By The People
description: Our mission is to strengthen civic engagement by connecting community service with policy action. Volunteering to identify local challenges and working with government officials to make tangible improvements.
summary: Our mission is to strengthen civic engagement by connecting community service with policy action.

name: Student Parents of UNLV
description:
Student Parents of UNLV supports enrolled UNLV students who are parents or caregivers. The organization creates community and peer support for students balancing academics, caregiving responsibilities, employment, and campus life. It provides a space for connection, shared problem solving, and mutual support among student parents across disciplines and family stages. Members also have opportunities to build professional networks, develop meaningful relationships, and strengthen connections across campus and the broader community.

 



 

The organization also focuses on advocacy and awareness of the unique barriers student parents face, including childcare access, scheduling constraints, financial strain, and academic continuity. Through collaboration with campus offices and student services, the organization works to improve access to resources and promote policies and practices that support student parent persistence, academic progress, and well being.

 

 

 


The Student Parents of UNLV org is committed to building sustainable, long term support initiatives that strengthen retention and success for student parents at UNLV.

 

 

 

 

 

Allies and friends who support student parents are also welcome to join and participate in the organization. Physical attendance is not required for membership, and students can engage in ways that fit their schedules and responsibilities.
summary: SPU supports enrolled students who are parents or caregivers. The org builds community, peer support, and advocacy around balancing academics and parenting. SPU connects members to campus resources and works toward long term support initiatives.

name: Queer Film Club
description:
QFC aims to create a setting that promotes learning, mutual understanding, and diversity through LGBTQIA+ films.

 We welcome ALL to join our club to learn more about the historical context, influence, and impact of these films, while creating meaningful connections with others and a sense of belonging and community on campus.

By featuring queer films, we hope that we can create a space for acknowledgement, enjoyment, and appreciation through the contextualization and open conversations held, with every movie.
summary:
This club showcases LGBTQIA+ films to create a setting that promotes learning, mutual understanding, and diversity.

 We welcome ALL to join our club to learn more about the historical context, influence, and impact of these films!

name: Ascend UNLV
description: Ascend business student organizations value professional development, community, and leadership for Pan-Asian students. Core values include fostering unity through shared vision, promoting continuous learning, adapting to new challenges, succeeding through hard work, and leading with optimism. The organization aims to build a strong community and provide professional opportunities for Asian and Pacific Islander (API) students in business professions.
summary: Ascend is a business student organization that promotes professional development, leadership, and community. Guided by values of unity, hard work, and optimism. Ascend is open and welcoming to students of all backgrounds to connect and socialize!

name: UNLV Filmmaking Club
summary: A filmmaking club where groups of members work to create films, network, and show their films at film festivals of all kinds.

name: Canine Companions Collar Scholars at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas
shortName: UNLV Collar Scholars / Collar Scholars at UNLV
description: Collar Scholars at UNLV is partnered with Canine Companions a national non-profit that provides service dogs free of charge to people with disabilities. Our mission is to support college puppy raisers and educate about working dogs in our community. Members can become involved as active members, puppy sitters, or puppy raisers.
summary: Collar Scholars at UNLV is partnered with Canine Companions a national non-profit that provides service dogs free of charge to people with disabilities. Our mission is to support college puppy raisers and educate about working dogs in our community.

name: The UNLV Oscar Club
description: The UNLV Oscar Club seeks to increase interest in and celebrate the rich history of the Academy Awards. UNLVOC strives to bring UNLV students and otherwise into the continued cherishment of the pivotal historical and cultural role the Oscars, and the films celebrated through it, have played in Film history and pop culture. Simultaneously, we will appreciate the entertainment value that can be found in following the cycle of the film industry and attempting to predict the Academy Awards year round. This club will primarily engage in various forms of Oscar Trivia, year-round Oscar Predictions, deep dives into Oscar history, and screenings of Oscar-winning and Academy Award nominated feature films and shorts. This club also aims to support any related RSOs and UNLV Film events.
summary: The UNLV Oscar Club seeks to celebrate the rich history of the Academy Awards via various forms of Oscar Trivia, Oscar Predictions, deep dives into Oscar history, and screenings of Oscar-winning and nominated films.

name: South Asian Law Student Association
summary: SALSA seeks to strengthen the rapidly growing South Asian legal community in Las Vegas with a recognized and trusted forum for professional growth and advancement that promotes civil rights and access to justice within the South Asian community.

name: Alliance for Media, Production, and Event Design
description:
About AMPED

At the Alliance for Media, Production, and Event Design (AMPED), we are dedicated to creating immersive live entertainment experiences for the community while educating our members on how to build those experiences from the ground up. We aim to teach and uplift the next generation of professionals in entertainment technology, entertainment engineering, graphic design, marketing, and more.

Our organization provides a unique platform for UNLV students to gain hands-on experience and professional development. Whether you are interested in technical execution or creative strategy, we offer opportunities to build qualified skills as engineers and technicians in a real-world production environment.

Our Core Pillars

To achieve our mission, we focus on four key areas:

Design: Creating inclusive entertainment experiences while practicing creativity. Encouraging start-to-finish event design practices to create immersive experiences for attendees.
Outreach: Spreading word of events to the UNLV and Las Vegas community. Evaluating progress with professionals and building a network in entertainment and live production.
Construction: Implement safe building practices in all aspects of our entertainment events. Focus on the fabrication of schematic designs.
Knowledge: Offering professional development opportunities and hands-on experience to all members of the organization to build qualified engineers and technicians. Teaching proper safety protocol for the live-event industry, closely adhering to OSHA standards.

Join the Crew

Membership is open to all actively enrolled UNLV students. To remain in good standing, members simply need to attend at least one meeting and one event per semester. There are no current dues required to join, making our hands-on training accessible to all.

 

Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/cHA264hraB
summary: AMPED is centered around creating live entertainment experiences from the ground up. We teach the next generation of professionals in entertainment technology, engineering, and design through hands-on fabrication and professional development.

name: American Association of Public Health Dentistry – UNLV Student Chapter
description:
Founded in 1937, the American Association of Public Health Dentistry (AAPHD) provides a focus for meeting the challenge to improve oral health. AAPHD membership is open to all individuals concerned with improving the oral health of the public.

AAPHD accepts the challenge to improve total health for all citizens through the development and support of effective programs of oral health promotion and disease prevention.


Our mission is to provide community awareness to future dental professionals and engage students in dialogue about current dental public health issues.
summary: AAPHD is the premier dental public health organization that promotes optimal and equitable oral health for all through education, research, practice, policy and advocacy.

name: UNLV Water Polo Club
summary: A club for people of all experiences to begin playing water polo.  Only requirement is to be comfortable in deep water.  Knowing how to tread water and/or swim is preferable.
